17111146.Objectives: Assess efficacy and safety of intravitreally administered bevacizumab in minimally classic and occult subfoveal choroidal neovascularization due to age-related macular degeneration
17111146.TypesofParticipants: Patients with minimally classic and occult subfoveal choroidal neovascularization due to age-related macular degeneration
17111146.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Changes in best-corrected visual acuity, central foveal thickness, and total macular volume; safety outcomes

16138974.Objectives: To examine the effects of intravitreal pegaptanib in patients with subfoveal neovascularization in age-related macular degeneration
16138974.TypesofParticipants: patients with subfoveal neovascularization in age-related macular degeneration
16138974.TypesofInterventions: intravitreal pegaptanib administered every sixth week for 48 weeks in three dosages (0.3 mg, 1.0 mg, 3.0 mg) vs subconjunctival sham injection
16138974.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: change in visual acuity (proportion avoiding moderate visual loss)

16314219.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (NVAMD)
16314219.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: efficacy and safety, minimal damage to retinal tissue, treatment of choroidal neovascular membrane formation

16678158.Objectives: To summarize the mechanism, preclinical and clinical studies, and adverse events of pegaptanib treatment for neovascular age-related macular degeneration.
16678158.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration

15625332.Objectives: evaluate pegaptanib for treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration
15625332.TypesofStudies: two concurrent, prospective, randomized, double-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging, controlled clinical trials
15625332.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration
15625332.TypesofInterventions: intravitreous injection of pegaptanib (0.3 mg, 1.0 mg, or 3.0 mg) or sham injections every 6 weeks for 48 weeks
15625332.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: proportion of patients losing fewer than 15 letters of visual acuity at 54 weeks; severe loss of visual acuity (30 letters or more); maintenance or gain of visual acuity; mean visual acuity over time; adverse events (endophthalmitis, traumatic lens injury, retinal detachment)

16763862.Objectives: to summarize and discuss recent experiences with intravitreal bevacizumab for neovascular AMD; to present a web-based platform for online data registration and pooled analyses
16763862.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular disease (AMD)
16763862.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: morphological and functional effects; safety and efficacy

16935207.Objectives: To discuss the molecular biology of choroidal neovascularization and the role of VEGF
16935207.TypesofInterventions: Anti-VEGF agents; combination of anti-VEGF strategies with established treatment modalities

17063425.Objectives: Evaluate the early treatment response following systemic and intravitreal bevacizumab therapy in patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17063425.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
17063425.TypesofInterventions: systemic bevacizumab (5 mg/kg) and intravitreal bevacizumab (1 mg)
17063425.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: best corrected visual acuity (VA), central retinal thickness (CRT) measured by optical coherence tomography (OCT), leakage evaluated by fluorescein angiography (FA)

12750101.Objectives: to determine the safety profile of multiple injection therapy
12750101.TypesofParticipants: patients with subfoveal choroidal neovascularization secondary to age-related macular degeneration
12750101.TypesofInterventions: intravitreal injection of anti-VEGF aptamer with and without photodynamic therapy
12750101.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: clinical evidence of toxicity and complications; vision improvement (stabilized or improved vision, 3-line or greater improvement on ETDRS chart)

16013991.Objectives: Testing pegaptanib in neovascular age-related macular degeneration
16013991.TypesofParticipants: Patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration
16013991.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreous injection of pegaptanib (0.3, 1.0, or 3.0 mg) every 6 weeks; sham injection
16013991.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Proportion of patients losing <15 letters of visual acuity; adverse effects (vitreous floaters, vitreous opacities, anterior chamber inflammation)

16935208.Objectives: To discuss the role of vascular endothelial growth factor in ocular angiogenesis and its involvement in ocular diseases
16935208.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration, diabetic macular edema, and diabetic retinopathy
16935208.TypesofInterventions: laser photocoagulation, photodynamic therapy with verteporfin, pegaptanib sodium, ranibizumab

17021319.Objectives: Compare ranibizumab with photodynamic therapy with verteporfin for predominantly classic neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17021319.TypesofParticipants: Patients with predominantly classic neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17021319.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreal ranibizumab (0.3 mg or 0.5 mg) plus sham verteporfin; sham injections plus active verteporfin therapy
17021319.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Proportion of patients losing fewer than 15 letters from baseline visual acuity at 12 months; visual acuity improvement by ≥15 letters; mean visual acuity change; adverse events (e.g., endophthalmitis, uveitis)

17003454.Objectives: To evaluate the safety of three dose regimens of intravitreal bevacizumab for the management of choroidal neovascularization associated with age-related macular degeneration
17003454.TypesofStudies: Phase 1 dose-escalation study, prospective, nonrandomized open-label study
17003454.TypesofParticipants: Patients with age-related macular degeneration and subfoveal choroidal neovascularization
17003454.TypesofInterventions: Single intravitreal injection of bevacizumab at doses of 1.0, 1.5, or 2.0 mg
17003454.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Clinical evidence of toxicity and complications; changes in best corrected visual acuity; lesion area and choroidal neovascularization area; macular morphology

17011860.Objectives: To provide an overview of angiogenesis and vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) and discuss the development approach, safety, and efficacy results of current and emerging anti-VEGF therapies for ocular diseases
17011860.TypesofStudies: Literature review and analysis of clinical trials
17011860.TypesofInterventions: anti-VEGF therapies (antiangiogenic agents targeting vascular endothelial growth factor)

17114590.Objectives: To review aspects of VEGF biology relevant to clinical use of anti-VEGF agents in ophthalmology

16724865.Objectives: To review new pharmacologic approaches to therapy for age-related macular degeneration
16724865.TypesofStudies: Phase III clinical studies, longitudinal trials, Age-Related Eye Disease Study (AREDS)
16724865.TypesofParticipants: Patients with age-related macular degeneration (AMD), including wet AMD and those with high-risk features
16724865.TypesofInterventions: Anti-VEGF drugs (pegaptanib, ranibizumab), corticosteroids (intravitreal triamcinolone, anecortave acetate), photodynamic therapy (PDT), combinations (PDT with triamcinolone, anti-VEGF with PDT or antifibrotic agents), pigment epithelium-derived factor, inhibitors of matrix-metallo-proteinases, gene therapy, small interfering RNA (siRNA), vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, zinc, lutein and zeaxanthin dietary supplements
16724865.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Efficacy, tolerability, modification of natural disease course, risk reduction for conversion from early- to late-stage AMD, improvement of macular pigment density

16544440.Objectives: Evaluation of pegaptanib for prevention of vision loss due to neovascular AMD
16544440.TypesofParticipants: Patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration

17145936.Objectives: to examine pegaptanib as a treatment for age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and diabetic macular edema (DME)
17145936.TypesofStudies: preclinical studies, animal model studies, randomized controlled trials, phase 3 trials (VISION trial), phase 2 trial
17145936.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and diabetic macular edema (DME)
17145936.TypesofInterventions: intravitreous injection of pegaptanib (anti-VEGF aptamer); sham injection as comparator
17145936.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: significant clinical benefit, all prespecified clinical end points

16518379.Objectives: Describe the development and clinical effectiveness of pegaptanib, a targeted anti-VEGF aptamer for ocular vascular disease
16518379.TypesofParticipants: patients with choroidal neovascularization associated with age-related macular degeneration
16518379.TypesofInterventions: pegaptanib sodium (Macugen), an RNA aptamer directed against VEGF-165

17107753.Objectives: To propose a hypothesis that topical application of new anti-VEGF agents is potentially useful for inhibiting corneal neovascularization and restoration of corneal clarity
17107753.TypesofInterventions: Topical application of new anti-VEGF agents such as pegaptanib, ranibizumab and bevacizumab
17107753.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Inhibition of corneal neovascularization and restoration of corneal clarity

15885778.Objectives: To investigate the maximum tolerated dose of ranibizumab administered as a single intravitreal injection
15885778.TypesofStudies: Open-label, 5-center, uncontrolled, prospective, dose-ranging, interventional case series
15885778.TypesofParticipants: Patients with subfoveal choroidal neovascularization secondary to age-related macular degeneration with best-corrected Snellen equivalent visual acuity of 20/100 or worse and considered ineligible for laser photocoagulation or photodynamic therapy
15885778.TypesofInterventions: Single intravitreal injection of ranibizumab at escalating doses (50, 150, 300, 500, 1000, and 2000 microg)
15885778.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Changes from baseline in visual acuity, intraocular pressure, intraocular inflammation, and production of antiranibizumab antibody; dose-limiting toxicity defined by intraocular inflammation, elevated intraocular pressure, reduced visual acuity, or hemorrhage

16647134.Objectives: Evaluate safety of pegaptanib sodium injection in treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration during 2 years of therapy
16647134.TypesofStudies: Two concurrent, prospective, randomized, multicenter, double-masked, sham-controlled studies
16647134.TypesofParticipants: Patients with all angiographic choroidal neovascularization lesion compositions of AMD
16647134.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreous pegaptanib sodium (0.3 mg, 1 mg, 3 mg) or sham injections
16647134.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: All reported adverse events, serious adverse events, and deaths

11672877.Objectives: To describe two approaches of drug targeting to choroidal neovascularization: passive targeting and active targeting
11672877.TypesofInterventions: drug delivery systems targeted to choroidal neovascularization, including passive targeting and active targeting

15936441.Objectives: Evaluate short-term safety of systemic bevacizumab and its effects on visual acuity and subfoveal choroidal neovascularization in neovascular AMD patients
15936441.TypesofParticipants: AMD patients with subfoveal CNV (N=9) with best-corrected VA letter scores 70-20 (Snellen 20/40-20/400)
15936441.TypesofInterventions: Systemic bevacizumab infusion (5 mg/kg) at baseline, with 1 or 2 additional doses at 2-week intervals
15936441.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Safety assessments, VA scores, OCT measurements, angiographic lesion characteristics

17219106.Objectives: Compare full macular translocation (FMT) with photodynamic therapy (PDT) in treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
17219106.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
17219106.TypesofInterventions: full macular translocation (FMT) and photodynamic therapy with verteporfin (PDT)
17219106.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: change in distant visual acuity (ETDRS lines), proportion with improvement of ≥1 ETDRS lines, proportion with <3 ETDRS lines vision loss

17152537.Objectives: Evaluate safety and efficacy of intravitreal bevacizumab followed by pegaptanib maintenance for neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17152537.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD) of all subtypes and a broad range of baseline vision
17152537.TypesofInterventions: intravitreal bevacizumab followed by pegaptanib sodium for 54 weeks
17152537.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: visual acuity measurements, retinal thickness, adverse events, intraocular pressure

17031284.Objectives: To review the biology of VEGF and the clinical development of ranibizumab, and to evaluate its safety and efficacy in neovascular AMD
17031284.TypesofStudies: Phase III, multicenter, randomized, double-masked, controlled pivotal trials
17031284.TypesofParticipants: Patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17031284.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Visual acuity change, rate of serious adverse events

12377190.Objectives: Inhibition of retinal neovascularization via intraocular viral-mediated delivery of anti-angiogenic agents
12377190.TypesofStudies: In vivo animal study using a mouse model of retinopathy of prematurity
12377190.TypesofParticipants: Mice (mouse model of retinopathy of prematurity)
12377190.TypesofInterventions: AAV-mediated intraocular delivery of anti-angiogenic factors (pigment epithelium-derived factor, tissue inhibitor of metalloproteinase-3, endostatin)

11927845.Objectives: To evaluate preclinical and phase 1A clinical safety and efficacy of EYE001 for exudative AMD
11927845.TypesofStudies: Preclinical animal model studies (Miles assay, rat corneal angiogenesis model, mouse ROP model) and phase 1A single ascending dose clinical trial

11927845.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreal injection of EYE001 (anti-VEGF pegylated aptamer)
11927845.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: VEGF-mediated vascular leakage, corneal neovascularization, retinal neovascularization, safety, visual acuity (ETDRS chart)

17101999.Objectives: To investigate the safety and efficacy of intravitreal ranibizumab treatment combined with verteporfin photodynamic therapy (PDT)
17101999.TypesofStudies: Phase I/II, multicenter, randomized, single-masked, controlled study
17101999.TypesofParticipants: Patients with predominantly classic choroidal neovascularization secondary to age-related macular degeneration
17101999.TypesofInterventions: Ranibizumab (0.5 mg) intravitreal injections combined with verteporfin photodynamic therapy; control: sham injections with PDT
17101999.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Proportion of patients losing fewer than 15 letters from baseline visual acuity at 12 months; incidence and severity of adverse events

16935211.Objectives: Report results from phase III clinical trials of ranibizumab for neovascular AMD
16935211.TypesofStudies: Phase III clinical trials (MARINA, ANCHOR); phase I-II trial (FOCUS); ongoing trials (PIER, PrONTO, SAILOR)
16935211.TypesofParticipants: Patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD) of all angiographic subtypes and lesion sizes
16935211.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreal ranibizumab (0.3 mg, 0.5 mg) monthly or variable dosing; combination with photodynamic therapy (PDT)
16935211.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Visual acuity improvement, incidence of systemic adverse events (myocardial infarction, stroke, thromboembolic events), safety outcomes

16154196.Objectives: Evaluate safety and efficacy of pegaptanib for diabetic macular edema
16154196.TypesofStudies: Phase II randomized double-masked multicenter dose-ranging controlled trial
16154196.TypesofParticipants: Individuals with diabetic macular edema, best-corrected visual acuity 20/50 to 20/320
16154196.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreous pegaptanib (0.3 mg, 1 mg, 3 mg) or sham injections
16154196.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Best-corrected visual acuity, central retinal thickness, need for additional photocoagulation

17050580.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of systemic bevacizumab therapy on pigment epithelial detachment secondary to age-related macular degeneration and to identify prognostic factors for PED regression and improvement in best corrected visual acuity
17050580.TypesofParticipants: Patients with age-related macular degeneration and pigment epithelial detachment (nine patients, nine eyes)

17050580.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Maximum PED height, greatest linear diameter (GLD), best corrected visual acuity (BCVA), retinal morphological changes

16763865.Objectives: To discuss side effects of VEGF inhibition and report first experiences with bevacizumab
16763865.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: adverse events, local side effects on retinal perfusion and survival of neuronal tissue, risk-benefit profile

15961063.Objectives: To discuss bevacizumab (Avastin) as a humanized anti-VEGF monoclonal antibody for cancer therapy and its role in pathological angiogenesis
15961063.TypesofInterventions: bevacizumab (Avastin) in combination with chemotherapy for metastatic colorectal cancer

17061236.Objectives: Report one case of retinal pigment epithelium tear following intravitreal bevacizumab injection for neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17061236.TypesofParticipants: a 59-year-old patient with occult choroidal neovascularization associated with serous pigment epithelial detachment secondary to age-related macular degeneration
17061236.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: development of retinal pigment epithelium tear

16483659.Objectives: To assess safety of repeated intravitreal injections of ranibizumab in treating neovascular AMD, and to assess changes in visual acuity and AMD lesion characteristics

16483659.TypesofParticipants: Sixty-four patients with subfoveal predominantly or minimally classic AMD-related choroidal neovascularization
16483659.TypesofInterventions: Ranibizumab intravitreal injections (0.3 mg or 0.5 mg) vs usual care
16483659.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Adverse events, intraocular pressure, visual acuity, and lesion characteristics assessed by fluorescein angiography and fundus photography

15973626.Objectives: Evaluate safety and efficacy of Ranibizumab for neovascular age-related macular degeneration
15973626.TypesofStudies: Experimental animal models; Phase I/II clinical trials; Phase III clinical trials; Combination therapy trials
15973626.TypesofParticipants: Patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration
15973626.TypesofInterventions: Intravitreal injection of Ranibizumab; combination therapy; intermittent optical coherence tomography-guided therapy

16033295.Objectives: evaluation of pegaptanib in exudative age-related macular degeneration
16033295.TypesofParticipants: patients with exudative age-related macular degeneration
16033295.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: proportion of responders (losing <15 letters of visual acuity) at 54 weeks; severe loss of visual acuity; legal blindness; angiographic effects; adverse events

16784022.Objectives: To review the current development status of small-molecule vascular disrupting agents, including their mechanisms, clinical evaluation, and combination therapy potential.
16784022.TypesofStudies: Review; phase I clinical trials; phase II clinical trials; preclinical studies.
16784022.TypesofInterventions: Small-molecule vascular disrupting agents (VDAs); combretastatin A4 phosphate (CA4P); 5,6-dimethylxantlenone-4-acetic acid (DMXAA); tubulin-binding agents; flavonoids.
16784022.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Tumor blood flow reduction; tumor response; destruction of central tumor regions.

16458968.Objectives: To report the short-term safety, biologic effect, and a possible mechanism of action of intravitreal bevacizumab in patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
16458968.TypesofParticipants: Patients with subfoveal neovascular AMD (81 eyes of 79 patients)

16458968.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Safety, Snellen visual acuity, OCT retinal thickness, angiographic lesion characteristics

17046701.Objectives: To test the role of vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) in diabetic macular edema (DME) with ranibizumab
17046701.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic diabetic macular edema
17046701.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: change in foveal thickness, visual acuity, macular volume

16205558.Objectives: To assess the vision benefit of treating early subfoveal choroidal neovascularization secondary to AMD with pegaptanib sodium

16205558.TypesofParticipants: Patients with early subfoveal choroidal neovascularization secondary to age-related macular degeneration
16205558.TypesofInterventions: 0.3 mg pegaptanib sodium; sham injections (usual care)
16205558.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Visual acuity (change in letters), responder rate (loss of <15 letters), severe vision loss, gain of ≥15 letters

17027972.Objectives: To evaluate the safety, efficacy, and durability of bevacizumab for the treatment of subfoveal choroidal neovascularization in patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17027972.TypesofParticipants: Age-related macular degeneration patients with subfoveal choroidal neovascularization, best-corrected visual acuity letter scores 70 to 20 (Snellen equivalent 20/40-20/400)

17027972.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Safety assessments, changes in visual acuity letter scores, optical coherence tomography central retinal thickness measurements

16603955.Objectives: To describe the short-term anatomical and visual acuity responses after intravitreal injection of bevacizumab in patients with choroidal neovascularization secondary to age-related macular degeneration
16603955.TypesofParticipants: patients with choroidal neovascularization secondary to age-related macular degeneration

16876249.Objectives: To assess the safety, pharmacokinetics, and biological activity of IV administration of vascular endothelial growth factor trap (VEGF Trap) in patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
16876249.TypesofParticipants: patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
16876249.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Safety, change in excess retinal thickness and volume assessed by optical coherence tomography, visual acuity measured by the Early Treatment Diabetic Retinopathy Study protocol

17151487.Objectives: To evaluate the short-term fluorescein angiographic and visual acuity effects of a single intravitreal injection of bevacizumab (Avastin) for the management of persistent new vessels (NV) associated with diabetic retinopathy
17151487.TypesofParticipants: diabetic patients with actively leaking NV refractory to laser treatment and best-corrected Early Treatment Diabetic Retinopathy Study visual acuity (BCVA) worse than 20/40
17151487.TypesofInterventions: intravitreal injection of 1.5 mg of bevacizumab (Avastin)
17151487.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: changes in total area of fluorescein leakage from active NV and BCVA

11309996.Objectives: Investigate optimal use of neovascular inhibitors in CNV treatment and their efficacy as primary or augmenting agents
11309996.TypesofInterventions: neovascular inhibitors, photoreactive laser treatment, feeder vessel treatment

16828500.Objectives: To evaluate the efficacy of a second year of pegaptanib sodium therapy in patients with neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD)
16828500.TypesofStudies: Two concurrent, multicenter, randomized, double-masked, sham-controlled studies
16828500.TypesofParticipants: Patients with all angiographic neovascular lesion compositions of AMD
16828500.TypesofInterventions: Pegaptanib sodium therapy (0.3 mg) and sham (control); continuation or discontinuation of therapy after 1 year
16828500.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Mean change in visual acuity over time; mean change in standardized area under the curve of visual acuity; proportions of patients losing >15 letters from week 54 to week 102; losing <15 letters from baseline to week 102; gaining >=0, >=1, >=2, and >=3 lines of visual acuity; progression to legal blindness (20/200 or worse)

17021318.Objectives: Evaluate ranibizumab for the treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration
17021318.TypesofStudies: multicenter, 2-year, double-blind, sham-controlled, randomized controlled trial
17021318.TypesofParticipants: patients with age-related macular degeneration with minimally classic or occult choroidal neovascularization
17021318.TypesofInterventions: intravitreal injections of ranibizumab (0.3 mg or 0.5 mg) vs sham injections
17021318.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: proportion of patients losing fewer than 15 letters from baseline visual acuity at 12 months; visual acuity improved by 15 or more letters; mean change in visual acuity; adverse events (presumed endophthalmitis, serious uveitis)

12508081.Objectives: To test the hypothesis that administration of triiodothyronine (T3) and hydrocortisone would decrease mortality and respiratory morbidity in preterm infants of less than 30 wk gestation
12508081.TypesofStudies: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial
12508081.TypesofParticipants: Preterm infants of less than 30 wk gestation
12508081.TypesofInterventions: Triiodothyronine (T3) 6 microg.kg(-1).d(-1) with hydrocortisone 1 mg.kg(-1).d(-1) (treatment) vs. 5% dextrose (placebo) as continuous i.v. infusion for 7 d, halved on d 5
12508081.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Death or ventilator dependence at 1 wk; death or oxygen dependence at 2 wk; relationship between free T3 (FT3) and free thyroxine (FT4) levels in first 7 d and primary outcome

10952698.Objectives: To investigate whether increased iodine intake from supplemented preterm formula would improve thyroid hormone levels in preterm babies and hence improve neurodevelopmental status (planned subsequent study)
10952698.TypesofInterventions: increased iodine intake from supplemented preterm formula (272 microg/l) versus standard (68 microg/l)

10796152.Objectives: assess the effects of iodine supplementation before or during pregnancy in areas of iodine deficiency
10796152.TypesofStudies: systematic review of acceptably controlled trials
10796152.TypesofParticipants: pregnant women in areas of iodine deficiency
10796152.TypesofInterventions: iodine supplementation before or during pregnancy
10796152.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: deaths during infancy and early childhood; prevalence of endemic cretinism; psychomotor development scores

10796406.Objectives: To assess whether thyroid hormone therapy in preterm infants without congenital hypothyroidism results in clinically important changes in neonatal and long term outcomes in terms of both benefits and harms
10796406.TypesofStudies: Systematic review of randomized and quasi-randomized trials
10796406.TypesofParticipants: Preterm infants less than 32 weeks gestation without congenital hypothyroidism
10796406.TypesofInterventions: Thyroid hormone therapy (thyroxine or triiodothyronine) as treatment or prophylaxis
10796406.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Neurodevelopmental outcomes (Bayley Mental and Psychomotor Development Indices, abnormal neurological outcome), mortality, cerebral palsy, sensorineural impairment, fraction of inspired oxygen, severity of respiratory distress syndrome

11981285.Objectives: To determine whether common neonatal interventions do more good than harm by studying long-term efficacy and safety
11981285.TypesofParticipants: High-risk, very low birthweight infants; mature infants with severe respiratory failure; extremely low birthweight infants; premature infants; asphyxiated term infants
11981285.TypesofInterventions: Promotion of breast-feeding; comprehensive follow-up care; cryotherapy for threshold retinopathy of prematurity; extracorporeal membrane oxygenation; indomethacin prophylaxis; thyroxine supplementation; head cooling
11981285.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Outcomes beyond initial hospital discharge; long-term efficacy and safety

11687092.Objectives: To assess whether thyroid hormone therapy in preterm infants without congenital hypothyroidism results in clinically important changes in neonatal and long term outcomes in terms of benefits and harms
11687092.TypesofStudies: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized and quasi-randomized trials
11687092.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Neurodevelopmental outcomes (Bayley MDI, PDI, cerebral palsy, RAKIT IQ), mortality, respiratory outcomes (fraction of inspired oxygen, duration of mechanical ventilation, incidence of chronic lung disease)

11335749.Objectives: Evaluate the effects of neonatal thyroxine supplementation on developmental outcome at early school age in very preterm children
11335749.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: cognitive (IQ), behavioral, motor, and neurologic functioning

10950228.Objectives: Investigate endocrine and haemodynamic effects of tri-iodothyronine treatment after cardiopulmonary bypass operations in children with congenital cardiac malformations

10950228.TypesofInterventions: Tri-iodothyronine infusion (2 microg/kg on day 1, then 1 microg/kg daily up to 12 days)
10950228.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Plasma thyroid hormone concentrations, systolic cardiac function (echocardiography), cardiac index, therapeutic intervention scoring system

10075094.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of thyroxine administration on behavioural outcome at 2 years in very preterm infants
10075094.TypesofParticipants: Very preterm infants of <30 weeks gestational age
10075094.TypesofInterventions: Fixed dose of thyroxine (8 µg/kg birthweight/day) or placebo for the first 6 weeks of life
10075094.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Behavioural outcome (externalizing and destructive behaviours) at age 2 years; plasma-free thyroxine levels

11809642.Objectives: To test the hypothesis that fusidic acid would not increase the treatment effect of disinfecting with povidone-iodine alone in children with impetigo
11809642.TypesofStudies: Double blind randomised placebo controlled trial
11809642.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Clinical cure and bacterial cure after one week

12730478.Objectives: evaluate potential cost-effectiveness of nutrition interventions to improve pregnancy outcomes in the developing world
12730478.TypesofInterventions: iodine supplementation, vitamin A and beta-carotene supplementation
12730478.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: infant deaths, maternal deaths, adverse pregnancy outcomes

11076326.Objectives: To evaluate the effects of early thyroxine (T4) administration in the premature infant
11076326.TypesofParticipants: newborns less than 32 weeks' gestation (premature infants)
11076326.TypesofInterventions: thyroxine (T4) supplementation (10 or 20 micrograms/kg; intravenous or through nasogastric tube) for 21 days
11076326.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: chronic lung disease (oxygen dependency at 28 days), death, grade III or IV intraventricular hemorrhage, periventricular leukomalacia, sepsis

12108072.Objectives: To assess the diagnostic and prognostic value of procalcitonin (PCT) for neonatal maternofetal infections and to estimate normal PCT ranges in uninfected newborns
12108072.TypesofParticipants: Newborns (neonates) hospitalized in the Department of Neonatology
12108072.TypesofInterventions: Measurement of procalcitonin (PCT) concentrations in blood samples
12108072.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Serum procalcitonin (PCT) concentrations; diagnostic and prognostic value for maternofetal infections

10420516.Objectives: Compare longitudinal iodine concentration in breast milk of preterm infants mothers with and without iodine supplementation
10420516.TypesofParticipants: mothers of premature infants and their preterm infants
10420516.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: iodine concentration in breast milk; mean iodine intake in preterm infants

14528403.Objectives: determine perinatal risk factors for transient hypothyroxinemia (TH)
14528403.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: total serum thyroxine (T4) levels, low T4 (<5 microg/dL)

12006715.Objectives: Assess whether complete kangaroo mother care (KMC) would affect longitudinal/developmental patterns of hormonal change
12006715.TypesofParticipants: Healthy preterm infants (<37 weeks gestational age, birth weight <2,001 g)
12006715.TypesofInterventions: Complete kangaroo mother care (KMC): early discharge, skin-to-skin contact, breast-feeding
12006715.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Levels of 17alpha-hydroxy-progesterone (17-OHP), thyroxine-stimulating hormone (TSH), and thyroxine (T(4))

11464382.Objectives: To describe the importance and risks of iodine supplementation during pregnancy
11464382.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prevention of pathophysiological changes in pregnancy, development of foetus

12018015.Objectives: Evaluate whether estimated iodine-131 exposures were risk factors for infant mortality, fetal death, and preterm birth in the years of highest releases, 1945 and 1946.
12018015.TypesofParticipants: Births, infant deaths, and fetal deaths from an eight county area surrounding the Hanford facility.
12018015.TypesofInterventions: Iodine-131 exposure from Hanford Nuclear Reservation releases.
12018015.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Preterm birth, infant mortality, fetal death.

10403191.Objectives: To measure iodine and thyroglobulin concentrations, as well as iodine, T3, T4 and sialic acid contents of thyroglobulin in thyroid glands of preterm and term newborns and infants; to assess the effect of L-T4 treatment
10403191.TypesofParticipants: Very preterm newborns (24-32 postmenstrual weeks, <5 days postnatal life), preterm and term newborns (34-41 weeks, <5 days postnatal life), infants (born at term, postnatal age 1-8 months)
10403191.TypesofInterventions: Daily dose of 10 microg/kg L-T4 for at least 3 days
10403191.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Thyroid weight, sialic acid content of thyroglobulin, intrathyroidal concentrations of iodine and thyroglobulin, level of thyroglobulin iodination, T4 and T3 content of thyroglobulin

10569217.Objectives: To study the effect of dietary zinc, copper and iodine supplements on the milk concentration of these micronutrients
10569217.TypesofParticipants: healthy Italian lactating mothers of good socioeconomic status and their normal full-term infants
10569217.TypesofInterventions: nutritional supplement (PerMamma Abbott) providing 20 mg zinc sulfate, 2 mg copper sulfate, 116 μg potassium iodide
10569217.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: milk concentrations of zinc, copper, and iodine at 3, 30, and 90 days postpartum

10037082.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of thyroxine (T4) supplementation on thyroid function, neurodevelopmental outcome, and mortality in very preterm infants
10037082.TypesofParticipants: Very preterm infants less than 30 weeks gestation
10037082.TypesofInterventions: Thyroxine (T4) supplementation at 8 microg/kg birth weight per day for the first 6 weeks after birth
10037082.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Thyroid hormone levels (T4, FT4, rT3, TSH, T3), mortality, morbidity, heart rate, neurodevelopmental outcome at 2 years (mental outcome, adverse outcome defined as death or abnormal developmental outcome)

12725550.Objectives: To compare serum concentrations of thyroid hormones (T4, T3, FT4, rT3) and TSH in cord blood and arterial blood at 18-24 h after birth between term newborns with and without asphyxia; to assess association between severity of hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy and altered thyroid hormone/TSH levels, and between mortality and FT4 levels
12725550.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: serum concentrations of T4, T3, FT4, rT3, TSH; hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy severity; mortality

12297896.Objectives: Assess whether silent iodine prophylaxis has affected iodine nutrition in Belgian neonates
12297896.TypesofParticipants: Full term and preterm neonates, lactating women
12297896.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Urinary iodine concentration, iodine content of breast-milk

10696788.Objectives: Investigate whether neonatal iodine deficiency increases spermatogenic function in rats and support the hypothesis that historic elimination of juvenile iodine deficiency may explain human sperm count decline
10696788.TypesofStudies: One-generation rat reproduction study with three diet groups (control, NLID, CLID) and two subgroups for testicular evaluation

10696788.TypesofInterventions: Maternal and postnatal dietary iodine manipulation: control (200 ppb I), neonatal LID (<20 ppb I from dams then control postweaning), and chronic LID (<20 ppb I from dams then 40 ppb I postweaning)
10696788.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Testes weight, daily sperm production, sperm motility/morphology/histopathology, and Sertoli cell and round spermatid counts

14510091.Objectives: Effect of single dose povidone-iodine on serum thyrotropin and thyroxine levels and urinary iodine excretion
14510091.TypesofInterventions: Single dose povidone-iodine for skin disinfection
14510091.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Serum thyrotropin and thyroxine levels, urinary iodine excretion, thyroid function

12368973.Objectives: survey on treatment practices for transient hypothyroxinemia of prematurity (THOP)
12368973.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: proportion of neonatologists treating and number of THOP patients treated with thyroid hormone

14594173.Objectives: Compare thyroid function tests of preterm infants born to preeclamptic mothers with placental insufficiency with preterm infants born to mothers without placental insufficiency
14594173.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: thyroid hormone levels (T3, FT3, FT4, TSH, TBG) in cord blood and postnatal days

10802462.Objectives: Cross-sectional and longitudinal reference values of cortical N(1) peak latency of the median nerve SEP in very preterm infants

10802462.TypesofInterventions: Placebo (control group within L-thyroxine supplementation trial)
10802462.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Cortical N(1) peak latency of the median nerve SEP

10086898.Objectives: To provide reference values for motor nerve conduction velocity (MNCV) in very preterm infants and compare extrauterine versus intrauterine maturation
10086898.TypesofParticipants: very preterm infants born at less than 30 weeks' gestation (placebo infants within an L-thyroxine supplementation trial)
10086898.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ulnar and posterior tibial motor nerve conduction velocity (MNCV)

12585349.Objectives: Report a case of transient neonatal hypothyroidism due to a maternal vegan diet
12585349.TypesofParticipants: 10-day-old infant with goitre and her vegan mother with goitre

14648215.Objectives: assess the relationship between transient hypothyroxinaemia of prematurity (THOP) and dopamine administration in very low birth weight newborns
14648215.TypesofParticipants: very low birth weight preterm infants
14648215.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: incidence of THOP, TSH and thyroxine levels

14611705.Objectives: study thyrotropin (TSH) levels in mothers of premature and low-birth-weight infants in Colombia, where iodized salt supplements the diet to correct iodine deficiency; examine the additional impact of salt restriction in mothers with hypertensive disorders
14611705.TypesofParticipants: mothers of premature and low-birth-weight infants; mothers with hypertensive disorders
14611705.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: maternal TSH levels; infant TSH and thyroxine levels; infant birth weight; gestational age

10427933.Objectives: To determine whether oral iodized oil (OIO) could adversely affect the antibody response to trivalent oral poliovirus vaccine (OPV)
10427933.TypesofInterventions: Oral iodized oil (OIO) co-administered with oral poliovirus vaccine (OPV)

14630985.Objectives: to examine the responses of thyroid parameters, cortisol, and the cardiovascular system to a single dose of triiodothyronine (T3) followed by daily thyroxine (T4) in preterm infants <28 weeks gestational age
14630985.TypesofParticipants: preterm infants less than 28 weeks of gestational age
14630985.TypesofInterventions: triiodothyronine (T3) single dose after birth plus daily thyroxine (T4) for 6 weeks; placebo
14630985.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: thyroid parameters (T4, free T4, T3, free T3, reverse T3, TSH), cortisol, pulse rate, blood pressure, cumulative dose of inotropic agents

10230106.Objectives: Review all presently available data on iodine nutrition in Belgium
10230106.TypesofParticipants: all age groups; pregnant women; neonates
10230106.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: iodine intake below recommendations; subclinical hypothyroidism with goiter; transient hypothyroidism resulting in minor brain damage; cost of therapy

1279660.Objectives: To describe experience with amiodarone treatment in two pregnant sisters with familial dilatative cardiomyopathy and ventricular malignant extrasystole
1279660.TypesofParticipants: Two pregnant sisters with familial dilatative cardiomyopathy and ventricular malignant extrasystole
1279660.TypesofInterventions: Amiodarone (400-200 mg/day) since beginning of pregnancy
1279660.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Maternal and neonatal thyroid function, neurological and motor development, adverse effects

10422335.Objectives: evaluation of the success of preventive provisions (iodine supplementation, fortification, salt iodization) after a 3-year intervention

10422335.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ioduria (urinary iodine concentration) in neonates and mothers

12030913.Objectives: investigate whether breast milk is a substantial resource of thyroid hormone for very preterm neonates and can alleviate transient hypothyroxinaemia
12030913.TypesofStudies: comparative study within placebo group of a randomized trial
12030913.TypesofParticipants: very preterm infants born at <30 weeks' gestational age

12030913.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: plasma concentrations of T4, free T4, T3, TSH, rT3, thyroxine-binding globulin (TBG); thyroxine concentration in breast milk

11298090.Objectives: To illustrate that diagnosis of congenital thyrotoxicosis may be overlooked in sick, premature, extreme low birth weight (ELBW) or intrauterine growth retarded (IUGR) infants and that management is complex

1185390.Objectives: To describe two infants with hypopituitarism and the clinicopathologic relationship with neonatal hepatitis syndrome
1185390.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: clinical and histologic evidence of neonatal hepatitis syndrome

11684535.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of multiple micronutrient supplementation on the growth of children aged 8-14 mo whose diets were poor in several micronutrients.
11684535.TypesofInterventions: Multiple micronutrient supplement (vitamins A, D, E, K, C, B-1, B-6, B-12, riboflavin, niacin, biotin, folic acid, pantothenic acid, iron, zinc, iodine, copper, manganese, selenium) vs placebo

1165528.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: laboratory data indicative of hypothyroidism; clinical and laboratory evidence of hyperthyroidism

14669608.Objectives: To assess efficacy of different methods of iodine prophylaxis in pregnant women living in conditions of goiter endemia
14669608.TypesofParticipants: pregnant women living in endemic goiter areas, including those with diffuse euthyroid goiter and those without thyroid pathology
14669608.TypesofInterventions: potassium iodide prophylaxis and potassium iodide combined with L-thyroxine

12589112.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: TSH suppression (<6 mU/l), serum T4 concentration, time to TSH suppression

11826199.Objectives: To examine the relationship between thyroid hormone levels and the outcome death and ventilator dependence at 2 weeks in preterm infants <30 weeks' gestation
11826199.TypesofStudies: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, clinical trial (placebo arm data)
11826199.TypesofParticipants: Preterm infants younger than 30 weeks' gestation
11826199.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Death and ventilator dependence at 2 weeks of age

12048083.Objectives: to assess the influence of povidone-iodine mediastinal irrigation used for the treatment of deep sternal wound infection (DSWI) on thyroid function

112084.Objectives: Evaluate normalized thyroxine as a screening test for hypothyroidism in full-term and preterm newborn babies
112084.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: serum thyroxine (T4), normalized thyroxine (T4N), thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels

14746671.Objectives: To investigate the iodine levels of urine from 1 month old breast-fed infants and the ones of milk and urine from the lactating women, and to observe the effects of different feeding methods (breast-feeding, mixed-feeding, bottle-feeding) on the iodine status of the infants during the weaning period

14746671.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: iodine levels in urine of infants and lactating women, milk iodine levels

22770758.Objectives: evaluate perioperative cardioprotective effect of high thoracic epidural analgesia (HTEA), primarily expressed as effect on cardiac performance and hemodynamics in patients undergoing cardiac surgery
22770758.TypesofParticipants: low-to-moderate risk patients aged 65-80 years scheduled for elective coronary artery bypass graft surgery with or without aortic valve replacement

23440670.TypesofParticipants: high risk cardiac surgical patients (renal, hepatic or pulmonary dysfunction, advanced age, morbid obesity)
23440670.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: haemodynamic stability, decreased catecholamine response, good pulmonary function, early extubation, early discharge from intensive care unit, decreased morbidity, pulmonary/cardiovascular/renal complications, excellent analgesia

19471757.Objectives: To compare spinal versus epidural block on incidence and duration of silent myocardial ischaemia in aged patients undergoing TURP surgery
19471757.TypesofParticipants: elderly male patients undergoing transurethral resection of prostate (TURP)
19471757.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: silent myocardial ischaemia detected by Holter monitoring

26030214.Objectives: To compare the perioperative morbidity of patients with cardiac stents after spine surgery who continue to take aspirin before and after the operation with a similar group of patients who preoperatively discontinued aspirin
26030214.TypesofParticipants: patients with cardiac stents undergoing spinal surgery
26030214.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: serious bleeding-related complications (e.g., spinal epidural hematoma), operative time, intraoperative estimated blood loss, hospital length of stay, transfusion of blood products, 30-day hospital readmission rates

22404668.Objectives: characterize the incidence, predictors, and outcomes of acute postoperative neurological deterioration in patients undergoing surgery for ruptured intracranial aneurysm
22404668.TypesofStudies: Secondary analysis of data from a multicenter randomized clinical trial (Intraoperative Hypothermia for Aneurysm Surgery Trial)
22404668.TypesofParticipants: patients with radiographically confirmed subarachnoid hemorrhage, World Federation of Neurosurgical Societies Grade I-III, undergoing surgery to secure ruptured intracranial aneurysm within 14 days
22404668.TypesofInterventions: surgery for ruptured intracranial aneurysm (including factors such as ventriculostomy, timing of surgery, intentional hypotension, etc.)
22404668.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Glasgow Outcome Scale (dichotomized: score 1 good vs >1 poor) at 90 days; acute postoperative neurological deterioration defined by NIHSS score change, Glasgow Coma Scale change, and new focal motor deficit

25500688.Objectives: Review the literature regarding various modalities for management of postoperative pain after cardiac surgery

25500688.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: opioid requirements, pain scores, morbidity, hospital stay, costs, conversion to chronic pain

25010820.Objectives: Determine whether a perioperative hemodynamic protocol based on noninvasive cardiac output monitoring decreases incidence of postoperative complications and hospital length of stay; secondary objectives: time to peristalsis recovery, incidence of wound infection, anastomotic leaks, and mortality
25010820.TypesofParticipants: adult patients scheduled for open colorectal surgery, gastrectomy, or small bowel resection requiring intensive care unit admission

22770692.Objectives: To evaluate the postoperative effect of high thoracic epidural analgesia on the time in the intensive care unit (ICU) and the quality of cardiac recovery in patients undergoing cardiac surgery
22770692.TypesofParticipants: Sixty low-risk patients 65 to 80 years of age scheduled for elective coronary artery bypass graft surgery with or without aortic valve replacement
22770692.TypesofInterventions: High thoracic epidural analgesia (HTEA) as a supplement to general anesthesia

21691758.Objectives: review pharmacological approaches to cardiovascular protection in the perioperative period, including effects of central neuraxial blocks and hypothermia on cardiovascular outcome
21691758.TypesofParticipants: patients with complex co-morbidities undergoing major surgery
21691758.TypesofInterventions: beta-blockers, α(2)-agonists, statins, anticoagulation, antiplatelet therapy, perioperative cardioprotection, central neuraxial blocks, hypothermia, postoperative pain therapy protocols

22436549.Objectives: evaluate the usefulness of thoracic epidural anesthesia for awake off-pump coronary artery bypass by examining autonomic neural state and incidence of arrhythmia

22436549.TypesofInterventions: thoracic epidural anesthesia alone, general anesthesia combined with thoracic epidural anesthesia, general anesthesia alone

29703025.Objectives: To determine the incidence and extent of hypothermia in thoracic surgery patients under combined general and regional anesthesia and evaluate its effect on outcomes.

24574444.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of perioperative gabapentin treatment for the prevention of persistent post-thoracotomy pain and to establish whether gabapentin has a significant therapeutic impact on acute postoperative pain.
24574444.TypesofStudies: randomized controlled trial, randomized double-blinded placebo-controlled trial

25831695.Objectives: Evaluate the impact of thoracic epidural analgesia on the frequency of postoperative atrial fibrillation in patients undergoing lung cancer surgery.
25831695.TypesofParticipants: Patients over 55 years old undergoing lung cancer surgery (lobectomy or pneumonectomy), ASA II-III physical status
25831695.TypesofInterventions: Thoracic epidural analgesia with ropivacaine, fentanyl, and epinephrine; compared to general anesthesia with intercostal blockade and systemic analgesia

29694674.Objectives: To compare local anaesthetics and regional anaesthesia versus conventional analgesia for the prevention of persistent postoperative pain beyond three months in adults and children undergoing elective surgery.
29694674.TypesofStudies: systematic review of randomized controlled trials
29694674.TypesofParticipants: adults and children undergoing elective non-orthopaedic surgery
29694674.TypesofInterventions: local anaesthetics and regional anaesthesia (including epidural anaesthesia, continuous infusion with local anaesthetic, intravenous infusion of local anaesthetics) versus conventional analgesia
29694674.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: persistent postoperative pain beyond three months

22762307.Objectives: evaluate the effect of high thoracic epidural analgesia (HTEA) on blood glucose level and insulin requirement in patients undergoing cardiac surgery
22762307.TypesofParticipants: low-risk patients aged 65-79 years scheduled for elective coronary artery bypass grafting with or without aortic valve replacement
22762307.TypesofInterventions: high thoracic epidural analgesia (HTEA) as supplement for general anaesthesia
22762307.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: blood glucose levels, insulin requirement, lactate levels, postoperative pain scores

28074788.Objectives: Understand differences in outcome such as transfusion requirement, myocardial ischemia, and morbidity when two different doses of heparin are used for systemic heparinization in off-pump coronary artery bypass surgery.
28074788.TypesofParticipants: elective patients scheduled for off-pump coronary artery bypass surgery
28074788.TypesofInterventions: 2 mg/kg vs 3 mg/kg intravenous unfractionated heparin
28074788.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: transfusion requirement, myocardial ischemic episodes, intraoperative and postoperative blood loss, need for intraaortic balloon counter pulsation

26384505.Objectives: To determine the efficacy and hemodynamic influence of intraoperative paravertebral bolus injection during cardiac surgery
26384505.TypesofParticipants: Hemodynamically unstable patients undergoing transapical transcatheter aortic valve implantation (TA-TAVI)
26384505.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Remifentanil administration, blood pressure measurements, duration of hypotension, use of inotropic and vasopressor agents

21560028.Objectives: Investigate early and late postoperative outcome results of awake TEA patients undergoing cardiac surgery compared with combined TEA-GA and GA

21560028.TypesofInterventions: Thoracic epidural anesthesia (TEA), combined TEA with general anesthesia (TEA-GA), general anesthesia (GA)

29926477.Objectives: To compare local anaesthetics and regional anaesthesia versus conventional analgesia for the prevention of persistent postoperative pain (PPP) beyond three months in adults and children undergoing elective surgery.
29926477.TypesofStudies: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (RCTs)
29926477.TypesofParticipants: Adults and children undergoing elective non-orthopaedic surgery (thoracotomy, breast cancer surgery, caesarean section, iliac crest bone graft harvesting, limb amputation, hernia repair, cardiac surgery, laparotomy)
29926477.TypesofInterventions: Local anaesthetics and regional anaesthesia (e.g., epidural anaesthesia, continuous infusion with local anaesthetic, intravenous infusion of local anaesthetics) versus conventional analgesia
29926477.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Persistent postoperative pain (PPP) beyond three months (e.g., at 3-18 months, 3-12 months)

24492175.Objectives: To present a case of spinal epidural hematoma after lung cancer surgery under epidural and general anesthesia
24492175.TypesofInterventions: Epidural and general anesthesia; right upper lobectomy; anticoagulant therapy with fondaparinux and warfarin; emergency decompression laminectomy and hematoma evacuation; rehabilitation
24492175.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Neurological recovery (motor function and sensation); prothrombin time-international normalized ratio

22008919.Objectives: To determine the effect of sevoflurane combination with epidural anesthesia on myocardial injury in patients with coronary artery disease undergoing non-cardiac surgery
22008919.TypesofParticipants: patients with coronary artery disease undergoing elective abdominal surgery
22008919.TypesofInterventions: sevoflurane combination with epidural anesthesia (compared with standard sevoflurane, propofol combination, and standard propofol)
22008919.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ST depression, serum levels of IL-6, IL-8, TNF-alpha, cardiac troponin I, glycogen phosphorylase BB

24957190.Objectives: To test the hypothesis that thoracic epidural analgesia reduces the risk of new-onset atrial arrhythmias after pulmonary resection

24957190.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: new-onset atrial arrhythmias, cardiovascular complications, pulmonary complications, time-weighted average pain score over 72 hours, duration of hospitalization

22790008.Objectives: Evaluate the benefit of adding intravenous ketamine to patient-controlled epidural analgesia after thoracic surgery
22790008.TypesofInterventions: intravenous ketamine (bolus at induction plus continuous infusion during surgery and first 48 h postoperatively) versus intravenous placebo (saline), both with thoracic epidural analgesia
22790008.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: cumulative epidural ropivacaine consumption, postoperative pain scores (numeric pain intensity scale), analgesic rescue consumption, residual pain, haemodynamics, respiratory recovery function (spirometry), incidence of nausea, residual pain at 1 and 3 months

27390640.Objectives: To analyze early postoperative outcome data of patients undergoing cardiac surgery under general anesthesia with comparison to patients receiving combined thoracic epidural anesthesia and general anesthesia.
27390640.TypesofInterventions: General anesthesia (GA) alone; combined thoracic epidural anesthesia (TEA) and general anesthesia
27390640.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Quality of analgesia, major organ outcome parameters, time to extubation, hospital stay, pain numeric rating scale scores, morphine requirements

27621865.Objectives: compare postoperative pain management and outcomes between thoracic epidural analgesia (TEA) and bilateral subcutaneous infusion pump catheters (On-Q)
27621865.TypesofParticipants: adults undergoing minimally invasive repair of pectus excavatum (MIRPE, modified Nuss)

27621865.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: length of stay (LOS), opioid use (morphine-equivalent opioid usage), pain scores

23611915.Objectives: determine whether neuraxial block was associated with a composite of cardiovascular death, non-fatal myocardial infarction and non-fatal cardiac arrest within 30 days of randomization
23611915.TypesofStudies: post hoc analysis of a randomized controlled trial
23611915.TypesofParticipants: non-cardiac surgical patients at high risk of cardiovascular complications
23611915.TypesofInterventions: neuraxial block (spinal, lumbar or thoracic epidural anaesthesia)
23611915.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: composite of cardiovascular death, non-fatal myocardial infarction and non-fatal cardiac arrest; myocardial infarction; stroke; death; clinically significant hypotension

25886332.Objectives: Compare preemptive (preoperative) versus preventive (at end of surgery) intravenous paracetamol regarding hemodynamic stability, pain control, duration of analgesia, cumulative doses of intraoperative opioids and their side-effects, and compare two postoperative analgesia protocols and their cumulative doses
25886332.TypesofInterventions: Intravenous paracetamol 1 g given 30 min before induction of anesthesia (preemptive) or 30 min before end of surgery (preventive); postoperative pethidine 0.5 mg/kg (IV or IM+IV) with two different protocols

22274169.Objectives: To test the hypothesis that general anesthesia plus thoracic epidural anesthesia has no impact on outcomes of off-pump coronary artery bypass surgery compared to general anesthesia followed by patient-controlled thoracic epidural anesthesia, while general anesthesia plus thoracic epidural anesthesia leads to higher vasoactive drug requirement
