22274169.TypesofInterventions: General anesthesia plus intraoperative thoracic epidural anesthesia (TEA) versus general anesthesia alone, with both groups receiving postoperative patient-controlled thoracic epidural anesthesia
22274169.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Proportion of vasoactive drug use, extubation times, visual analog scale pain scores, heart rate, blood pressure, blood gases

23357523.Objectives: To evaluate the impact of thoracic epidural anaesthesia on intraoperative haemodynamics in patients undergoing OPCAB surgery
23357523.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing off-pump coronary artery bypass (OPCAB) surgery
23357523.TypesofInterventions: thoracic epidural anaesthesia with general anaesthesia versus general anaesthesia only
23357523.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: mean arterial blood pressure (MAP), heart rate (HR), central venous pressure (CVP), incidence of new arrhythmias

23789281.Objectives: To investigate the influence of different anesthetic techniques (general vs combined epidural and general anesthesia) and surgery strategies (on-pump vs off-pump) on postoperative bleeding complications and the need for blood transfusions during perioperative period
23789281.TypesofParticipants: patients scheduled for coronary artery bypass surgery (CABG)
23789281.TypesofInterventions: off-pump vs on-pump surgery; general anesthesia vs combined general and high thoracic epidural anesthesia
23789281.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: postoperative bleeding complications; need for blood transfusion; quantity of transfused blood or blood products; chest reopening

26139745.Objectives: Evaluate nonanalgesic benefits of combined thoracic epidural analgesia with general anesthesia in high risk elderly off pump coronary artery bypass patients
26139745.TypesofParticipants: Elderly high risk patients (age ≥70 years, with comorbidities) undergoing off pump coronary artery bypass surgery
26139745.TypesofInterventions: Thoracic epidural analgesia combined with general anesthesia (study group) versus general anesthesia only (control group)
26139745.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Postoperative complications, total intensive care unit stay, hospital stay, stress response (serum interleukin-6, TNF-α, cortisol, troponin I, CK-MB, HsCRP, time to mobilization, time to extubation)

29088950.Objectives: evaluate and assess viscoelastic hemostatic assays, and appraise the evidence supporting their use in neurosurgical populations
29088950.TypesofParticipants: neurosurgical patients, including those on antithrombotic agents and trauma patients
29088950.TypesofInterventions: viscoelastic hemostatic assays (thromboelastography and rotational thromboelastometry)

24382615.Objectives: To compare the incidence of early cardiac complications between patients who reported prescribed use of β-blockers and/or angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors before aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage and patients who did not.
24382615.TypesofParticipants: adult patients with acute aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage
24382615.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: cardiac enzyme levels, 12-lead electrocardiograms, chest radiographs, Holter monitoring, echocardiograms, ventricular and supraventricular ectopy

27182242.Objectives: Influence of two different anesthetic and analgesic methods on early cognitive function of elderly patients receiving non-cardiac surgery
27182242.TypesofParticipants: elderly patients who underwent non-cardiac surgery
27182242.TypesofInterventions: combined spinal and epidural anesthesia and epidural analgesia (observation group); general anesthesia and intravenous analgesia (control group)
27182242.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: incidence of cognition impairment; Visual Analogue Scale (VAS) Score; neuropsychological function; cognitive function

22356414.Objectives: To compare three anaesthetic protocols for umbilical surgery in calves regarding adequacy of analgesia, and cardiopulmonary and hormonal responses.
22356414.TypesofInterventions: Group INH: induction with xylazine and ketamine, maintenance isoflurane; Group INJ: induction with xylazine and ketamine, maintenance ketamine; Group EPI: high volume caudal epidural anaesthesia with xylazine and procaine; all received peri-umbilical procaine infiltration and pre-operative IV flunixin
22356414.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Intra-operative nociception assessed by VAS scale; cardiopulmonary variables: heart rate, cardiac output, mean arterial blood pressure, systemic vascular resistance, arterial pH, PaCO2; endocrine stress response: cortisol levels; hypoxaemia and vascular shunt

27630133.Objectives: to study the effects of thoracic epidural anesthesia on right ventricular function and ventricular-pulmonary coupling
27630133.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: right ventricular contractility (ESV25, Ees), stroke work, dP/dtmax, ejection fraction, effective arterial elastance, ventricular-pulmonary coupling, cardiac output, systemic vascular resistance, mean arterial blood pressure

25113260.Objectives: to investigate the TCM-induced cardiac function by transpulmonary thermodilution and its impact on clinical outcome of SAH
25113260.TypesofParticipants: 46 consecutive postoperative SAH patients who developed TCM
25113260.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: delayed cerebral ischemia (DCI), 3-month functional outcome on modified Rankin Scale of 4-6, correlation with LVEF, CFI, ELWI

24228013.Objectives: determine the incidence of intraoperative maternal hypotension during fetoscopic surgery for twin-twin transfusion syndrome (TTTS) and evaluate the impact of intraoperative hypotension on fetal survival
24228013.TypesofParticipants: 328 TTTS patients with recipient twin cardiomyopathy undergoing fetoscopic surgery under epidural anesthesia
24228013.TypesofInterventions: maternal nifedipine therapy for fetal cardiomyopathy
24228013.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: incidence of intraoperative maternal hypotension, acute fetal survival rate (within 5 days), fetal survival rate at birth, vasopressor requirement (phenylephrine dose, number of treatments)

24488821.Objectives: To compare thoracic paravertebral with thoracic epidural analgesia in thoracotomy for lung surgery
24488821.TypesofStudies: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials
24488821.TypesofParticipants: Patients undergoing thoracotomy for lung surgery
24488821.TypesofInterventions: Thoracic paravertebral block vs. thoracic epidural analgesia
24488821.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Visual analogue scale scores at rest and during activity/coughing at 4-8, 24 and 48 h; hypotension; urinary retention

22900930.Objectives: evaluate the efficacy of thoracic epidural anesthesia (TEA) in preventing postoperative atrial fibrillation (POAF) in adult patients undergoing coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG)
22900930.TypesofParticipants: adult patients undergoing coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG)
22900930.TypesofInterventions: thoracic epidural anesthesia (TEA) in addition to general anesthesia

28235533.Objectives: Compare analgesic efficacy and side effects of postoperative patient-controlled epidural analgesia with plain ropivacaine 0.1% with/without fentanyl 2.5 μg/mL vs plain bupivacaine 0.0625% with fentanyl 2.5 μg/mL in patients undergoing bilateral total knee replacement
28235533.TypesofParticipants: Ninety American Society of Anesthesiologists I to II post-total knee replacement patients
28235533.TypesofInterventions: Postoperative patient-controlled epidural analgesia with plain ropivacaine 0.1% alone, ropivacaine 0.1% with fentanyl 2.5 μg/mL, or plain bupivacaine 0.0625% with fentanyl 2.5 μg/mL; rescue epidural boluses of respective study drug
28235533.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Postoperative pain profile, total PCEA drug used, heart rate, noninvasive blood pressure, side effects, patient satisfaction, pain scores, rescue drug requirements, motor blockade

25918718.Objectives: Compare HTEA and intravenous PCA in CABG patients based on haemodynamic parameters and myocardial functions
25918718.TypesofParticipants: Patients scheduled for elective coronary bypass graft surgery (CABG)
25918718.TypesofInterventions: High thoracic epidural analgesia (HTEA) with levobupivacaine versus intravenous patient-controlled analgesia (PCA) with morphine
25918718.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Troponin I, CK-MB, total antioxidant capacity, malondialdehyde, VAS pain scores, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, cardiac index

23531633.Objectives: To investigate possible effects of high thoracic epidural anesthesia on mixed venous oxygen saturation in coronary artery bypass grafting surgery
23531633.TypesofParticipants: patients scheduled for coronary artery bypass grafting surgery
23531633.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: mixed venous oxygen saturation, hemodynamic variables, recovery score, extubation time, visual analogue scale, duration of stay in ICU and hospital

25417134.Objectives: To assess the impact of epidural analgesia versus systemic opioid-based analgesia on length of hospital stay and recovery of bowel function in thoracotomy patients
25417134.TypesofInterventions: epidural analgesia with ropivacaine + sufentanil versus systemic opioid-based analgesia with controlled-release oxycodone
25417134.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: length of hospital stay, recovery of bowel function (dejection time), opioid doses at dismissal

22379904.Objectives: investigate methods of decreasing negative effects on intestine perfusion which leads to metabolic disorder
22379904.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing cardiac surgery of descending aorta and cross-clamping before celiac trunk
22379904.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: level of lactate and glucose; intraluminal tonometry data; intestine motility

24216528.Objectives: Describe the state of the art in preventing postthoracotomy pain
24216528.TypesofInterventions: thoracic paravertebral block, thoracic epidural analgesia, intercostal analgesia, intrathecal opioids, nonopioid analgesics (acetaminophen, NSAIDs, COX-2 inhibitors), opioids as rescue, multimodal analgesia techniques, multidisciplinary rehabilitation program
24216528.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: postthoracotomy pain, postoperative morbidity, recovery, hospital stay, convalescence

27366446.Objectives: effects of anaesthetic technique on mother and newborn in parturients with cardiac diseases undergoing Caesarean section
27366446.TypesofParticipants: parturients with cardiac diseases undergoing Caesarean section
27366446.TypesofInterventions: general anaesthesia; regional anaesthesia (spinal, epidural, CSE)
27366446.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: week of pregnancy, NYHA classification, valvular lesion rates, APGAR scores, postoperative complications, hospital stay, newborn weights

23509236.Objectives: evaluate the feasibility of two existing VATS approaches for post-thoracotomy pain and long-term survival
23509236.TypesofParticipants: patients with clinical T1N0M0 non-small-cell lung cancer
23509236.TypesofInterventions: assisted VATS lobectomy (via anterolateral small thoracotomy with rib spreader, combination of thoracoscopic and direct views) and complete VATS lobectomy (only monitor, smaller-access thoracotomy without rib spreader)
23509236.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: perioperative variables (operation time, blood loss, morbidity, mortality), post-thoracotomy pain, use of additional painkillers, disease-free and overall 5-year survival rates

23851286.Objectives: To identify clinical conditions or interventions for which an association between volume and outcome has been investigated. To identify clinical conditions or interventions for which an association between volume and outcome has been proved. To analyse the distribution of Italian health providers by volume of activity. To measure the association between volume of care and outcomes of the health providers of the Italian NHS.
23851286.TypesofStudies: Systematic review of systematic reviews and HTA reports; analysis of Italian hospital administrative data (National Outcome Programme 2011)
23851286.TypesofInterventions: AIDS, abdominal aortic aneurysm, coronary angioplasty, myocardial infarction, knee arthroplasty, coronary artery bypass, cancer surgery (breast, lung, colon, colon rectum, kidney, liver, stomach, bladder, oesophagus, pancreas, prostate); cholecystectomy, brain aneurysm, carotid endarterectomy, hip fracture, lower extremity bypass surgery, subarachnoid haemorrhage, neonatal intensive care, paediatric heart surgery

23430415.Objectives: Evaluate changes in cerebral blood flow during awake off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting and compare with changes under general anesthesia using continuous monitoring of regional cerebral oxygen saturation
23430415.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting
23430415.TypesofInterventions: thoracic epidural anesthesia, general anesthesia, or a combination
23430415.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: regional cerebral oxygen saturation (rSO2), mean arterial pressure, cerebrovascular impairment ratio, EuroSCORE

12535435.Objectives: To determine the effectiveness and safety of fluoride toothpastes in the prevention of caries in children and to examine factors potentially modifying their effect
12535435.TypesofStudies: Randomized or quasi-randomized controlled trials with blind outcome assessment
12535435.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: caries increment measured by the change in decayed, missing and filled tooth surfaces (D(M)FS)

14583954.Objectives: To determine the effectiveness and safety of fluoride varnishes, gels, mouthrinses, and toothpastes in the prevention of dental caries in children and to examine factors potentially modifying their effect.
14583954.TypesofStudies: Randomized or quasi-randomized controlled trials with blind outcome assessment
14583954.TypesofParticipants: children up to 16 years (children and adolescents)
14583954.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Caries increment measured by the change in decayed, missing and filled tooth surfaces (D(M)FS); prevented fraction (PF)

28243757.Objectives: evaluate the effect of toothpastes containing 1100 ppm F associated with nano-sized sodium hexametaphosphate (HMPnano) on enamel demineralization in vitro
28243757.TypesofInterventions: toothpastes with different fluoride concentrations (0, 550, 1100 ppm F) and nano-sized sodium hexametaphosphate (0.25%, 0.5%, 1.0%)
28243757.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: surface hardness (SHf), integrated loss subsurface hardness (ΔKHN), integrated mineral loss (g_HAp × cm^-3), enamel fluoride concentrations

12917928.Objectives: To determine the effectiveness and safety of fluoride mouthrinses in the prevention of dental caries in children and to examine factors potentially modifying their effect.
12917928.TypesofStudies: Randomised or quasi-randomised controlled trials with blind outcome assessment
12917928.TypesofParticipants: children up to 16 years; children and adolescents
12917928.TypesofInterventions: Fluoride mouthrinses compared with placebo or no treatment
12917928.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: caries increment measured by the change in decayed, missing and filled tooth surfaces (D(M)FS); prevented fraction (PF)

30106524.Objectives: determine total fluoride (TF), total soluble fluoride (TSF) and insoluble fluoride (IF) concentrations in 22 fluoride toothpastes commercially available in South Africa
30106524.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: total fluoride (TF) concentration, total soluble fluoride (TSF) concentration, insoluble fluoride (IF) concentration

9482041.Objectives: To assess the caries prevalence of children living in areas with 0.25 or 2.5 ppm fluoride in drinking water, and to relate caries experience to the severity of dental fluorosis
9482041.TypesofParticipants: Children aged 6-16 years, lifelong residents of rural villages
9482041.TypesofInterventions: Drinking water fluoride concentration (0.25 ppm vs 2.5 ppm)
9482041.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Caries prevalence (DMFT, dmft, decayed permanent teeth), dental fluorosis severity (Dean's index)

12218276.Objectives: To determine the maximum amount of enamel remineralisation achievable with daily applications of high-concentration fluoride gel and fluoridated toothpaste, and to compare the efficacy of gel + toothpaste versus toothpaste alone.
12218276.TypesofInterventions: Fluoridated topical gel (12500 ppm F) + fluoridated toothpaste (1450 ppm F); fluoridated toothpaste alone
12218276.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Enamel remineralisation (lesion size reduction, mineral content profiles); fluoride concentration (loosely bound and bound fluoride)

23230807.Objectives: Describe how a school-based fluoride varnish programme is experienced by participating adolescents
23230807.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Themes: conceptions in relation to the individual, school dental nurse, and group and school

12166721.Objectives: assess the role of predictors of caries experience among children in urban and rural areas of northern Tanzania
12166721.TypesofParticipants: children aged 9-14 years from urban and rural areas of northern Tanzania consuming drinking water with varying fluoride concentrations
12166721.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: dental caries assessed using decayed, missing, filled teeth (DMFT) index and WHO criteria

24100637.Objectives: To measure the effects of a 5,000-ppm F toothpaste on caries-related factors in dental plaque and saliva.
24100637.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: approximal fluid fluoride concentration, lactic acid production rate on tongue, approximal plaque pH (AUC5.7, AUC6.2, maximum pH fall, minimum pH), salivary buffer capacity, salivary mutans streptococci counts

14649403.Objectives: Investigate caries experience in young Australian adults, compare with similar aged cohorts over past 30 years, relate caries experience to lifetime exposure to fluoridated water
14649403.TypesofParticipants: Australian Army recruits, young adults aged 17-35 years

27619370.Objectives: Determine the concentration of 222Rn, 226Ra, and 238U in 25 toothpastes from Penang markets and monitor heavy metals
27619370.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: concentration of 222Rn, 226Ra, 238U; annual effective dose; pH; concentrations of heavy metals; electrical conductivity

17514449.Objectives: determine the prevalence and the social factors influencing selected aspects of oral health behaviour
17514449.TypesofStudies: cross-sectional survey (German Health Interview and Examination Survey for Children and Adolescents, KiGGS)
17514449.TypesofParticipants: 17,641 children and adolescents aged 0 to 17 years, representative sample for Germany
17514449.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: teeth brushing frequency, dental check-up frequency, use of pharmaceutical preparations for caries prevention

7774172.Objectives: determine the prevalence of dental caries in children ages 6-14 from fluoridated and non-fluoridated areas and the effects of receiving certain fluoride preventive procedures during childhood
7774172.TypesofParticipants: children ages 6-14 from two communities in British Columbia, Canada
7774172.TypesofInterventions: lifelong consumption of fluoridated water; use of fluoride supplements (for 4 yr or more, and less than 4 yr)
7774172.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: decayed or filled tooth surfaces per child (modified Decayed, Missing and Filled Tooth Surface Index D1D2MFS); caries prevalence scores; pit and fissured surfaces; smooth surfaces

19702099.Objectives: To determine the dental caries experience in relation to the severity of dental fluorosis and to evaluate the dietary snacking habits of adolescents
19702099.TypesofParticipants: Adolescents aged 13-15 years, from a peri-urban primary school in Nairobi, Kenya
19702099.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Severity of dental fluorosis (TFI scores), dental caries experience (DMFT), dietary snacking habits

9892778.Objectives: assess need for defluoridation/fluoridation of water supplies and caries and fluorosis prevalence in 12-year-old schoolchildren
9892778.TypesofInterventions: different concentrations of fluoride in drinking water (high: >=2.5 mg/l; low: <=0.3 mg/l)
9892778.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: DMFT, DMFS, caries-free status, Community Fluorosis Index (CFI), fluorosis prevalence

28004389.Objectives: Determine the effectiveness of community-based population-level oral health promotion interventions in preventing dental caries and gingival and periodontal disease among children from birth to 18 years of age.
28004389.TypesofStudies: Individual- and cluster-randomised controlled trials (RCTs), controlled before-and-after studies, quasi-experimental and interrupted time series
28004389.TypesofParticipants: Children from birth to 18 years of age
28004389.TypesofInterventions: Community-based oral health promotion interventions including dietary interventions, oral health education alone or combined with supervised toothbrushing with fluoridated toothpaste or professional preventive oral care, and other strategies (e.g., policy, educational activities, professional oral health care, supervised toothbrushing programmes, motivational interviewing)
28004389.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Dental caries (measured as decayed, missing and filled deciduous teeth/surfaces (dmft/s); Decayed, Missing and Filled permanent teeth/surfaces (DMFT/S)) and gingival or periodontal disease

27629283.Objectives: To determine the effectiveness of community-based population-level oral health promotion interventions in preventing dental caries and gingival and periodontal disease among children from birth to 18 years of age.
27629283.TypesofStudies: Systematic review of individual- and cluster-randomised controlled trials, controlled before-and-after studies, quasi-experimental and interrupted time series
27629283.TypesofParticipants: Children from birth to 18 years of age
27629283.TypesofInterventions: Community-based population-level interventions including dietary interventions, oral health education alone, oral health education in combination with supervised toothbrushing with fluoridated toothpaste, and in combination with professional preventive oral health care, motivational interviewing, policy, educational activities
27629283.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Dental caries measured as decayed, missing and filled deciduous teeth/surfaces (dmft/s) and Decayed, Missing and Filled permanent teeth/surfaces (DMFT/S); gingival and periodontal disease

24437300.Objectives: To determine the quantity of Streptococcus mutans and the ratio of S. mutans to total bacteria in the dental plaque of different caries-susceptible children
24437300.TypesofParticipants: children aged 3 years old to 4 years old, with and without caries
24437300.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: quantities of S. mutans, total bacteria, ratio of S. mutans to total bacteria

12752542.Objectives: Compare costs and consequences of caries preventive programs in a caries high-risk population
12752542.TypesofInterventions: Four preventive programs with step-wise increase in fluoride content, contact with dental personnel, and cost
12752542.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Costs (treatment costs, patient/family costs) and averted DeMFS (decayed enamel and dentine missing and filled surface)

30803490.Objectives: characterize the kinetics of silver and fluoride after topical application of silver diamine fluoride
30803490.TypesofInterventions: topical application of 38% silver diamine fluoride to 5 teeth (approximately 50 microliters, estimated 4-11 milligrams per participant)
30803490.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: serum and urine silver and fluoride levels; serum peak concentration and time to peak; elimination half-life; 24-hour urinary fluoride

11460274.Objectives: To determine whether fluoride in tablet or liquid form would be more efficacious with children with cleft lip and/or palate; comparative anti-caries effects of tablet and liquid fluorides in cleft children
11460274.TypesofStudies: Two-year clinical investigation; randomized controlled trial (randomly selected into control, tablet and liquid fluoride groups)
11460274.TypesofParticipants: Cleft children (children with cleft lip and/or palate); 115 children (59 boys and 56 girls) between 22 and 26 months old
11460274.TypesofInterventions: Tablet fluoride and liquid fluoride; 0.25 mg F daily as dietary fluoride supplements
11460274.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Caries assessed using DMF index (DMFT and DMFS increments) at baseline, first year and second year

15195724.Objectives: To evaluate and compare the retention rate, marginal integrity and caries preventing effects of a polyacid-modified resin composite based fissure sealant (Dyract Seal) to that of a resin based fluoride fissure sealant (Delton FS+)
15195724.TypesofParticipants: 53 patients (27 female, 26 male), 7 to 10 years old
15195724.TypesofInterventions: Dyract Seal (polyacid-modified resin composite based fissure sealant) and Delton FS+ (resin based fluoride fissure sealant), applied using invasive technique
15195724.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: retention rate, marginal integrity, caries preventing effects

25448884.Objectives: To analyze caries experience in relation to the occurrence of fluorosis in 12 year olds in a natural fluoride area
25448884.TypesofParticipants: 12-year-old lifetime residents of Klaipeda, Lithuania, exposed to 1.7-2.2 ppm F in tap water
25448884.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: DMFT index, DMFS index, DFS on selected proximal surfaces, TF index for fluorosis severity

10477992.Objectives: To determine the comparative effects of sodium (NaF) and amine fluoride dentifrices, as well as the effect of different silica abrasives used in NaF dentifrices, on caries formation in rats.
10477992.TypesofParticipants: Harlan Sprague Dawley mixed sex, weanling, littermate rats
10477992.TypesofInterventions: 0.31% NaF with precipitated silica I, 0.31% NaF with sodium bicarbonate and hydrated silica, placebo, amine fluoride (0.14%) containing silica (Elmex), 0.31% NaF with precipitated silica II (Sensodyne F), 0.31% NaF with hydrated silica
10477992.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: caries scores using Keyes scoring method

9879918.Objectives: To study approximal caries development following intensive fluoride mouthrinsing in teenagers
9879918.TypesofInterventions: Intensive fluoride mouthrinsing (0.045% NaF solution, 10 ml daily for 3 days, twice a year)
9879918.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Approximal DFS (decayed and filled surfaces) measured by bitewing radiographs

7669208.Objectives: Estimate average fluoride and sugar intake in Mauritius to assess feasibility of sugar fluoridation for caries prevention
7669208.TypesofStudies: Survey on Diet, Health and Lifestyle of Youth in Mauritius (1990); Mauritius Diet and Health Survey
7669208.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Daily total sugar intake; daily frequencies of sugar-containing food consumption; mean fluoride intakes per day; comparison with recommended level for caries prevention

18813678.Objectives: analyze fluoride concentration in the public water supply in Chapecó, Santa Catarina State, Brazil, from 1995 to 2005 and to assess the effectiveness of a fluoridation quality intervention in 2003

21985895.Objectives: evaluate the effect of toothpastes with SnF in the prevention of erosive enamel wear
21985895.TypesofStudies: single-centre, randomized, double-blind, in situ study with split-mouth and crossover design
21985895.TypesofInterventions: stannous fluoride-containing toothpastes (coded M and PE) and sodium fluoride toothpaste (coded C)
21985895.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: enamel wear depth measured by optical profilometry

9165186.Objectives: To test the caries-preventive efficacy of prenatal fluoride supplementation
9165186.TypesofParticipants: Pregnant women in first trimester and their children followed until age 5
9165186.TypesofInterventions: Prenatal fluoride supplements (1 mg fluoride daily as tablet during last 6 months of pregnancy) versus placebo; both groups encouraged to use postnatal dietary fluoride supplements
9165186.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Dental caries at ages 3 and 5; fluorosis at age 5

25726345.Objectives: to investigate the association between water fluoridation and reduction in hospitalization rates due to odontogenic infections
25726345.TypesofParticipants: children younger than 18 years from municipalities in Israel
25726345.TypesofInterventions: water fluoridation (optimally fluoridated areas with fluoride ≥0.7 mg/L vs nonoptimally fluoridated areas with ≤0.5 mg/L)
25726345.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: hospitalization rates per 10,000 children for odontogenic infections

15489367.Objectives: To compare and measure the changes in size of post-orthodontic demineralized white lesion enamel lesions treated with a low fluoride (50 ppm) versus a non-fluoride mouthrinse/toothpaste regime
15489367.TypesofStudies: double-blind prospective randomized clinical controlled trial
15489367.TypesofParticipants: patients identified as having post-orthodontic demineralized white lesions on removal of their fixed appliance
15489367.TypesofInterventions: low fluoride (50 ppm) mouthrinse/toothpaste regime vs. non-fluoride mouthrinse/toothpaste regime
15489367.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: lesion size and proportion (DWL%t) and percentage reduction (ADPR) at debond, 12 weeks, and 26 weeks

17827963.Objectives: evaluate the efficacy of topical fluoride varnish applications on white spot lesion formation in adolescents during treatment with fixed orthodontic appliances
17827963.TypesofParticipants: adolescents aged 12-15 years referred for maxillary treatment with fixed orthodontic appliances
17827963.TypesofInterventions: topical fluoride varnish (Fluor Protector) or placebo varnish every 6th week
17827963.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: incidence and progression of white spot lesions on upper incisors, cuspids, and premolars scored from digital photographs

16512107.Objectives: describe perceived dental health status and oral health-related knowledge, attitudes, and behavior in Chinese urban adolescents; assess associations of oral health variables with socio-economic status and school performance; analyse relative effect of socio-behavioral risk factors on perceived dental health, perceived need for dental care, and experience of dental symptoms
16512107.TypesofParticipants: Chinese urban adolescents, age groups 11, 13, and 15 years
16512107.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: perceived dental health status, perceived need for dental care, experience of dental symptoms, toothbrushing frequency, use of fluoridated toothpaste, dentist visits, smoking, alcohol use

15470832.Objectives: To assess the impact of a programme regularly supplying free fluoride toothpaste to children on the prevalence and severity of fluorosis and other developmental defects of enamel.
15470832.TypesofStudies: Randomised, controlled, parallel three-group clinical trial
15470832.TypesofParticipants: Children from the north west of England, aged 8-9 years, consuming drinking water containing less than 0.1 ppm F
15470832.TypesofInterventions: Free fluoride toothpaste containing either 440 ppm F or 1450 ppm F
15470832.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Developmental defects of enamel and dental fluorosis (TF index) recorded on upper central incisors from wet and dry images

23041912.Objectives: evaluate and compare changes of dental caries prevalence and severity in 7-8 year old schoolchildren in six Lithuanian regions from 1983 to 2009, and propose recommendations
23041912.TypesofParticipants: 7-8 year old schoolchildren in six regions of Lithuania
23041912.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: dental caries prevalence and severity (df-t, DMF-T), oral hygiene status (OHI-S)

19686543.Objectives: To investigate if everyday exposure to triclosan via triclosan-containing toothpaste causes an increase in plasma 4beta-hydroxycholesterol, indicative of CYP3A4 induction, and/or alterations in thyroid hormonal status
19686543.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Plasma levels of 4beta-hydroxycholesterol and thyroid hormones

11928433.Objectives: To assess the prevalence of fluorosis at the age of 8, 14 and 20 years; to evaluate the prevalence of caries and fluorosis at the age of 8 and 14 years in relation to the use of fluoride tablets during the first 5 years of age.
11928433.TypesofInterventions: Use of fluoride tablets during the first 5 years of life
11928433.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Prevalence of fluorosis, prevalence of caries

16110207.Objectives: Evaluate fingernails and urine as biomarkers of fluoride exposure from fluoridated dentifrice and varnish
16110207.TypesofParticipants: children aged 4-7 years, caries-free and with past caries experience
16110207.TypesofInterventions: fluoridated dentifrice (1570 ppm F), placebo dentifrice, fluoride varnish (2.26% F)
16110207.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: fingernail fluoride concentrations, 24-hour urinary fluoride output, total fluoride intake

16419430.Objectives: To study the dental fluorosis and caries in the permanent teeth of 12 to 13-year-old children in fluorosis-endemic areas; to assess the relationship between fluorosis and the fluoride content of the drinking water and the relationship between caries and the fluoride content of the water; to analyze the effect of fluoride intake and water stored in clay pots on dental fluorosis
16419430.TypesofInterventions: Fluoride concentration of drinking water; storage of water in clay pots
16419430.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: TF score for dental fluorosis; DMF-T index for dental caries

15881301.Objectives: To assess dental caries experience in Australian Army recruits and examine factors such as age, education, and lifetime exposure to fluoridated drinking water

26092033.Objectives: To evaluate the effects of water fluoridation (artificial or natural) on the prevention of dental caries and on dental fluorosis.
26092033.TypesofStudies: Systematic review with meta-analysis; prospective studies with concurrent control for caries; any study design with concurrent control for fluorosis.
26092033.TypesofParticipants: Populations of all ages receiving fluoridated or non-fluoridated water, primarily children.
26092033.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Caries indices: dmft, DMFT, proportion caries free; dental fluorosis of aesthetic concern and any level.

19486461.Objectives: determine caregiver treatment acceptability and preferences for five preventive dental treatments for early childhood caries in young Hispanic children
19486461.TypesofParticipants: parents/caregivers of Hispanic children attending Head Start programs
19486461.TypesofInterventions: toothbrushing with fluoride toothpaste, fluoride varnish, xylitol in food for children, xylitol gum for mothers, chlorhexidine rinse for mothers
19486461.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: acceptability ratings (1-5 scale), treatment preference scores (summed preferences within pairs, range 0-4)

23748452.Objectives: evaluate concentrations of calcium, inorganic phosphate, and fluoride in carious dentin and different layers of sound dentin
23748452.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: concentrations of calcium, inorganic phosphate, and fluoride

30211440.Objectives: To test the hypothesis that parental factors in early childhood influence approximal caries experience in young adults.
30211440.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: approximal caries experience (approximal caries lesions stratified into initial or manifest caries and approximal fillings, grouped as DimFa)

19346053.Objectives: evaluate the impact of toothpaste slurry abrasivity and toothbrush filament diameter on abrasion of eroded dentin in vitro
19346053.TypesofInterventions: brushing with toothpaste slurries of different abrasivity (RDA values 10, 20, 50, 100) and toothbrush filament diameters (0.15, 0.20, 0.25 mm) after acid erosion

234130.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of a sodium fluoride dentifrice on dental caries incidence in children
234130.TypesofParticipants: Schoolchildren residing in a fluoride-deficient area
234130.TypesofInterventions: Sodium fluoride dentifrice (0.1% F) vs nonfluoride dentifrice; initial prophylaxis for some children

6365424.Objectives: assess the caries preventive effect of a toothpaste containing 1.2% SMFP
6365424.TypesofParticipants: 10-12-year-old children from a wide socioeconomic background
6365424.TypesofInterventions: 1.2% sodium monofluorophosphate dentifrice in aluminium oxide trihydrate base vs placebo toothpaste without fluoride
6365424.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Dental caries assessed by clinical and radiographic examinations (DMFT, DMFS, DMFSU indices)

7960166.Objectives: To compare the effect of four dose levels of sodium monofluorophosphate (SMFP) and a single dose level of sodium fluoride (NaF) on DMFS, DMFT, and DFS Interproximal indices
7960166.TypesofStudies: Three-year, double blind, randomised clinical trial
7960166.TypesofInterventions: Four dose levels of sodium monofluorophosphate (SMFP) dentifrices (1500, 2000, 2500 ppm F) and a single dose level of sodium fluoride (NaF) dentifrice (2000 ppm F)

8556745.Objectives: Evaluate the caries-reducing effect of a modified toothpaste technique on approximal caries in preschool children
8556745.TypesofParticipants: Preschool children (4 years old at baseline)
8556745.TypesofInterventions: Modified toothpaste technique (specific instructions on spreading paste, expectoration, rinsing, and post-brushing behavior) vs no instruction; two fluoride dentifrices (A and B)
8556745.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Approximal caries increment (dfs) scored on bite-wing radiographs

17558688.Objectives: To investigate if co-treatment with a combination of GnRH agonist and GnRH antagonist may be useful in preserving ovarian function and fertility in younger women during chemotherapy
17558688.TypesofParticipants: young female patients (≤35 years) with malignant diseases or other severe medical conditions receiving chemotherapy
17558688.TypesofInterventions: simultaneous GnRH agonist and GnRH antagonist co-treatment during chemotherapy
17558688.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: normal basal hormonal profile (FSH, LH, estradiol), resumption of spontaneous menses, pregnancy/conception

16007873.Objectives: preservation of fertility and ovarian function during cytostatic treatment; review value and potential clinical applications of GnRH analogues and cryopreservation
16007873.TypesofParticipants: young patients of reproductive age suffering from malignant or systemic disease
16007873.TypesofInterventions: pharmacological therapy with GnRH analogues and cryopreservation of ovarian tissue

16254024.Objectives: to assess the activity of temporary ovarian suppression with goserelin to prevent chemotherapy-induced early menopause in young breast cancer patients
16254024.TypesofStudies: phase II clinical trial, two-stage optimal phase II Simon design
16254024.TypesofParticipants: premenopausal women with early breast cancer, median age 38 years (range 29-47)
16254024.TypesofInterventions: goserelin 3.6 mg every 4 weeks before and during chemotherapy (primarily CEF regimen)
16254024.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: resumption of ovarian function defined by resumption of menstrual activity or FSH value ≤ 40 IU/l within 12 months after last chemotherapy

12057729.Objectives: To compare the efficacy and safety of Bravelle s.c., Bravelle i.m., and Follistim s.c. in patients undergoing controlled ovarian hyperstimulation for IVF-ET
12057729.TypesofStudies: Open-label, randomized, parallel group, multicenter study
12057729.TypesofParticipants: Infertile premenopausal women with regular ovulatory menstrual cycles undergoing IVF-ET
12057729.TypesofInterventions: Bravelle (highly purified human FSH) subcutaneous or intramuscular; Follistim (recombinant follitropin-beta) subcutaneous; leuprolide acetate for down-regulation; hCG administration, oocyte retrieval, and ET
12057729.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Mean number of oocytes retrieved; patients with ET, chemical, clinical and continuing pregnancies; mean peak serum E2 levels; adverse events; injection site pain scores

18258763.Objectives: Evaluation of GnRH analogue administration to patients with Hodgkin lymphoma during chemotherapy and prevention of ovarian damage depending upon chemotherapy regimen
18258763.TypesofParticipants: Hodgkin lymphoma patients of fertile age (18-35 years)
18258763.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: gonadotrophin levels (FSH, LH) and premature ovarian failure (POF)

18824495.Objectives: Assess the effect of GnRH-analogues and oral contraceptives for fertility preservation in women during chemotherapy
18824495.TypesofInterventions: GnRH-analogues (GnRH-a) or oral contraceptives (OC) co-treatment with chemotherapy

15053070.Objectives: to find a quick, reliable and economic way to suppress the pituitary-gonadal axis by combining GnRH-agonists with GnRH-antagonists in order to preserve future gonadal function
15053070.TypesofParticipants: six postmenarchal young women (aged 15.4 +/- 0.7) years with haematological malignancies
15053070.TypesofInterventions: combination of D-Trp6-GnRH-a (3.75 mg) and cetrorelix (3 mg)
15053070.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: suppression of gonadotrophin secretion within 96 hours; future gonadal function

1835587.Objectives: Assess the effect of chemotherapy with or without buserelin on ovarian function in premenopausal women with breast cancer
1835587.TypesofInterventions: CAF plus buserelin; CMF with buserelin; CMF alone
1835587.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Serum oestradiol, FSH, LH levels; ovarian ablation/function

10899500.Objectives: To determine the efficacy and safety of Repronex SC as compared with Repronex IM and Pergonal IM in patients undergoing IVF
10899500.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal women with regular ovulatory menstrual cycles undergoing IVF for infertility attributable to tubal factors, endometriosis (stage I or II), or unknown factors
10899500.TypesofInterventions: Down-regulation with leuprolide acetate followed by up to 12 days of treatments with gonadotropins (Repronex SC, Repronex IM, or Pergonal IM), hCG administration, oocyte retrieval, and embryo transplant
10899500.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Mean number of oocytes retrieved, chemical, clinical, and continuing pregnancies, incidence of oocyte retrieval and embryo transfer, and peak serum E2 concentrations

16205599.Objectives: evaluate the administration of GnRh analogs to prevent gonadal toxicity associated with cancer treatment
16205599.TypesofParticipants: fertile women affected by oncohematological diseases (Hodgkin's lymphoma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, acute leukemia)
16205599.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ovarian function (menstrual cycle, blood ovarian hormones)

11223377.Objectives: Evaluate GnRH-a cotreatment to minimize gonadotoxic effect of chemotherapy and measure inhibin-A and -B as prognostic factors for resumption of ovarian function
11223377.TypesofStudies: prospective clinical protocol with control group
11223377.TypesofParticipants: women with lymphoma, leukemia, and nonmalignant diseases (e.g., systemic lupus erythematosus), aged 15-40 years
11223377.TypesofInterventions: monthly injection of depot D-TRP6-GnRH-a cotreatment with chemotherapy
11223377.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: resumption of spontaneous ovulation and menses, ovarian function, premature ovarian failure, FSH, LH, estradiol, inhibin-A and -B levels

18452079.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of gonadotropin-releasing hormone analog (GnRHa) treatment on preventing treatment-related ovarian failure and infertility in women with Hodgkin lymphoma
18452079.TypesofParticipants: women of reproductive age with Hodgkin lymphoma
18452079.TypesofInterventions: monthly triptorelin (gonadotropin-releasing hormone analog) in addition to frontline polychemotherapy with or without radiotherapy; for refractory/relapsed patients: salvage treatment with or without peripheral blood stem cell transplantation
18452079.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: recovery of regular menses, menstrual abnormalities, amenorrhea, correlation with age/disease stage/therapeutic load, conception after treatment

16388519.Objectives: To retrospectively evaluate the effect of Gn-RH analogue as ovarian protection during adjuvant chemotherapy in premenopausal women with early breast carcinoma.
16388519.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal women with high-risk, early breast carcinoma (median age 43 years, ER-positive or ER-negative, Stage II or III)
16388519.TypesofInterventions: Gn-RH analogue plus adjuvant chemotherapy (CMF, anthracycline-based, high-dose with autologous PBPC transplant, taxane for c-erb-2+); radiation therapy; aromatase inhibitor for ER-positive after chemo
16388519.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Normal menses resumption, pregnancies, recurrence-free survival at 5 and 10 years, overall survival at 5 and 10 years

18971681.Objectives: Description of the recent progress in fertility preservation despite gonadotoxic chemotherapy, using GnRH-agonists
18971681.TypesofParticipants: patients with hematologic diseases (Hodgkin lymphoma), young breast cancer patients, patients with nonmalignant autoimmune diseases (systemic lupus erythematosus), women of reproductive age exposed to gonadotoxic chemotherapy
18971681.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: chemotherapy-associated gonadotoxic effect, premature ovarian failure, menometrorrhagia during chemotherapy-induced thrombocytopenia, ovarian damage

17173900.Objectives: Preservation of ovarian function and reduction of gonadotoxicity related to chemotherapy
17173900.TypesofParticipants: young women with Hodgkin's disease receiving chemotherapy
17173900.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: spontaneous ovulation and menses, bone mineral density

19301406.TypesofParticipants: young cancer survivors, prepubertal children, adolescent children, adult survivors of childhood cancer
19301406.TypesofInterventions: ovarian cryopreservation, oocyte freezing, gonadal suppression with GnRH analogs, third party reproduction techniques

15853442.Objectives: discuss chemotherapy-induced ovarian failure manifestations and management
15853442.TypesofParticipants: female cancer survivors; women at high-risk of breast cancer
15853442.TypesofInterventions: hormonal replacement therapy (HRT); alternative treatments; therapies to preserve fertility and reduce ovarian damage
15853442.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ovarian failure; irreversible amenorrhoea; follicle stimulating hormone level ≥30 MIU/mL; estrogen levels; bone mass; fracture risk; hot flashes; osteoporosis; sexual dysfunction; infertility

12538505.TypesofParticipants: premenopausal women with breast cancer; women less than 50 years of age; women with good-risk early-stage breast cancer
12538505.TypesofInterventions: ovarian ablation/suppression; tamoxifen; adjuvant chemotherapy; surgical oophorectomy; radiation-induced ovarian failure; medical castration with luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone analogues; ovarian ablation/suppression combined with tamoxifen; other endocrine therapy
12538505.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: disease-free survival; overall survival; clinical benefit; long-term morbidity of estrogen deprivation

15817583.Objectives: To evaluate ovarian function and fertility after intensified CHOP (Mega-CHOP) in women with non-Hodgkin lymphoma
15817583.TypesofParticipants: women aged ≤40 years with aggressive non-Hodgkin lymphoma
15817583.TypesofInterventions: four cycles of intensified CHOP (cyclophosphamide 2000-3000 mg/m2 per cycle, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2, prednisone 100 mg/day every 3 weeks); some patients also received D-TRP6-GnRH analogue (Decapeptyl) for minimizing gonadal toxicity
15817583.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: premature ovarian failure (POF), fertility retention, spontaneous conception, delivery of healthy babies

14679153.Objectives: To compare sequential chemotherapy followed by goserelin with each modality alone for premenopausal lymph node-negative breast cancer
14679153.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal and perimenopausal women with lymph node-negative breast cancer
14679153.TypesofInterventions: Goserelin alone, CMF chemotherapy alone, CMF followed by goserelin

14501238.Objectives: Summarize recent progress in various attempts to prevent premature ovarian failure in young women with unconsumed fertility potential undergoing gonadotoxic chemotherapy
14501238.TypesofParticipants: adolescents and young women with systemic malignancies (lymphomas, leukaemia) undergoing gonadotoxic chemotherapy
14501238.TypesofInterventions: ovarian cryopreservation, auto- or xenotransplantation, in-vitro maturation of thawed primordial follicles, in-vitro fertilization, gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonist, disruption of acid sphingomyelinase gene or sphingosine-1-phosphate
14501238.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prevention of premature ovarian failure, preservation of fertility, safety and efficiency of oocyte/follicle/ovarian tissue cryopreservation, minimization of follicle depletion

19153828.Objectives: Examine if goserelin concomitant to CMF-chemotherapy protects the ovaries from premature failure in premenopausal breast cancer patients
19153828.TypesofInterventions: CMF-chemotherapy, goserelin, tamoxifen, goserelin plus tamoxifen, no endocrine treatment

18675959.Objectives: To determine whether GnRHa administration before and during combination chemotherapy for breast cancer could preserve posttreatment ovarian function in young women
18675959.TypesofParticipants: Eighty patients with unilateral adenocarcinoma of the breast and with no metastasis who had undergone modified radical mastectomy or breast-conserving surgery plus full axillary lymph node dissection
18675959.TypesofInterventions: combined GnRHa and chemotherapy or chemotherapy alone
18675959.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Return of spontaneous menstruation and ovulation; hormonal changes (FSH, LH, E2, P) during and after treatment

15588464.Objectives: Compare cetrorelix and leuprolide acetate for prevention of premature LH surge; assess benefit of adding r-hLH to cetrorelix treatment
15588464.TypesofInterventions: Leuprolide acetate (before ovarian stimulation with r-hFSH); cetrorelix 3 mg on stimulation day 7 with or without r-hLH 150 IU on days 7-10
15588464.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Number of MII oocytes retrieved; secondary: duration of stimulation, total r-hFSH dose, pregnancy rates, adverse events, estradiol per follicle

18197006.Objectives: Review current topics on adjuvant therapy for premenopausal patients with early breast cancer
18197006.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal patients with early breast cancer
18197006.TypesofInterventions: Chemotherapy, endocrine therapy (tamoxifen), luteinizing-hormone-releasing hormone agonists, aromatase inhibitors, ovarian function preservation during chemotherapy
18197006.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Recurrence, death, patient outcome, chemotherapy-induced amenorrhea, ovarian failure, infertility, ovarian function, menopausal symptoms

12182445.Objectives: To investigate hCG and insulin-stimulated progesterone production by human granulosa-lutein cells in vitro
12182445.TypesofParticipants: human granulosa-lutein cells (hGLC) from IVF-ET patients
12182445.TypesofInterventions: GnRH agonist, GnRH antagonist, hCG, insulin

16392882.Objectives: review of its use in the treatment of early breast cancer in premenopausal and perimenopausal women
16392882.TypesofParticipants: pre- or perimenopausal women with early, hormone receptor-positive breast cancer
16392882.TypesofInterventions: goserelin, adjuvant goserelin monotherapy, adjuvant chemotherapy, tamoxifen

17454195.Objectives: To evaluate the best method to assess the ovarian reserve and the ovarian protective effect of GnRH-analog in women with Hodgkin disease treated with chemotherapy
17454195.TypesofParticipants: Young women with Hodgkin disease treated with chemotherapy
17454195.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Serum FSH, LH, inhibin B, AMH, antral follicular count (AFC), menstrual function

16019373.Objectives: To explore the effects of a GnRH agonist depot (goserelin acetate) in women with Hodgkin's disease receiving chemotherapy while taking add-back on prevention of premature ovarian failure
16019373.TypesofInterventions: GnRH agonist depot (goserelin acetate) plus continuous combined estrogen-progestin preparation
16019373.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: premature ovarian failure, hormonal profile (FSH, LH, 17beta-estradiol, progesterone, inhibin B), resumption of menstruation, hypergonadotropic state (2 x FSH > 30 U/l), pregnancy

15477635.Objectives: Determine the role of ovarian ablation in primary and metastatic breast cancer management and identify unresolved issues
15477635.TypesofParticipants: premenopausal women, women under 50, hormone-receptor positive breast cancer patients, young breast cancer survivors
15477635.TypesofInterventions: ovarian ablation (surgical oophorectomy, ovarian irradiation), LHRH analogues, tamoxifen, cytotoxic chemotherapy

11371127.Objectives: to study whether gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) analogs could prevent the early onset of ovarian insufficiency postchemotherapy and protect fertility
11371127.TypesofParticipants: adolescent female cancer patients (premenarchal and postmenarchal)
11371127.TypesofInterventions: GnRH analogs (leuprolide acetate) administered before and during polychemotherapy
11371127.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ovarian function (menarche, menstruation, ovulatory cycles, pregnancy), hypergonadotrophic hypoestrogenic amenorrhea

15302283.Objectives: Compare the efficacy and safety of three different ratios of human-derived follicle-stimulating hormone/human menopausal gonadotropin (human-derived FSH:hMG, Bravelle and Repronex) mixed together in the same syringe and administered subcutaneously once daily, to in vitro fertilization (IVF) patients <34 years or 34 to 40 years of age.
15302283.TypesofStudies: Two randomized, prospective, age stratified, IVF studies.
15302283.TypesofInterventions: Pituitary suppression with leuprolide acetate, randomization to one of three treatment groups followed by gonadotropin stimulation for up to 15 days. FSH:hMG ratios: Group 1, 1:1 throughout; Group 2, 3:0 changed to 1:1 after GS day 5; Group 3, 2:1 increased to 3:1, 4:1, or 5:1 after GS day 5 as needed.
15302283.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Mean number of oocytes retrieved; peak estradiol levels; dose and duration of stimulation; implantation rates; adverse events; injection site pain; pregnancy and live birth rates.

11984088.Objectives: investigate protective effects on ovarian function, efficacy and tolerability of goserelin added to adjuvant chemotherapy for early breast cancer
11984088.TypesofParticipants: premenopausal patients with early breast cancer
11984088.TypesofInterventions: goserelin 3.6 mg every 28 days for 1 year added to adjuvant chemotherapy (cyclophosphamide, methotrexate and 5-fluorouracil or anthracycline-based regimen, some with high-dose chemotherapy and autologous peripheral blood progenitor cell transplantation); tamoxifen for 5 years in estrogen receptor-positive patients
11984088.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: resumption of normal menses, serum estradiol levels, disease-free survival, recurrence-free survival, overall survival, pregnancy, adverse events

10329050.Objectives: Assess long-term effect of GnRH-a combined with high-dose progestogens in treatment of atypical endometrial hyperplasia in surgical high-risk patients and women desiring fertility
10329050.TypesofParticipants: Patients with atypical endometrial hyperplasia who had surgical or anesthetic risk history or wished to preserve fertility potential
10329050.TypesofInterventions: 500 mg norethisterone acetate weekly for 3 months and 3.75 mg Triptorelin depot every month for 6 months
10329050.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Regression, persistence, recurrence, progression at 5-year follow-up

19339000.Objectives: To investigate methods of fertility preservation in younger women exposed to adjuvant chemotherapy for breast cancer
19339000.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal women exposed to adjuvant chemotherapy for breast cancer
19339000.TypesofInterventions: ovarian protection by gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, cryopreservation of embryos after in vitro fertilization, preservation of operatively sampled ovarian tissue or eggs after stimulation and puncture

12069736.Objectives: Compare the efficacy and safety of short acting buserelin and nafarelin intranasal spray versus long acting leuprorelin depot for pituitary desensitisation in women undergoing COH for IVF
12069736.TypesofParticipants: women undergoing controlled ovarian hyperstimulation for in-vitro fertilisation
12069736.TypesofInterventions: buserelin 150 microg nasal spray three times daily, nafarelin 400 microg nasal spray twice daily, leuprorelin depot 3.75 mg intramuscular or subcutaneous injection
12069736.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: clinical pregnancy rates per oocyte retrieval and per embryo transfer, ongoing pregnancy rates, incidence of side-effects (allergic nasal reactions), duration between starting GnRHa and beginning of next menstrual period, number of abandoned cycles

11697620.Objectives: Evaluate the efficacy and safety of leuprorelin in preventing heavy vaginal bleeding in nonmenopausal women undergoing myelosuppressive chemotherapy or radiochemotherapy for hematological malignancies
11697620.TypesofParticipants: nonmenopausal women with leukemia, lymphoma or myeloma and therapy-induced thrombocytopenia
11697620.TypesofInterventions: subcutaneous leuprorelin (GnRH agonist) with concomitant nomegestrol acetate for first 35 days
11697620.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prevention of heavy vaginal bleeding, incidence of spottings and metrorrhagias, adverse events

17010060.Objectives: To update on ovarian suppression/ablation as adjuvant therapy for premenopausal early breast cancer
17010060.TypesofStudies: Prospective randomized trials, multiple-arm studies, ongoing trials
17010060.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal women with early breast cancer (estrogen receptor-positive)
17010060.TypesofInterventions: Ovarian ablation (surgical ovariectomy or irradiation); LHRHa (goserelin); tamoxifen; chemotherapy; combined regimens
17010060.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Disease-free survival; risk of recurrence; tolerability

11937116.Objectives: compare efficacy of Dienogest versus Decapeptyl as consolidation therapy for surgery in the treatment of endometriosis
11937116.TypesofStudies: multicenter, open, randomized, parallel-group clinical trial
11937116.TypesofParticipants: Women with grade 2, 3, and 4 endometriosis at initial laparoscopy
11937116.TypesofInterventions: Dienogest 1 mg tablet daily; Decapeptyl 3.75 mg IM injection every 4 weeks
11937116.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: change in Revised American Fertility Society score at post-treatment laparoscopy

1388047.Objectives: To evaluate the effectiveness of aromatase inhibition alone or in combination with GnRH agonists for treating premenopausal breast cancer patients
1388047.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal breast cancer patients, including those who relapsed from goserelin treatment
1388047.TypesofInterventions: Aromatase inhibitors (4-hydroxyandrostenedione) and GnRH agonist (goserelin) alone or in combination
1388047.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Plasma oestrogen levels and objective clinical response

10981642.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of a GnRH agonist (GnRH-a) co-treatment in preserving ovarian function and minimizing gonadotoxicity in young women with systemic lupus erythematosus treated with chemotherapy.
10981642.TypesofParticipants: Young women with SLE and other severe connective tissue diseases
10981642.TypesofInterventions: Monthly GnRH agonist (Decapeptyl) co-treatment with alkylating agent chemotherapy (cyclophosphamide or chlorambucil)
10981642.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Incidence of premature ovarian failure (POF), hypergonadotropic amenorrhea, hormone levels (FSH, LH, progesterone, 17-beta-estradiol), and ovarian sonography findings

18230566.Objectives: to review fertility preservation techniques and infertility management in young breast cancer patients
18230566.TypesofInterventions: embryo cryopreservation, oocyte cryopreservation, cryopreservation of ovarian tissue, coprescription of LH-RH analogues during chemotherapy, assisted reproduction techniques (IVF, oocyte donation, embryo donation)

18820006.Objectives: determine the clinical evidence concerning GnRH agonists as ovarian protectors during gonadotoxic therapies
18820006.TypesofInterventions: GnRH agonist co-treatment concomitant with chemotherapy
18820006.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ovarian function preservation, premature ovarian failure

16697968.Objectives: Prevention of gonadal toxicity and preservation of gonadal function and fertility in young women with systemic lupus erythematosus treated by cyclophosphamide
16697968.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal women with SLE, young women
16697968.TypesofInterventions: Cyclophosphamide treatment, gonadotropin-releasing hormone analogue (GnRH-a) injection
16697968.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Gonadal function, fertility, premature ovarian failure (POF), FSH and LH concentrations, hypergonadotropic amenorrhea

19015974.Objectives: To examine ovarian reserve following chemotherapy in women with Hodgkin's disease
19015974.TypesofInterventions: ovarian tissue cryopreservation (OTCP) and gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonist (GnRH-a) co-treatment prior to ABVD chemotherapy
19015974.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: menstrual cycle resumption, amenorrhea, ovarian failure, conception

11287012.Objectives: To review the latest progress in the prevention of ovarian failure induced by chemo/radiotherapy, as well as the latest advances in culture technology and transplantation of frozen-thawed ovarian tissue
11287012.TypesofInterventions: GnRH analogs, ovarian tissue cryopreservation, transplantation, in vitro culture

12425754.Objectives: To review the role of ovarian suppression in management of premenopausal women with early-stage breast cancer
12425754.TypesofParticipants: Premenopausal women with hormone-responsive early-stage breast cancer
12425754.TypesofInterventions: Ovarian suppression with GnRH analogue (e.g., goserelin), chemotherapy

17159194.Objectives: compare quality of life and menopausal symptoms among premenopausal patients with lymph node-negative breast cancer receiving chemotherapy, goserelin, or sequential combination, and investigate differential effects by age
17159194.TypesofParticipants: pre- and perimenopausal women with lymph node-negative breast cancer
17159194.TypesofInterventions: six courses of classical CMF chemotherapy; ovarian suppression with goserelin for 24 months; six courses of classical CMF followed by 18 months of goserelin
17159194.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: quality of life, menopausal symptoms (amenorrhea, hot flashes), hot flashes scores

16765589.Objectives: Compare CMF chemotherapy versus goserelin as adjuvant therapy for node-negative, hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer in premenopausal patients
16765589.TypesofParticipants: premenopausal patients with node-negative, hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer
16765589.TypesofInterventions: three cycles of cyclophosphamide, methotrexate, and 5-fluorouracil (CMF) chemotherapy; goserelin every 28 days for 2 years
16765589.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: first-failure events (ipsilateral locoregional recurrence, contralateral breast cancer, distant failure, death without recurrence), deaths of any cause

17386122.TypesofParticipants: premenopausal women with hormone receptor-positive breast cancer
17386122.TypesofInterventions: LH-RH agonists (e.g., goserelin), tamoxifen, chemotherapy (CMF), aromatase inhibitors, surgical oophorectomy
17386122.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: recurrence, death, disease-free survival, estradiol and LH suppression

10509550.Objectives: To assess the efficacy and tolerability of a calcium antagonist/beta-blocker fixed combination tablet used as first-line antihypertensive therapy in comparison with an angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor and placebo
10509550.TypesofParticipants: Patients with uncomplicated essential hypertension (diastolic blood pressure between 95 and 110 mm Hg)
10509550.TypesofInterventions: Felodipine-metoprolol combination tablet (Logimax) 5/50 mg daily; enalapril 10 mg daily; placebo (dose doubling allowed if needed)
10509550.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Blood pressure control (diastolic ≤90 mmHg 24 h after dose); incidence of adverse events; withdrawal due to adverse events; change in patients' well-being

10496190.Objectives: evaluate clinical characteristics and response to therapy of black compared with white patients with heart failure
10496190.TypesofStudies: post-hoc analysis of Vasodilator-Heart Failure Trials (V-HeFT I and V-HeFT II)
10496190.TypesofParticipants: male patients with heart failure (black and white)
10496190.TypesofInterventions: hydralazine plus isosorbide dinitrate, enalapril, placebo
10496190.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: mortality, hospitalization for congestive heart failure, blood pressure, cardiac size, plasma norepinephrine levels, plasma renin activity

10099045.Objectives: assesses how angiotensin II influences renal disease and explores the effectiveness of losartan in patients with renal disease
10099045.TypesofParticipants: patients with renal disease, renally impaired patients, patients with renal insufficiency or failure
10099045.TypesofInterventions: losartan (selective A-II receptor blocker), ACE inhibitors, combination therapy
10099045.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: effectiveness for hypertension, renal hemodynamic effects, uricosuric effect, absence of cough

10063787.Objectives: To assess the efficacy and safety of bisoprolol/6.25-mg hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ), amlodipine, and enalapril in black and nonblack patients
10063787.TypesofStudies: Pooled analysis of two comparative studies with subgroup analysis
10063787.TypesofParticipants: Hypertensive patients (black and nonblack) with sitting diastolic blood pressure 95-114 mmHg
10063787.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Mean change from baseline in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, blood pressure control (diastolic BP ≤ 90 mmHg), incidence of drug-related adverse events, discontinuation rate due to lack of blood pressure control or adverse experiences

10232492.Objectives: survey primary care physicians' antihypertensive prescribing practices and their treatment recommendations for patients with comorbidities
10232492.TypesofParticipants: primary care internists, family practitioners, and general practitioners
10232492.TypesofInterventions: antihypertensive agents (including ACE inhibitors, selective beta-blockers)
10232492.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prescribing practices, initial treatment recommendations, reported utilization for specific comorbidities, adherence to JNC guidelines

10340840.TypesofInterventions: antihypertensive drug therapy, diuretics, calcium antagonists, renin-angiotensin-aldosterone-kinin system targeting
10340840.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: blood pressure control, target-organ damage (stroke, congestive heart failure, renal failure), cardiovascular disease complications, proteinuria

10635087.Objectives: To evaluate the quality of drug prescription in a primary care clinic in Geneva
10635087.TypesofStudies: prescription analysis comparing data from previous surveys
10635087.TypesofParticipants: 701 outpatients attending first consultations between May 14 and June 6, 1997
10635087.TypesofInterventions: NSAIDs, paracetamol, muscle relaxants, psychotropic drugs, ACE inhibitors, calcium antagonists, beta-blockers, diuretics
10635087.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prescription rates, proportion of patients prescribed each drug, adherence to guidelines

10353294.Objectives: To review renal protective effects of antihypertensive drugs and current status
10353294.TypesofParticipants: Patients with diabetic and nondiabetic nephropathies, hypertensive patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus
10353294.TypesofInterventions: Antihypertensive drugs including ACE inhibitors, calcium antagonists (dihydropyridine and nondihydropyridine), angiotensin II-receptor antagonists; tight blood pressure control
10353294.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Blood pressure reduction, proteinuria, urinary albumin excretion, progression to overt nephropathy, decline in glomerular filtration rate, renal failure progression, cardiovascular complications

10052649.Objectives: Evaluate therapeutic potentials of new classes of antihypertensive drugs
10052649.TypesofInterventions: Losartan (angiotensin II receptor antagonist), Fenoldopam (dopamine D1-receptor agonist), Kentaserin (serotonin 5-HT2A receptor antagonist), Monatepil (dual alpha-receptor and calcium channel blocker), Alatriopril (dual ACE and endopeptidase inhibitor)

10408592.Objectives: Compare eprosartan and enalapril on cough and blood pressure in unselected hypertensive patients
10408592.TypesofStudies: Double-blind, randomised, parallel-group, multicentre, international study
10408592.TypesofParticipants: Unselected hypertensive patients (528 patients with hypertension)
10408592.TypesofInterventions: Eprosartan (200 or 300 mg b.i.d.) and enalapril (5-20 mg u.i.d.), with optional addition of hydrochlorothiazide (12.5-25 mg/day)
10408592.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Primary: incidence of persistent dry cough not due to upper respiratory infection; Secondary: change in sitting diastolic blood pressure, overall incidence of cough, response rate (sitting diastolic BP <90 mmHg or ≥10 mmHg reduction from baseline)

10136603.Objectives: to analyse the cost-effectiveness of the switch towards more expensive antihypertensive drugs in Sweden
10136603.TypesofInterventions: switch from diuretics and beta-blockers to ACE-inhibitors and calcium-antagonists
10136603.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: incremental cost per life-year gained, risk reduction for coronary heart disease

10588090.Objectives: to better define the long-term benefit of angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs) in the treatment of hypertension and heart failure
10588090.TypesofParticipants: hypertensive patients, patients with heart failure, type 1 diabetes with proteinuria, post-myocardial infarction with systolic dysfunction
10588090.TypesofInterventions: angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors and angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs)

10491730.Objectives: Clinical implications of indapamide sustained release 1.5 mg in hypertension
10491730.TypesofStudies: Multicentre European clinical trials, comparative study (LIVE study)
10491730.TypesofParticipants: Hypertensive patients, including elderly, patients with diabetes, target organ damage, left ventricular hypertrophy
10491730.TypesofInterventions: Indapamide SR 1.5 mg; comparators: amlodipine, hydrochlorothiazide, enalapril
10491730.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Blood pressure control, left ventricular mass index, circulating K+ levels, efficacy, tolerability

10451035.Objectives: To test whether the haemodynamic effect of acute intrabrachial administration of captopril is related to the circulating and/or vascular renin-angiotensin system in hypertensive patients.
10451035.TypesofParticipants: hypertensive patients with primary aldosteronism and suppressed plasma renin activity, low renin essential hypertensive patients, normal renin essential hypertensive patients, high renin renovascular hypertensive patients
10451035.TypesofInterventions: intrabrachial administration of captopril (0.25, 2.5, 25 microg/100 ml/min per 20 min each dose)

10423078.Objectives: Determine effects of newer versus older classes of blood pressure lowering drugs in patients with hypertension; and blood pressure lowering treatments versus untreated or less treated control conditions in patient groups at high risk of cardiovascular events
