27146448.Objectives: association between Medicaid insurance and clinical characteristics, treatment, or outcomes of patients with atrial fibrillation
27146448.TypesofStudies: registry-based cohort study using data from ORBIT-AF outpatient registry
27146448.TypesofParticipants: adults with atrial fibrillation, Medicaid vs. non-Medicaid patients
27146448.TypesofInterventions: oral anticoagulation (warfarin or novel oral anticoagulants: dabigatran, rivaroxaban)
27146448.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: proportion prescribed OAC, proportion prescribed NOACs, time in therapeutic range, all-cause mortality, stroke/systemic embolism, major bleeding

26521717.Objectives: Evaluate perioperative outcomes among patients continuing aspirin therapy and those in whom treatment is stopped preoperatively during laparoscopic partial nephrectomy
26521717.TypesofParticipants: Patients on chronic aspirin therapy undergoing laparoscopic partial nephrectomy
26521717.TypesofInterventions: Laparoscopic partial nephrectomy; perioperative aspirin continuation vs discontinuation
26521717.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Intraoperative blood loss, bleeding related complications, major postoperative complications, thromboembolic events, operative time, transfusion rate, length of hospital stay, rehospitalization rate, surgical margin status

26123057.Objectives: Determine if an aggressive approach to VTE prophylaxis would reduce VTE rates on the inpatient vascular surgical service
26123057.TypesofParticipants: vascular inpatients from a single institution
26123057.TypesofInterventions: subcutaneous heparin initiation within 24 hours of admission, intermittent compression devices, compression stockings
26123057.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: overall rate of VTE, DVT rate, pulmonary embolism rate

25524184.Objectives: To compare the outcome of transarterial angioembolization (TAE) and surgery with endoscopically unmanageable non-variceal hemorrhage of the upper gastrointestinal tract
25524184.TypesofParticipants: patients with endoscopically unmanageable non-variceal upper gastrointestinal hemorrhage
25524184.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: re-bleeding, post procedural complications, 30-day mortality, blood transfusions, hospital stay

26928310.Objectives: Present a case of anti-VEGF-related thrombotic microangiopathy in a child with nephrotic syndrome
26928310.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: development of nephrotic syndrome and thrombotic microangiopathy; spontaneous remission within 3 months

26879545.Objectives: Feasibility and safety of weekly intraperitoneal versus intravenous paclitaxel therapy from the day of radical surgery for gastric cancer
26879545.TypesofParticipants: patients with linitis plastica or T4 cancer with high risk of peritoneal metastasis or recurrence and resectable cancer
26879545.TypesofInterventions: intraperitoneal paclitaxel (60 mg/m2) and intravenous paclitaxel (80 mg/m2) administered on day of surgery and on days 14, 21, 28, 42, 49, 56
26879545.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 2-year survival rate; safety endpoints including surgical complications, adverse events, relative dose intensity, death, anaphylaxis

26692120.Objectives: Evaluate the efficacy and safety of dabigatran compared with warfarin during perioperative period of catheter ablation for non-valvular atrial fibrillation
26692120.TypesofParticipants: Patients with drug-resistant paroxysmal non-valvular atrial fibrillation (NVAF)
26692120.TypesofInterventions: Dabigatran vs warfarin during perioperative period of catheter ablation
26692120.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Incidence of embolism, presence of intracardiac thrombus, incidence of bleeding, thromboembolic events, duration of hospitalization, vascular death, all-cause mortality, net clinical benefit, adverse events

27401184.Objectives: to identify risk factors for mortality in patients admitted to intensive care units with pneumonia
27401184.TypesofStudies: secondary analysis of a randomized controlled trial (PROTECT)
27401184.TypesofParticipants: patients admitted to intensive care units with a primary diagnosis of pneumonia

12057100.Objectives: To evaluate photodynamic therapy as an adjuvant therapy to surgical resection for malignant pleural mesothelioma.
12057100.TypesofInterventions: photodynamic therapy (PDT) with photosensitizing agent and light

12730818.Objectives: to identify the effectiveness of perioperative pneumoperitoneum to prevent air leak after the lobectomy-bilobectomy operations for lung cancer
12730818.TypesofParticipants: patients who had lobectomy-bilobectomy operations for lung cancer and whose remnant lung had failed to fill the half of the hemithoracic cavity under 30 cm H(2)O positive pressure ventilation during the operation with totally relaxed diaphragm
12730818.TypesofInterventions: perioperative pneumoperitoneum vs without perioperative pneumoperitoneum
12730818.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: duration of postoperative air leak, total chest tube drainage time

11673215.Objectives: To compare standard therapy with and without nasal-mask NPPV in patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory insufficiency after lung resection
11673215.TypesofParticipants: patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory insufficiency after lung resection
11673215.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: need for endotracheal mechanical ventilation (ETMV); in-hospital and 120-day mortality rates; duration of stay in the ICU; duration of in-hospital stay

15223462.Objectives: To report a case of successful extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) therapy combined with methylprednisolone in a patient with pneumonia-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome after pneumonectomy
15223462.TypesofParticipants: a patient with pneumonia-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome after pneumonectomy
15223462.TypesofInterventions: extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) and methylprednisolone therapy
15223462.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: improvement of pulmonary and systemic organ function

15919483.Objectives: To find potential donors from cases without lung procurement
15919483.TypesofStudies: Retrospective cohort study, univariable and multivariable logistic regression analysis
15919483.TypesofParticipants: Potential lung donors (cases considered for lung procurement)
15919483.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Lung harvesting (procurement), P/F ratio, heart rate, respiratory rate, PEEP, chest X-ray abnormalities, blood chemistries, sputum culture, steroid administration

16276197.Objectives: describe lung isolation and selective application of continuous positive airway pressure using an endobronchial blocker in a patient with sickle cell disease and unilateral necrotizing Clostridium perfringens pneumonia
16276197.TypesofParticipants: 12-yr-old male with sickle cell disease and persistent necrotizing pneumonia of the left lung
16276197.TypesofInterventions: endobronchial blocker placement into left main stem bronchus for lung isolation and application of continuous positive airway pressure to the left lung for 48 hrs
16276197.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: lung aeration within 48 hrs, avoidance of lung resection, successful extubation 2 days after blocker removal

17257820.Objectives: To investigate whether prophylactic use of noninvasive pressure support ventilation (NIPSV) administered pre- and postoperatively reduces postoperative pulmonary function impairment
17257820.TypesofParticipants: Patients with preoperative FEV1 <70% predicted, scheduled for elective lobectomy for lung cancer
17257820.TypesofInterventions: Noninvasive pressure support ventilation (NIPSV) pre- and postoperatively
17257820.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Arterial blood gases (PaO2), pulmonary function (FVC, FEV1), hospital stay, incidence of major atelectasis

15648983.Objectives: evaluate if oxygenation could be improved by use of a simple oxygen reservoir or application of 5 cmH2O continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) to the non-ventilated lung compared with an open airway
15648983.TypesofParticipants: patients with lung malignancy undergoing thoracotomy requiring at least 60 minutes of one-lung ventilation before lung lobe excision
15648983.TypesofInterventions: open airway, oxygen reservoir, continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) 5 cmH2O applied to the non-ventilated lung
15648983.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: arterial oxygenation measured by arterial blood gas (PaO2)

17484789.Objectives: to debate the data for and against the more widespread application of NPPV; assist the clinician to identify both good and poor candidates for NPPV
17484789.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: intubation rate, mortality, hospital stay, and other important clinical outcomes

17505679.Objectives: To weigh the beneficial and undesirable effects of thoracic epidural anesthesia on intraoperative respiratory mechanics.
17505679.TypesofParticipants: Patients scheduled for partial lung resection
17505679.TypesofInterventions: Active thoracic epidural anesthesia (15 ml 0.5% bupivacaine with 1:200,000 epinephrine and 2 mg morphine) versus placebo (15 ml 0.9% saline with same additives)
17505679.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Lower inflection point (LIP), respiratory system compliance, lung compliance

19325478.Objectives: To evaluate whether high-frequency percussive ventilation (HFPV) improves oxygenation, secretion removal, and reduces hospital length of stay compared to CPAP during thoracotomy for pulmonary resection.
19325478.TypesofParticipants: Patients undergoing elective pulmonary partial resection; specifically chronic obstructive pulmonary disease patients
19325478.TypesofInterventions: High-frequency percussive ventilation (HFPV) versus continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) for nondependent lung; both groups with dependent lung on continuous mechanical ventilation
19325478.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Oxygenation (PaO2), amount of secretions, sputum production, length of stay, cardiocirculatory variables, blood gas analysis, SpO2, body temperature, chest radiography findings

18453351.Objectives: Review anesthetic considerations in candidates for lung volume reduction surgery
18453351.TypesofParticipants: Patients with severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease undergoing lung volume reduction surgery
18453351.TypesofInterventions: Anesthetic management including induction, lung isolation, fluid management, ventilatory support, and thoracic epidural analgesia

17992482.Objectives: Review of clinical advances in advanced COPD
17992482.TypesofParticipants: Patients with advanced COPD, including hypercapnic patients, patients with predominant emphysema, and 'pink puffers'
17992482.TypesofInterventions: Oxygen therapy, inhalative antibiotics (aminoglycosides), long-acting morphine, noninvasive ventilation, physical training, lung volume reduction surgery, bullectomy, lobectomy, endoscopic lung volume reduction, lung transplantation
17992482.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Performance limitation, dyspnea, quality of life, life span, hospital admissions, mortality, hypercapnia reduction

20159726.Objectives: evaluate the effects of noninvasive positive pressure ventilation (NPPV) on postoperative pulmonary function recovery in patients undergoing thoracic surgeries
20159726.TypesofInterventions: noninvasive positive pressure ventilation (NPPV) on the basis of conventional treatment
20159726.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: lung function parameters, volume of residual cavity, postoperative pulmonary complications

20887415.Objectives: compare pulmonary function between COPD and healthy-lung patients during operative and post-operative periods
20887415.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing planned lobectomy for cancer with healthy lung function or moderate COPD
20887415.TypesofInterventions: ventilation without external PEEP, 9 ml/kg tidal volume during two-lung ventilation reduced to 6 ml/kg during one-lung ventilation
20887415.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: peroperative gas exchange (PaO2, PaO2/FiO2), venous admixture, respiratory mechanical parameters, post-operative spirometric measurements (forced vital capacity, forced expiratory volume)

21357311.Objectives: determine whether atelectasis can be avoided and if postoperative lung function is improved
21357311.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: atelectasis, postoperative lung function, pulmonary complications

20829341.Objectives: To test whether protective ventilatory settings attenuate lung impairment during one-lung ventilation compared with conventional ventilation in patients undergoing lung resection surgery.
20829341.TypesofParticipants: patients with American Society of Anesthesiology physical status 1-2 scheduled for elective lobectomy
20829341.TypesofInterventions: protective ventilation strategy (FIO2 0.5, Vt 6 mL/kg, positive end-expiratory pressure 5 cm H2O, pressure-controlled ventilation) during one-lung ventilation
20829341.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: composite primary end point: PaO2/FIO2 < 300 mm Hg and/or presence of newly developed lung lesions (lung infiltration and atelectasis) within 72 hours; safety: SpO2, PaCO2, peak inspiratory pressure

21515479.Objectives: To investigate the effects of different ventilation modes for one lung ventilation anesthesia on arterial blood-gas, airway pressure, intrapulmonary shunt, and F(A)/F(I) changes in patients receiving sevoflurane inhalation
21515479.TypesofParticipants: ASA class II-III patients with lung cancer undergoing pulmonary lobectomy
21515479.TypesofInterventions: Different ventilation modes (volume-controlled ventilation without PEEP, pressure-controlled ventilation, small tidal volume ventilation with PEEP) during one-lung ventilation anesthesia with sevoflurane inhalation
21515479.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Arterial blood-gas, airway pressure, intrapulmonary shunt, F(A)/F(I) changes, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, SpO2, peak pressure, pulmonary compliance

21675066.Objectives: To test mini-ventilation to the non-dependent lung and compare it to continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) to improve oxygenation during lung resection
21675066.TypesofParticipants: adult patients undergoing lung resection surgery
21675066.TypesofInterventions: mini-ventilation (portable time-cycled ventilator, respiratory rate 8 breaths/min, tidal volume 0.1-0.15 l) and continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) of 5 cmH2O applied alternately to the non-dependent lung
21675066.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: arterial oxygen partial pressure, peak inspiratory pressure, dynamic compliance in the dependent lung, surgeon's evaluation of surgical field exposure

2154154.Objectives: To compare deposition of aerosol to the lung from a metered-dose inhaler (MDI) and aerosol holding chamber and from a jet nebulizer in ventilator-dependent patients
2154154.TypesofParticipants: Ventilator-dependent patients receiving assisted ventilation and inhaled bronchodilators because of airflow limitation
2154154.TypesofInterventions: 4 puffs (800 micrograms) of radiolabeled fenoterol by MDI plus aerosol holding chamber vs 1.75 ml (1,750 micrograms) of radiolabeled fenoterol solution by jet nebulizer
2154154.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Lung deposition measured as percent of given dose

22613197.Objectives: To evaluate the prophylactic use of helmet CPAP after pulmonary lobectomy in preventing acute respiratory failure and improving oxygenation
22613197.TypesofInterventions: Helmet CPAP (2 cycles for 120 min alternating with analog oxygen therapy) vs continuous oxygen therapy (air-entrainment mask, FIO2 0.4)
22613197.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: P(aO2)/F(IO2) ratio, incidence of postoperative complications, mortality, length of hospital stay, ICU readmission

2198997.Objectives: to review non-conventional techniques for ventilatory support, including high frequency ventilation, tracheal insufflation, constant flow ventilation, extracorporeal membrane oxygenation, and extracorporeal carbon dioxide removal
2198997.TypesofParticipants: patients with respiratory failure; neonates; adults
2198997.TypesofInterventions: high frequency ventilation (HFV); tracheal insufflation (TRIO) of oxygen; constant flow ventilation (CFV); extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO); extracorporeal carbon dioxide removal with low positive pressure ventilation (ECCO2R-LFPPV)
2198997.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: adequate gas exchange; ventilation; oxygenation; PaCO2 levels; survival

21968296.Objectives: quantify CPAP effects upon lung function and functional capacity in early postoperative lung resection, and evaluate if CPAP prolongs air leak through the chest drain
21968296.TypesofParticipants: patients in the postoperative period of lung resection
21968296.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: arterial blood gas analysis, peak expiratory flow (PEF), respiratory muscle strength, spirometry, 6-min walk test (6MWT), air leak through the chest drain

23497934.Objectives: To demonstrate that preoperative non-invasive ventilation for 7 days, at home, reduces postoperative respiratory and cardiovascular complications of lung resection surgery in high-risk population
23497934.TypesofStudies: Prospective, randomized, controlled open-label multicenter study
23497934.TypesofParticipants: Patients suitable for lobectomy or segmentectomy for primary bronchial carcinoma with obstructive or restrictive lung disease, obesity, or chronic cardiac insufficiency
23497934.TypesofInterventions: Preoperative non-invasive ventilation at home for 7 days
23497934.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Composite of all respiratory and cardiac complications

24496783.Objectives: assess the efficacy of prophylactic CPAP on the Pa(O2)/FI(O2) ratio measured the day after surgery in patients undergoing lung resection surgery
24496783.TypesofInterventions: Boussignac CPAP at 5-7 cm H2O during the first 6 h after surgery; supplemental oxygen through a Venturi mask
24496783.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Pa(O2)/FI(O2) ratio; chest radiograph; postoperative course; incidence of postoperative pulmonary complications; stay in PACU and hospital

24474079.Objectives: to assess the efficacy of continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) associated with physiotherapy, compared with physiotherapy alone after lung resection
24474079.TypesofInterventions: CPAP (continuous positive airway pressure) associated with physiotherapy vs physiotherapy alone
24474079.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: oxygenation index, Borg scale, pain scale, presence of thoracic drains and air losses

23106907.Objectives: To evaluate the effectiveness of a standardized physiotherapy protocol in reducing pulmonary complications after lung resection in children.
23106907.TypesofStudies: retrospective and prospective, interventional, descriptive, and quantitative study
23106907.TypesofInterventions: standardized physiotherapy protocol including mask with positive expiratory pressure of 10 cm H2O, expiratory rib cage compression, coughing, lifting the upper limbs, and ambulation starting within 4 hours after surgery and continuing 3 times daily
23106907.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: postoperative atelectasis, need for fiberoptic bronchoscopy, time of chest tube removal, hospital stay

25033701.Objectives: evaluate an 'open lung' ventilation strategy using low tidal volumes, low respiratory rate, low FiO2, and high continuous positive airway pressure in patients undergoing major lung resections
25033701.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing major lung resections (twelve consecutive patients, open thoracotomies for lobectomies and segmentectomies)
25033701.TypesofInterventions: open lung ventilation strategy (low tidal volumes, low respiratory rate, low FiO2, high continuous positive airway pressure) compared to conventional ventilator settings
25033701.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: oxygenation, lung compliance, postresection oxygenation (SpO2/FiO2), postoperative chest x-ray findings (atelectasis or infiltrates), need for supplemental oxygen, hospital stay, complications, mortality

26407182.Objectives: To assess the efficacy and safety of NIPPV for preventing complications in patients who underwent pulmonary resection for lung cancer
26407182.TypesofStudies: Randomised clinical trials (RCTs) and quasi-randomised trial
26407182.TypesofParticipants: Patients who underwent pulmonary resection for lung cancer
26407182.TypesofInterventions: Non-invasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV) compared with no intervention or conventional respiratory therapy
26407182.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Pulmonary complications, intubation rates, mortality, postoperative consumption of antibiotics, length of intensive care unit stay, length of hospital stay, adverse effects related to NIPPV

2519936.Objectives: evaluate technical problems of blind insertion of intrapleural catheters and intraoperative analgesic effects of bupivacaine
2519936.TypesofInterventions: blind insertion of intrapleural catheters with injection of 0.5% bupivacaine or isotonic saline; standard anesthesia with isoflurane, oxygen, and muscle relaxant
2519936.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: catheter position, lung surface holes, pneumothorax, plasma bupivacaine concentrations, isoflurane requirement

26263797.Objectives: To investigate the effects of a 1:1 inspiratory-to-expiratory (I:E) ventilation ratio on oxygenation and respiratory mechanics during one-lung ventilation in patients with low diffusion capacity of lung for carbon monoxide.
26263797.TypesofParticipants: Patients with preoperative DLCO less than 80% scheduled for lung lobectomy requiring one-lung ventilation under general anesthesia
26263797.TypesofInterventions: Pressure-controlled ventilation with 5 cm H2O PEEP and tidal volume 5-8 mL/kg, comparing 1:1 vs 1:2 I:E ratio during one-lung ventilation, applied in crossover order
26263797.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Arterial oxygen tension, arterial carbon dioxide tension, peak airway pressure, mean airway pressure, dynamic compliance, physiologic dead space, intrapulmonary shunt fraction

27310989.Objectives: To investigate indirect relationship between changes of CO2 expirogram and selective lung recruitment; compare ventilation parameter adjustment based on CO2 expirogram versus preset settings during OLV in right-side thoracic surgery patients
27310989.TypesofParticipants: Hypoxemic patients during one-lung ventilation undergoing right-side thoracic surgery
27310989.TypesofInterventions: Adjustable ventilation parameters (tidal volume, respiratory rate, positive end-expiratory pressure, inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio) based on phase III slopes of CO2 expirogram
27310989.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: SpO2, phase III slopes of CO2 expirogram, oxygenation and gas exchange

28437264.Objectives: To investigate the effect of MgSO4 on pulmonary function after video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS)
28437264.TypesofParticipants: patients scheduled for pulmonary lobectomy or segmentectomy via VATS
28437264.TypesofInterventions: MgSO4 50 mg/kg intravenously for 10 min, followed by a continuous infusion of 15 mg/kg/h during surgery
28437264.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: pulmonary function tests (FEV1, FVC, peak expiratory flow rate); rocuronium requirement; postoperative patient-controlled analgesia consumption; postoperative pneumonia

27166086.Objectives: To investigate protective effects of additional ipsilateral ventilation of low tidal volume and high frequency on lung functions in patients receiving lobectomy
27166086.TypesofInterventions: Additional ipsilateral ventilation of low tidal volume (1-2 ml/kg) and high frequency (40 times/min)
27166086.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: PaO2/FiO2 ratio; lung injury score (interstitial edema, alveolar edema, neutrophil infiltration, alveolar congestion)

26547348.Objectives: To observe the effect of one lung ventilation (OLV) preconditioning on perioperative oxygenation during pediatric video-assisted thoracoscopic surgeries (VATS)
26547348.TypesofParticipants: Children aged 5 days to 11 years undergoing VATS for empyema, mediastinal tumor, diaphragmatic hernia, pulmonary lobectomy, esophageal disease
26547348.TypesofInterventions: OLV preconditioning protocol with sequential 5-8 min OLV and 5 min TLV using a 5-Fr pediatric endobronchial blocker at FiO2 1.0, with PEEP when SpO2 < 95%
26547348.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Oxygenation index (PaO2/FiO2), SpO2, surgical conditions, postoperative ventilation time, length of stay, postoperative atelectasis

3318740.Objectives: Compare conventional ventilation versus high-frequency jet ventilation (HFJV) in relation to postoperative morbidity in thoracic surgery
3318740.TypesofInterventions: Conventional ventilation (double-lumen endobronchial tube and collapse of one lung); high-frequency jet ventilation (HFJV)
3318740.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Incidence of postoperative chest infections; chest drain volumes; length of postoperative hospital stay; arterial blood oxygen tensions at 4 hours, 24 hours, and 7 days postoperatively

367546.Objectives: Management of patients with lung contusion
367546.TypesofParticipants: patients with lung contusion (pulmonary contusion from chest trauma)
367546.TypesofInterventions: oxygen therapy, ventilatory support, broad-spectrum antibiotics

28598916.Objectives: Assess whether protective lung ventilation during one-lung ventilation is associated with less clinically relevant pulmonary morbidity after lung resection
28598916.TypesofStudies: prospective observational study with propensity score matching
28598916.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing pulmonary resection with one-lung ventilation
28598916.TypesofInterventions: protective lung ventilation (tidal volume <8 mL/kg) vs non-protective lung ventilation (≥8 mL/kg) with crystalloid restriction and peak airway pressure <30 cm H2O
28598916.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: pneumonia and/or acute respiratory distress syndrome

9228366.Objectives: To investigate the short-term effects of noninvasive ventilatory support (NIVS) on pulmonary gas exchange, ventilatory pattern, systemic hemodynamics, and pleural air leaks in patients submitted to elective lung resection
9228366.TypesofStudies: Prospective, randomized, parallel, and controlled investigation
9228366.TypesofParticipants: Patients electively submitted to lung resection because of varied clinical reasons
9228366.TypesofInterventions: Noninvasive ventilatory support with a nasal ventilatory support system (BiPAP) for 1 hour
9228366.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Arterial blood gases, ventilatory pattern, systemic hemodynamics, pleural air leaks

8408940.Objectives: Investigation into the incidence of post-operative complications after thoracic surgery with 3 different physiotherapy masks for prevention of post-operative pulmonary complications
8408940.TypesofParticipants: Patients undergoing heart surgery, pulmonary resection, or exploratory thoracotomy
8408940.TypesofInterventions: Continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP), positive expiratory pressure (PEP), inspiratory resistance - positive expiratory pressure (IR-PEP) masks
8408940.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Forced vital capacity (FVC), arterial oxygen tension (PaO2), atelectasis on chest X-ray, patient questionnaire on mask preference

7985254.Objectives: compare the effect of three different physiotherapy masks on the incidence of postoperative complications after thoracic surgery
7985254.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing heart surgery, pulmonary resection, or exploratory thoracotomy
7985254.TypesofInterventions: continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP), positive expiratory pressure (PEP), or inspiratory resistance-positive expiratory pressure (IR-PEP) as adjuncts to routine chest physiotherapy
7985254.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: forced vital capacity (FVC), arterial oxygen tension (PaO2), chest X-ray examination (atelectasis), patient questionnaire opinions

9296413.Objectives: compare PaO2 values during selective lobar blockade vs total lung collapse with or without continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP)
9296413.TypesofInterventions: Selective lobar collapse and total lung collapse with or without continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) of 5 cm H2O
9296413.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: PaO2, arterial blood pressure, heart rate, arterial oxygen saturation

9620517.Objectives: evaluate the effect of isoflurane and sevoflurane on oxygenation and shunt fraction during one-lung ventilation
9620517.TypesofParticipants: patients undergoing lobectomy for lung cancer scheduled for long-term one-lung ventilation

17489478.Objectives: Evaluate the effect of polyethylene fiber, glass fiber, and flowable resin liner on microleakage in Class II adhesive restorations
17489478.TypesofInterventions: flowable resin liner, polyethylene fiber (Ribbond), glass fiber (everStick NET), resin composite restoration

12797564.Objectives: To evaluate the ability of various lining materials to reduce cervical marginal microleakage and internal voids within Class II resin-based composite restorations.
12797564.TypesofInterventions: various lining materials including flowable composites, compomers, and resin-modified glass-ionomer cements (RMGI) used in direct composite or sandwich techniques
12797564.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: cervical marginal microleakage (dye penetration length ratio) and internal voids (area ratio of voids to sectioned restorations)

2076239.Objectives: evaluate and compare the clinical performance of two posterior composite materials, and two placement techniques, unlaminated and laminated to glass ionomer base
2076239.TypesofParticipants: patients with Class II carious lesions
2076239.TypesofInterventions: Status composite, Ful-Fil composite, laminate technique with glass ionomer base, unlaminated technique
2076239.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: anatomic form, marginal adaptation, color, secondary caries, marginal discoloration, contact, gingival margin discrepancy (radiographic)

17082278.Objectives: Evaluate the 24-month performance of a packable resin-based composite/dentin bonding system and a high-viscosity glass ionomer cement in restorations placed in primary molars with the atraumatic restorative treatment (ART) approach
17082278.TypesofParticipants: Children aged 6 through 10 years with bilateral matched pairs of carious posterior Class I and II primary teeth
17082278.TypesofInterventions: Packable resin-based composite/dentin bonding system and high-viscosity glass ionomer cement placed using the ART approach
17082278.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Survival rates at 24 months evaluated according to U.S. Public Health Service Ryge criteria

16128999.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of a thin layer of flowable composite on microleakage in Class II direct packable composite resin restorations on young permanent teeth in vitro
16128999.TypesofParticipants: sound human premolars and molars extracted for orthodontic reasons
16128999.TypesofInterventions: use of flowable composite resin as intermediate layer (Alert/Flow-it or Pyramid/Aeliteflo) with packable composite
16128999.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: microleakage measured by methylene blue dye penetration after thermocycling

15241910.Objectives: To evaluate the 4-year clinical performance of the compomer Hytac in Class I and II cavities of permanent posterior teeth
15241910.TypesofParticipants: Patients with Class I and II cavities in permanent posterior teeth
15241910.TypesofInterventions: Compomer restorations (Hytac) with self-conditioning adhesive (Hytac OSB) without phosphoric acid-etching
15241910.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Clinical performance (modified USPHS criteria), success rate, failure rate, marginal integrity, survival probability

17910217.Objectives: Evaluate the incidence of postoperative sensitivity in resin-based posterior restorations
17910217.TypesofInterventions: Direct composite restorations using total-etch adhesive system (Prime & Bond NT) and resin composite (TPH Spectrum)
17910217.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Postoperative sensitivity (presence and triggering stimuli) at 24 hours, 7, 30, and 90 days

17472181.Objectives: Evaluate postoperative sensitivity in composite resin restorations using various liners
17472181.TypesofParticipants: 80 teeth with Class I and Class II cavities
17472181.TypesofInterventions: composite resin restorations using different treatment protocols, including self-etching primers as bonding agents
17472181.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: postoperative sensitivity to cold water at various temperatures up to three months

11208208.Objectives: Determine the influence of flowable composite linings on marginal microleakage and internal voids in class II composite restorations performed by practitioners with different levels of experience
11208208.TypesofInterventions: Flowable composite lining (Prodigy/Revolution or Tetric Ceram/Tetric Flow) vs no lining; operator experience (experienced vs untrained)
11208208.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: gingival marginal microleakage and internal voids (at gingival wall interface, cervical and occlusal parts)

12615021.Objectives: To test the hypothesis that filling technique, cavity configuration and use of a low-viscosity composite liner influence resin bond strength to the dentin of class II cavities gingival floor; and analyze the failure modes.
12615021.TypesofInterventions: Horizontal layering, faciolingual layering, oblique layering, bulk filling, with or without low-viscosity composite liner (Tetric Flow Chroma)
12615021.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Resin bond strength (tensile) and failure modes (by SEM)

15451239.Objectives: Measure resin/dentin bond strengths and evaluate gap formation at the pulpal floor of occlusal composite restorations using a flowable resin as a liner with either bulk or incremental placement of the restorative composite
15451239.TypesofInterventions: flowable resin liner (AEliteFlo or Revolution); bulk vs incremental composite placement; One-Step adhesive with Renew composite; OptiBond SOLO adhesive with Herculite XRV composite
15451239.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: microtensile bond strength; presence of internal resin-dentin gaps; Knoop hardness

14579881.Objectives: To evaluate the 3-year clinical performance of the compomer Hytac in Class I and II cavities of permanent posterior teeth
14579881.TypesofParticipants: Patients with permanent posterior teeth requiring Class I or II restorations
14579881.TypesofInterventions: Compomer Hytac with self-etching adhesive Hytac OSB
14579881.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Modified USPHS criteria, success rate, annual failure rate

10323159.Objectives: assess sensitivity experienced and reported by patients following treatment of primary carious lesions with Class I or Class II amalgam restorations
10323159.TypesofParticipants: patients with primary carious lesions, previously untreated teeth
10323159.TypesofInterventions: no liner; two coats of copal varnish; dentin adhesive resin liner; resin-modified glass-ionomer liner

26220911.TypesofParticipants: Adults with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis and active psoriatic arthritis
26220911.TypesofInterventions: Apremilast (Otezla), oral phosphodiesterase 4 inhibitor, twice-daily dosing
26220911.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Severity and extent of plaque psoriasis (including nail, scalp, palmoplantar manifestations); signs and symptoms of psoriatic arthritis, skin involvement, enthesitis, dactylitis; adverse events (diarrhea, nausea, nasopharyngitis, upper respiratory tract infection)

11837570.Objectives: To understand the pathogenic relationship between Helicobacter pylori and skin diseases; examine serum IgG antibodies, perform gastroscopy, and evaluate therapeutic efficacy of antibacterial eradication therapy in H. pylori-positive patients with chronic skin diseases.
11837570.TypesofParticipants: Japanese patients with chronic skin diseases resistant to conventional therapies, including chronic urticaria, pruritus cutaneous, atopic dermatitis, nummular dermatitis, prurigo chronica multiformis, psoriasis vulgaris, and erythroderma.
11837570.TypesofInterventions: Antibacterial eradication therapy for Helicobacter pylori
11837570.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Therapeutic efficacy (percentage of patients with improvement in skin disease)

24283892.Objectives: review the available evidence for the benefit of tonsillectomy as a treatment for palmoplantar pustulosis and psoriasis
24283892.TypesofParticipants: patients with psoriasis (palmoplantar pustulosis, plaque psoriasis, guttate psoriasis)

28213862.Objectives: Review of apremilast in psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis
28213862.TypesofStudies: Phase 3 randomized controlled trials (ESTEEM, PALACE, ACTIVE)
28213862.TypesofParticipants: Patients with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis and active psoriatic arthritis (including DMARD-naïve and DMARD-experienced)
28213862.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Psoriasis severity, nail/scalp/palmoplantar psoriasis, pruritus, Dermatology Life Quality Index, visual analogue scale for pruritus and skin discomfort/pain, enthesitis, dactylitis, physical function, fatigue, ≥20% improvement in modified ACR response criteria (ACR20)

22465364.Objectives: evaluate the safety and possible efficacy of subconjunctival sirolimus for the treatment of chronic active anterior uveitis
22465364.TypesofStudies: Prospective, nonrandomized, open-label clinical trial (pilot trial)
22465364.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic active anterior uveitis
22465364.TypesofInterventions: subconjunctival injection of 30 μL (1320 μg) sirolimus
22465364.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 2-step reduction in anterior chamber inflammation within 4 weeks; safety (adverse events)

19333944.Objectives: To assess the efficacy and safety of golimumab in patients with active psoriatic arthritis (PsA)
19333944.TypesofParticipants: adult patients with active psoriatic arthritis who had at least 3 swollen and 3 tender joints and active psoriasis
19333944.TypesofInterventions: golimumab 50 mg or 100 mg subcutaneous injection every 4 weeks; placebo
19333944.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ACR20 response, PASI 75% improvement, SF-36, HAQ disability index, NAPSI, physician's global assessment of psoriatic nail disease, PsA-modified MASES index

29220542.Objectives: To evaluate long-term efficacy and safety of apremilast in biologic-naive patients with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis and safety of switching from etanercept to apremilast in the phase 3b LIBERATE trial
29220542.TypesofParticipants: Biologic-naive patients with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis
29220542.TypesofInterventions: Apremilast 30 mg BID, etanercept 50 mg QW, placebo, switching to apremilast
29220542.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Psoriasis Area and Severity Index (PASI), Scalp Physician Global Assessment (ScPGA), Nail Psoriasis Severity Index (NAPSI), Dermatology Life Quality Index (DLQI), pruritus visual analog scale (VAS), adverse events

16467019.Objectives: To show that rifampicin has a therapeutic effect in eruptive psoriasis and to try to explain its mode of action

19150270.Objectives: to review and assess the use of mycophenolate mofetil in treating various inflammatory skin conditions
19150270.TypesofParticipants: patients with inflammatory skin conditions (e.g., psoriasis, autoimmune blistering disorders, dermatitides, connective tissue disorders)

26918950.Objectives: assess the efficacy and safety of apremilast in treatment of psoriatic arthritis (PsA) with meta-analysis method
26918950.TypesofInterventions: apremilast (20 mg and 30 mg) vs placebo
26918950.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ACR20 at week 16, Health Assessment Questionnaire-Disability Index scores, serious adverse events (AEs), adverse events

20137493.Objectives: assess the prevalence of cell-wall-deficient bacteria infection in patients with psoriasis and investigate the link between psoriasis and CWDB
20137493.TypesofStudies: cross-sectional study and randomized controlled trial
20137493.TypesofParticipants: psoriasis patients, chronic tonsillitis patients, and controls
20137493.TypesofInterventions: antibiotic therapy versus systemic therapy without antibiotic; in vitro stimulation with bacteria antigens
20137493.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prevalence of CWDB carriage, CWDB detection after antibiotic therapy, IFN-gamma and IL-10 levels, PBMC proliferation

23569359.Objectives: To provide an overview of current treatments for psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis, and summarize findings from Phase II clinical trials of apremilast.
23569359.TypesofStudies: pilot trials, Phase II trials, double-blinded randomized multicenter studies, Phase III trials
23569359.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: PASI-75, American College of Rheumatology-20 scores, incidence of adverse events

1564153.Objectives: confirm the effectiveness of antibiotics in the treatment of streptococcus-associated psoriasis
1564153.TypesofInterventions: penicillin V or erythromycin, with or without rifampin

3086388.Objectives: To evaluate the effect of adding rifampin to penicillin or erythromycin therapy in patients with streptococcal-associated psoriasis
3086388.TypesofInterventions: rifampin combined with penicillin or erythromycin

18703961.Objectives: determine reliability of the Test of Infant Motor Performance (TIMP) in infants with spinal muscular atrophy, type I (SMA-I)
18703961.TypesofStudies: reliability study (interrater reliability and test-retest reliability)
18703961.TypesofParticipants: infants with spinal muscular atrophy, type I (SMA-I); 11 infants genetically confirmed, age range 37-501 days, 3 on pulmonary support
18703961.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: TIMP scores (Test of Infant Motor Performance Total Score)

25597614.Objectives: Evaluate feasibility, safety, and effects on strength and motor function of a home-based, supervised progressive resistance strength training exercise program in children with spinal muscular atrophy types II and III
25597614.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy types II and III
25597614.TypesofInterventions: home-based supervised progressive resistance strength training exercise program, 3 times weekly for 12 weeks
25597614.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: feasibility (visit adherence, safety (pain, adverse events)), strength, motor function

25410841.Objectives: determine the effects of routine daily home air-stacking maneuvers on pulmonary function in spinal muscular atrophy and congenital muscular dystrophy patients, and identify associations between spinal deformities and the effects
25410841.TypesofParticipants: patients with spinal muscular atrophy and congenital muscular dystrophy (18 NMD patients: 10 CMD, 8 SMA)
25410841.TypesofInterventions: routine daily home air-stacking maneuvers with manual resuscitators for four to six months
25410841.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: FVC, PEF, maximum insufflation capacity (MIC), assisted and unassisted peak cough flow (APCF, UPCF)

23542189.Objectives: examine the reliability and validity of the Test of Infant Motor Performance Screening Items (TIMPSI) in infants with type I spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)
23542189.TypesofParticipants: infants with type I spinal muscular atrophy
23542189.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: TIMPSI score, ability to reach, ventilatory support

25846132.Objectives: to construct a composite score of global function that could discriminate among people with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)
25846132.TypesofParticipants: 126 participants with spinal muscular atrophy types 2 and 3
25846132.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Hammersmith Functional Motor Scale-Expanded, Upper Limb Module, 6-minute walk test, SMA functional composite score

17980600.Objectives: To assess kinematic, kinetic and electromyographic (EMG) walking patterns in subjects with spinal muscular atrophy type III and compare with healthy controls to identify characteristic compensatory changes
17980600.TypesofParticipants: Subjects with spinal muscular atrophy type III (SMA group) and healthy subjects (CONTROL group)
17980600.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Kinematic, kinetic, and EMG walking patterns; muscle strength at ankle and knee joints assessed via isokinetic dynamometry

16107648.Objectives: evaluate the efficiency of running-based training on type 2 spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)-like mice
16107648.TypesofParticipants: type 2 spinal muscular atrophy model mice (SMN-null mouse carrying one copy of a transgene of human SMN2)
16107648.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: survival (life span), motor function, extent of neuronal death in lumbar anterior horn, motoneuron survival, alternative splicing pattern of exon 7 in SMN2 gene, muscle phenotype (slow-twitch soleus and fast-twitch plantaris)

25418505.Objectives: Investigate the effect of 12 weeks of cycle ergometer training in patients with spinal muscular atrophy type III
25418505.TypesofParticipants: Patients with spinal muscular atrophy type III and healthy subjects
25418505.TypesofInterventions: 12-week cycle ergometer training program (42 sessions, 30 minutes, 65-70% of VO2max)
25418505.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: VO2max, muscle strength, functional tests, self-reported activities of daily living

24675128.Objectives: To identify the physical and psychosocial effects of equine-assisted activities and therapies (EAATs) on children with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) from the perspective of the children and their parents.
24675128.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy and their parents

21654447.Objectives: To describe the Wilmington Robotic Exoskeleton (WREX) and evaluate its effect on maintaining arm improvement in muscular disease
21654447.TypesofParticipants: patients with arthrogryposis multiplex congenita and spinal muscular atrophy type II
21654447.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: motion analysis, clinical examination, qualitative questionnaire (functionality, quality of life, trunk inclination, contracture effects)

26915343.Objectives: To compare the effects of high intensity swimming and low intensity running on motor units and to evaluate their efficiency in alleviating SMA symptoms in a type 3 SMA-like mouse model.
26915343.TypesofParticipants: Type 3 spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)-like mouse model
26915343.TypesofInterventions: High intensity swimming; low intensity running (long-term, 10 months)
26915343.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Resistance to muscle damage, energetic metabolism, muscle fatigue, motor behavior, motor neuron survival, neuromuscular junction maintenance, skeletal muscle phenotypes, neuromuscular excitability

10589852.Objectives: To examine the potential benefit of respiratory muscle training (RMT) on preservation of respiratory muscle strength and respiratory load perception (RLP) in children with neuromuscular diseases.
10589852.TypesofParticipants: Children (mean age 12.2 ± 1.8 yr) with Duchenne's muscular dystrophy or spinal muscular atrophy type III, and age-, weight-, and sex-matched healthy controls
10589852.TypesofInterventions: Incremental respiratory muscle training with resistive inspiratory and expiratory loads for 6 months (trained group) vs. similar exercises with no load (non-trained group)
10589852.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Maximal static inspiratory pressure (Pi(max)), maximal static expiratory pressure (Pe(max)), and respiratory load perception (modified Borg visual analog scale 0-10)

27379813.Objectives: to mitigate treatment-related skeletal muscle loss in patients with genitourinary cancers using resistance exercise training

10918264.Objectives: To show whether a correlation exists between the intensity of specific respiratory muscle training and the improvement of strength and endurance in inspiratory musculature in patients with neuromuscular disorders
10918264.TypesofParticipants: patients with neuromuscular disorders (NMD); 13 with Duchenne muscular dystrophy and 3 with spinal muscular atrophy
10918264.TypesofInterventions: inspiratory muscle training (IMT) at home with a special training apparatus for 9 months
10918264.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: maximal inspiratory mouth pressure (PI(MAX)) as parameter for inspiratory muscle strength; 12s-maximum voluntary ventilation (12s-MVV) test as parameter for inspiratory muscle endurance

3173043.Objectives: To study the effects of strength training in patients with selected neuromuscular disorders
3173043.TypesofParticipants: patients with spinal muscular atrophy, limb-girdle muscular dystrophy, or facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy
3173043.TypesofInterventions: dynamic weight training (unilateral arm curls and bilateral leg press) 3 times per week for 9 weeks
3173043.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: maximum isometric, dynamic, and isokinetic strength; contractile properties of elbow flexors; computerized tomography of upper arms and thighs; muscle biopsies; fatigue (number of repetitions before fatigue)

26585990.Objectives: To describe current knowledge about physical training in patients with neuromuscular diseases associated with weakness and wasting, review studies of exercise in myopathies and motor neuron diseases with emphasis on spinobulbar muscular atrophy (SBMA), and provide suggestions for future investigations.
26585990.TypesofParticipants: patients with neuromuscular diseases, spinobulbar muscular atrophy patients
26585990.TypesofInterventions: exercise therapy, physical training, different types of exercise

18216203.Objectives: To investigate the effects of exercise-induced NMDA receptor activation on motor unit development and survival in a type 2 SMA mouse model
18216203.TypesofParticipants: type 2 spinal muscular atrophy model mice (type 2 SMA-like mice)
18216203.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: motor unit maturation rate, motor neuron survival, NR2A subunit expression, muscle development, life span

24157400.Objectives: To evaluate safety of whole-body vibration training in ambulatory children with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) and spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)
24157400.TypesofParticipants: ambulatory children with Duchenne muscular dystrophy and spinal muscular atrophy
24157400.TypesofInterventions: Whole-body vibration training on Galileo MedM at home (3 × 3 min twice a day, 5 days a week for 8 weeks)
24157400.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: safety (clinical tolerance and serum creatine kinase levels); time function tests, muscle strength, angular degree of dorsiflexion of ankles, 6-min walking test distance

12865054.Objectives: To develop a functional motor scale for children with spinal muscular atrophy type 2 and type 3, especially those with limited mobility, to assess motor ability and monitor clinical progression
12865054.TypesofStudies: Scale development and validation study with cohort application
12865054.TypesofParticipants: Children with spinal muscular atrophy type 2 and type 3; normal cohort (healthy children)
12865054.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Functional motor ability measured by the Hammersmith functional motor scale

15627939.Objectives: To assess bone mineral density in children with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and to evaluate bone mineral density in relation to age and motor disability.
15627939.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA type II and III), aged 5.5 to 20 years
15627939.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: total body bone mineral density measured by dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA)

3951727.Objectives: Discuss psychosocial aspects and rehabilitation in adolescents and adults with progressive motor disability due to spinal muscular atrophy
3951727.TypesofParticipants: adolescents and adults with spinal muscular atrophy
3951727.TypesofInterventions: rehabilitation (postural education, prescription of braces, passive and active movements, respiratory physiotherapy), psychological support, instructions for home treatment

24122959.Objectives: To understand the relationship between leg muscle weakness and fatigue during walking in spinal muscular atrophy type 3
24122959.TypesofParticipants: Ambulatory patients with spinal muscular atrophy type 3, aged 9-49 years
24122959.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Muscle activity (RMS), stride length, velocity, knee flexor and hip abductor strength, disease duration, leg strength, 6MWT distance

19432834.Objectives: To evaluate whether children with spinal muscular atrophy type II were able to learn glossopharyngeal pistoning for lung insufflation, and to evaluate the effects of GI on pulmonary function and chest expansion
19432834.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy type II
19432834.TypesofInterventions: glossopharyngeal pistoning for lung insufflation (10 cycles, four times per week, for 8 weeks)
19432834.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ability to learn GI (GI volume), inspiratory vital capacity, peak expiratory flow, chest expansion at xiphoid process and fourth costa, symptoms (dizziness, tension in chest)

3452446.Objectives: To study cardiorespiratory responses (VO2 max, heart rate, ventilation) during cycle ergometer exercise and examine correlation with muscle strength in patients with spinal muscular atrophy and limb-girdle dystrophy
3452446.TypesofParticipants: patients with spinal muscular atrophy (N=8) and limb-girdle dystrophy (N=8)
3452446.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: maximum oxygen consumption (VO2 max), maximum heart rate, maximum ventilation, muscle strength (manual test)

26624487.Objectives: To test the efficacy and safety of the head lift exercise for swallowing dysfunction in spinal and bulbar muscular atrophy (SBMA).
26624487.TypesofParticipants: Patients with genetically confirmed spinal and bulbar muscular atrophy (SBMA)
26624487.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Tongue pressure, swallowing functional questionnaire scores, motor functional scales, videofluorography parameters

22930275.Objectives: investigate the extent of autonomic dysfunction and the effects of a running-based exercise on the altered cardiorespiratory function in type 2 SMA-like mice
22930275.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: cardiac conduction parameters (bradycardia, arrhythmia, conduction velocity), ventricular fibrosis, cardiac muscle maturation, contractile protein expression

22593987.Objectives: To expose the growing problem of dysphagia in patients with neuromuscular diseases and to systematize therapeutic activities during rehabilitation
22593987.TypesofParticipants: 25-year-old male with spinal muscular atrophy type Ib and full-blown mechanical and oropharyngeal dysphagia
22593987.TypesofInterventions: Stretching of cervical muscles according to Anderson, massage of soft tissues in TMJ region, acupressure of masseter attachment, postisometric relaxation of masseter muscles, active TMJ movements, active tongue exercises, swallowing maneuvers; 20-minute sessions three times per week for 8 weeks
22593987.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Quantitative TMJ mobility measured with millimeter ruler; subjective assessment of eating and swallowing

28270443.Objectives: To determine whether muscle-specific transgenic overexpression of PGC-1β affects the detraining response following endurance training and whether PGC-1β signaling is sufficient to drive an endurance exercise phenotype.
28270443.TypesofInterventions: muscle-specific PGC-1β overexpression, endurance training and detraining protocol
28270443.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: peak oxygen uptake (ΔVO2max), running performance, in situ half relaxation time, gene expression markers

27015431.Objectives: assess the reliability/validity of the 6MWT in SMA
27015431.TypesofParticipants: patients with ambulatory spinal muscular atrophy
27015431.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Six-Minute Walk Test, peak oxygen uptake, Hammersmith Functional Motor Scale Expanded, lower extremity manual muscle testing, knee flexion hand-held dynamometry, 10-m walk/run

21404286.Objectives: To evaluate fatigue in ambulatory spinal muscular atrophy patients using quantitative gait analysis during the 6-minute walk test
21404286.TypesofParticipants: Ambulatory spinal muscular atrophy patients and healthy controls
21404286.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 6-minute walk test distance, gait speed, dynamic balance, support base, stride length

19171827.Objectives: To examine the effect of aerobic exercise in patients with spinal and bulbar muscular atrophy (SBMA).
19171827.TypesofParticipants: patients with spinal and bulbar muscular atrophy (SBMA)
19171827.TypesofInterventions: regular cycling exercise for 12 weeks (moderate-intensity aerobic conditioning)
19171827.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: maximum oxygen uptake (Vo2max), maximal work capacity (Wmax), muscle morphology, citrate synthase (CS) activity, body composition, electromyography (EMG), static strength, lung function, plasma proteins, hormones, activities of daily living (ADL)

1543427.Objectives: Determining upper extremity strength and function in children with spinal muscular atrophy type II
1543427.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy type II
1543427.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: upper extremity strength (manual muscle testing, sphygmomanometer, pinchometer); functional abilities (mobility, hand function, ADL)

15492357.Objectives: assess the survival pattern, ambulatory status, and functional status of children with spinal muscular atrophy
15492357.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA types I, II, III) in Hong Kong
15492357.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: survival probabilities, ambulatory status (ability to walk 100 meters), functional status using the Functional Independence Measure for Children (WeeFIM) including self-care, mobility, and cognition domains

26966799.Objectives: To describe the physical therapy services received by individuals with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA).
26966799.TypesofParticipants: Individuals with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and their caregivers
26966799.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Setting, frequency, duration, and type of physical therapy; therapies administered by caregivers; clinical status; sociodemographic profile

22938881.Objectives: Prevention and management of limb contractures in neuromuscular diseases

21509821.Objectives: Assess the effectiveness of simulated resistance training (SRT) exercise combined with alendronate (ALEN) in mitigating or preventing disuse-associated losses in cancellous bone microarchitecture and formation.
21509821.TypesofInterventions: Simulated resistance training (SRT) consisting of muscle contractions once every 3 days (four sets of five repetitions, 1000 ms isometric + 1000 ms eccentric); alendronate (ALEN) at 10 µg/kg body weight three times per week; hind limb unloading (HU)
21509821.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Cancellous bone-formation rate (BFR), bone volume, trabecular thickness, osteoid surface (OS/BS), osteoclast surface (Oc.S/BS), osteoblast surface (Ob.S/BS), serum TRACP5b, adipocyte number, osteocyte apoptosis

26582176.Objectives: to investigate whether chronic AICAR treatment elicits beneficial effects on motor abilities and neuromuscular histopathology in a mouse model of severe SMA
26582176.TypesofParticipants: SMNΔ7 mice (severe spinal muscular atrophy mouse model)
26582176.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: motor performance, survival, skeletal muscle atrophy, neuromuscular junction structure, spinal cord pathology (MN loss, glial reaction), SMN expression

20211907.Objectives: To study the performance of the Six-Minute Walk Test in spinal muscular atrophy
20211907.TypesofParticipants: ambulatory patients with spinal muscular atrophy
20211907.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 6MWT total distance, 10-m walk/run, Hammersmith Functional Motor Scale-Expanded (HFMSE), forced vital capacity, handheld dynamometry

18379217.Objectives: to compare the effects of periodic restriction of blood flow to lower extremities with those of isometric exercise on disuse muscular atrophy and weakness induced by immobilization and unloading
18379217.TypesofInterventions: restriction of blood flow (RBF) and isometric training (IMT)
18379217.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: muscle strength, thigh/leg circumferences, serum growth hormone levels

17661376.Objectives: analyze effects of gravitational unloading on muscular fatigability and effectiveness of resistive vibration exercise to counteract these changes
17661376.TypesofStudies: 8 weeks of horizontal bedrest with or without daily resistive vibration exercise
17661376.TypesofInterventions: horizontal bedrest; daily resistive vibration exercise
17661376.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: knee extensor fatigability (% decrease in maximal voluntary isometric torque per minute exercise); muscle blood flow

17917110.Objectives: To define new therapeutic strategies for neuromuscular disorders by investigating exercise-induced neuroprotection and identifying molecules that mimic exercise effects
17917110.TypesofParticipants: SMA model mice; mouse models of spinal muscular atrophy and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
17917110.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: life span increase, improvement of motor capacities, motoneuron survival, overproduction of circulating neurotrophic factors (e.g., IGF-I), modifications of intrinsic properties of motoneurons

23431212.Objectives: describe the clinical decision-making process using the Hypothesis-Oriented Algorithm for Clinicians II (HOAC II) for an infant with hypotonia and gross motor delay
23431212.TypesofInterventions: physical therapy evaluation and clinical decision-making using HOAC II
23431212.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: diagnosis of type 1 spinal muscular atrophy, pharyngeal-phase dysphagia, mild aspiration, global weakness, feeding difficulties

26104324.Objectives: To determine the effects of exercise on soleus muscle in severe burn and muscle disuse atrophy.
26104324.TypesofInterventions: 40% scald burn, hindlimb unloading, resistance exercise (climbing)
26104324.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: soleus mass, isometric twitch force, tetanic force, specific tetanic force, fatigue index

20573738.Objectives: describe outcomes of goal-directed non-invasive ventilation and mechanical insufflation/exsufflation in spinal muscular atrophy type I
20573738.TypesofParticipants: children with spinal muscular atrophy type I (SMA type I)
20573738.TypesofInterventions: non-invasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV), mechanical insufflation/exsufflation (MI-E)
20573738.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: sleep studies, extubation success, chest wall shape improvement, parental satisfaction

26789431.Objectives: To take a closer look at the different training methods for the most relevant indications and to discuss these results in the context of current literature.
26789431.TypesofParticipants: patients with neuromuscular diseases (muscular dystrophy, spinal muscular atrophy, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, paralysis of the phrenic nerve, spinal cord injuries), interstitial lung diseases, sarcoidosis, left ventricular heart failure, pulmonary arterial hypertension, kyphoscoliosis, obesity, obstructive lung diseases (COPD, asthma, cystic fibrosis, non-CF bronchiectasis), and patients undergoing weaning from respirator
26789431.TypesofInterventions: respiratory muscle training methods: resistive load, threshold load, normocapnic hyperpnea; specific diagnostics of respiratory muscle function and individual introduction to training

2612097.Objectives: To study muscle fiber type abnormalities in bulbospinal muscular atrophy and compare with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
2612097.TypesofParticipants: patients with bulbospinal muscular atrophy (BSA) and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)
2612097.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: histopathological findings: fiber type grouping, type 2B deficiency, type 2C fiber, atrophy factor, hypertrophy factor

15229669.Objectives: To evaluate the effects of treatment in a warm climate compared to treatment in Norway for patients with neuromuscular diseases
15229669.TypesofParticipants: 47 children and 40 adults with neuromuscular diseases (primary myopathy or hereditary neuropathy)
15229669.TypesofInterventions: Rehabilitation treatment in a warm climate (Lanzarote) versus treatment in Norway
15229669.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Pain, quality of life, depression, and physical test results

20868338.Objectives: Describe an aquatic therapy program and its outcomes for a child with type III spinal muscular atrophy
20868338.TypesofParticipants: 3-year-old girl with type III spinal muscular atrophy
20868338.TypesofInterventions: Aquatic therapy twice per week for 45-minute sessions for 14 weeks, including aquatic activities for gross motor skills and functional mobility
20868338.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Gross Motor Function Measure (GMFM), Peabody Developmental Motor Scales (PDMS-2), GAITRite system (walking velocity, stride length, single-limb support time)

15912800.Objectives: To report a case of bilateral long thoracic nerve palsy due to weight training
15912800.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: scapular winging, serratus anterior muscle weakness and atrophy, elevated serum CK, CT atrophy, EMG neurogenic change

9109983.Objectives: Evaluate the effect of adding hydrotherapy to conventional physiotherapy in patients with spinal muscular atrophy types II and III
9109983.TypesofParticipants: Patients with spinal muscular atrophy type II and III
9109983.TypesofInterventions: Conventional physiotherapy with hydrotherapy (30°C, twice a week, 30-45 minutes per session over 2 years)
9109983.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Physiotherapeutic evaluations, Manual Muscular Test (MMT), Barthel Ladder, assessment of deformities and muscle strength

16950434.Objectives: to compare the ulcer-free period during follow-up in both study groups
16950434.TypesofParticipants: patients with active venous leg ulcer (CEAP C6)
16950434.TypesofInterventions: ambulatory compression therapy vs ambulatory compression therapy with SEPS and treatment of superficial venous incompetence
16950434.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ulcer-free period, ulcer healing and recurrence rates

21656715.Objectives: clarify the role of incompetent perforators (IPs) in venous leg ulcers; focused on safety, patient satisfaction and the fate of IPs after subfascial endoscopic perforator surgery (SEPS), or saphenous surgery alone
21656715.TypesofParticipants: patients aged 30-78 years with an open or recently healed venous ulcer, and with an incompetent saphenous vein and IPs
21656715.TypesofInterventions: saphenous surgery alone, or in combination with SEPS
21656715.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: safety, patient satisfaction, fate of IPs, remaining IPs on duplex imaging, short-term clinical benefit

14981453.Objectives: systematic review of health outcomes in patients with severe chronic venous insufficiency treated with surgical management that incorporated subfascial endoscopic perforator surgery (SEPS)
14981453.TypesofStudies: systematic review, 1 randomized trial and 19 case series
14981453.TypesofParticipants: patients with severe chronic venous insufficiency, CEAP classification secondary cause (E(S)), deep venous involvement (A(D)), obstructive (P(O))
14981453.TypesofInterventions: subfascial endoscopic perforator surgery (SEPS), with or without concomitant superficial venous ablation
14981453.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ulcer healing, ulcer recurrence, complications (wound infection, hematoma, neuralgia, deep venous thrombosis)

11840425.Objectives: To determine which patients benefit from subfascial endoscopic perforator vein surgery (SEPS) in advanced chronic venous insufficiency (CVI) and venous ulceration.
11840425.TypesofParticipants: Patients with advanced chronic venous insufficiency (CVI) and venous ulceration, including those with primary valvular incompetence (PVI) and postthrombotic (PT) syndrome.
11840425.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Clinical and hemodynamic improvements, wound complication rates.

10512597.Objectives: Indications and results of subfascial endoscopic perforator vein surgery
10512597.TypesofParticipants: Patients with advanced chronic venous insufficiency, stasis skin changes, and healed or active venous ulcerations
10512597.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Wound complications, ulcer healing, ulcer recurrence, perforator reflux

9423722.Objectives: Comparison of open and endoscopic subfascial division for ulcer healing and wound complications
9423722.TypesofInterventions: Open subfascial division (modified Linton approach) vs endoscopic subfascial division (mediastinoscope)
9423722.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Ulcer healing rate, wound infection rate, length of hospital stay

15183623.Objectives: To assess healing and recurrence rates after treatment with compression with or without surgery in people with leg ulceration
15183623.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic venous leg ulceration and superficial venous reflux (with or without deep reflux)
15183623.TypesofInterventions: compression (multilayer compression bandaging weekly until healing then class 2 below-knee stockings) with or without ablative superficial venous surgery
15183623.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 24-week healing rates and 12-month ulcer recurrence rates

11331845.Objectives: investigated the long-term results of ulcer healing and recurrence rates and compared them with preoperative and postoperative duplex findings
11331845.TypesofParticipants: patients with venous ulceration on the medial side of the lower leg (CEAP 6)
11331845.TypesofInterventions: endoscopic subfascial division of incompetent perforating veins (SEPS) versus open subfascial division (modified Linton procedure)
11331845.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: ulcer healing rates, recurrence rates, development of new incompetent perforating veins

12822732.Objectives: Role of subfascial endoscopic perforator vein ligation in surgical treatment of venous ulcers
12822732.TypesofParticipants: Patients with venous ulcers resulting from perforator vein incompetence (PVI) with or without deep venous insufficiency (DVI), and patients with post-thrombotic (PT) syndrome
12822732.TypesofInterventions: Subfascial endoscopic perforator vein ligation (SEPS) and medical management

10192031.Objectives: To investigate whether incompetent leg perforating veins are more frequent with increasing severity of chronic venous disease and whether surgical management is required.

10163328.Objectives: To evaluate the value of ultrasound biomicroscopy for ciliodestructive procedures.

12498508.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Choroidal effusion and shallowing of the anterior chamber
