11713091.Objectives: to describe cyclophotocoagulation procedures for glaucoma and examine evidence on patient selection and efficacy of transscleral and endoscopic techniques
11713091.TypesofStudies: randomized controlled trial, noncomparative case series
11713091.TypesofParticipants: patients with refractory glaucoma who have failed trabeculectomy or tube shunt procedures; patients with minimal useful vision and elevated intraocular pressure; patients with no visual potential and need pain relief; patients whose general medical condition precludes invasive surgery or who refuse more aggressive surgery; patients with acute onset of neovascular glaucoma
11713091.TypesofInterventions: transscleral cyclophotocoagulation, endoscopic cyclophotocoagulation, noncontact Nd:YAG laser, semiconductor diode laser

10221807.Objectives: Evaluate mitomycin C-augmented trabeculectomy combined with postoperative subconjunctival 5-fluorouracil and laser suture lysis in the treatment of refractory pediatric glaucoma
10221807.TypesofParticipants: Pediatric patients with refractory glaucoma (age range 0.05 to 16 years)
10221807.TypesofInterventions: Mitomycin C trabeculectomy (0.4 mg/mL for 3 to 5 minutes) with postoperative subconjunctival 5-fluorouracil and/or laser suture lysis
10221807.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Intraocular pressure between 4 and 16 mm Hg without further glaucoma surgery or devastating complication; success rate; complications (e.g., uncontrolled IOP, wound leak, endophthalmitis, chorioretinal detachment, shallow anterior chamber, 5-fluorouracil toxicity, cataract formation)

12140396.Objectives: To describe outcomes of combining cyclophotocoagulation and tube-shunt glaucoma drainage implants for refractory glaucoma
12140396.TypesofParticipants: patients with refractory glaucoma treated with both cyclophotocoagulation and tube-shunt glaucoma drainage implant in the same eye
12140396.TypesofInterventions: combining cyclophotocoagulation and tube-shunt glaucoma drainage implants, either sequentially or simultaneously
12140396.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: intraocular pressure, number of glaucoma medications, vision, complications

10457699.Objectives: determine the effectiveness of 0.5% apraclonidine in reversing IOP increases after pupillary dilation in patients with chronic open-angle glaucoma
10457699.TypesofStudies: controlled clinical study (within-subject design with treated and untreated eyes)

11549985.Objectives: To calculate comparative costs and the cost-effectiveness of brimonidine 0.2% and betaxolol 0.25% as first-line therapy for patients with primary open-angle glaucoma
11549985.TypesofStudies: multicenter, randomized, double-blind, head-to-head comparative effectiveness study with a decision tree model
11549985.TypesofParticipants: men and women, 21 years of age or older, with newly diagnosed or currently untreated ocular hypertension or open-angle glaucoma
11549985.TypesofInterventions: brimonidine 0.2% and betaxolol 0.25% as initial monotherapy with possible add-on therapies including laser and surgery
11549985.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: clinical success rate and cost-effectiveness ratio

10340983.Objectives: compare effectiveness of selective laser trabeculoplasty (SLT) vs argon laser trabeculoplasty (ALT) in lowering intraocular pressure (IOP) in patients with medically uncontrolled open angle glaucoma
10340983.TypesofParticipants: patients with medically uncontrolled open angle glaucoma
10340983.TypesofInterventions: selective laser trabeculoplasty (SLT) and argon laser trabeculoplasty (ALT)
10340983.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: intraocular pressure (IOP) lowering, anterior chamber reaction

10472966.Objectives: To determine whether a decreased corneal thickness is a primary factor in the lower intraocular pressure readings found in normal-tension glaucoma patients
10472966.TypesofParticipants: patients with normal-tension glaucoma and age- and sex-matched glaucoma patients

11231772.Objectives: To investigate the effects of timolol maleate with preservative and its preserved (PV) and nonpreserved vehicles (NPV) (benzalkonium chloride) on the blood-aqueous barrier and angiographic cystoid macular edema (CME) in early postoperative pseudophakia.
11231772.TypesofParticipants: patients with ocular hypertension, normal tension glaucoma, and primary open-angle glaucoma who underwent surgery for cataracts
11231772.TypesofInterventions: timolol maleate with preservative; preserved vehicle (PV); nonpreserved vehicle (NPV); diclofenac sodium; fluorometholone acetate
11231772.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: disruption of the blood-aqueous barrier (flare amount measured by laser flare cell meter); angiographic cystoid macular edema (CME) detected by fluorescein angiography; mean daily fluctuations in intraocular pressure

10445774.Objectives: to study whether 0.2% ropivacaine or 1% lidocaine with hyaluronidase are suitable local anesthetics for retrobulbar/peribulbar block for transscleral cyclophotocoagulation or retinal panphotocoagulation
10445774.TypesofParticipants: glaucoma patients undergoing krypton laser cyclophotocoagulation and diabetic retinopathy patients undergoing argon laser panphotocoagulation
10445774.TypesofInterventions: retrobulbar/peribulbar block with 0.2% ropivacaine or 1% lidocaine supplemented with hyaluronidase
10445774.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: adequacy of analgesia, need for supplementary blocks, eye movements at 10 minutes and at end of treatment, need for patching at discharge

10604052.Objectives: To determine and establish the significance of central corneal thickness in patients with low-tension glaucoma compared with those with chronic open-angle glaucoma, ocular hypertension, and healthy eyes
10604052.TypesofParticipants: 25 patients with low-tension glaucoma, 80 patients with chronic open-angle glaucoma, 16 patients with ocular hypertension, and 50 control subjects
10604052.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Central corneal thickness measured by ultrasonic pachymetry and intraocular pressure measured by Goldmann applanation tonometry

11220271.Objectives: compare inflammation of the anterior chamber in combined cataract and glaucoma surgery with that in pure filtering surgery
11220271.TypesofParticipants: patients with primary chronic open-angle glaucoma (PCOG)
11220271.TypesofInterventions: combined cataract and glaucoma surgery (phacoemulsification with foldable IOL implantation via same incision); single goniotrephination; cataract extraction via clear cornea with foldable IOL
11220271.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: laser flare (inflammation), intraocular pressure, number of drugs used for glaucoma

11448321.Objectives: Compare safety and efficacy of AGN 192024 vs timolol
11448321.TypesofParticipants: patients with elevated intraocular pressure, ocular hypertension, glaucoma
11448321.TypesofInterventions: AGN 192024 (0.003%, 0.01%, 0.03% once/twice daily), timolol 0.5% twice daily, vehicle control
11448321.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: mean change in IOP, adverse events, hyperemia grading, laser flare meter analysis, heart rate, blood pressure

11078837.Objectives: To characterize optic disk blood flow in patients with open-angle glaucoma compared with age-matched healthy control subjects
11078837.TypesofParticipants: patients with open-angle glaucoma and age-matched healthy control subjects
11078837.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: optic disk cup and neuroretinal rim blood flow, fundus pulsation amplitude in cup and macula, visual field mean deviation

11744581.Objectives: Describe the perioperative management of homozygous protein C deficiency and the successful use of activated protein C concentrate in a patient undergoing surgical procedures under general anesthesia
11744581.TypesofParticipants: Female baby with homozygous protein C deficiency and purpura fulminans
11744581.TypesofInterventions: Activated protein C concentrate administration; surgical procedures: left iridectomy, Broviak catheter implantation and replacement, right iridectomy
11744581.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Intraoperative course, postoperative complications (massive cutaneous necrosis and gangrene), uneventful procedures

11735786.Objectives: To compare the risk of cataract formation in eyes with and without prior trabeculectomy and to assess other risk factors for cataract.
11735786.TypesofParticipants: Patients with medically uncontrolled open-angle glaucoma
11735786.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Cataract formation (cataract surgery or confirmed severe lens opacity with best-corrected visual acuity less than 65 letters)

11992857.Objectives: To compare the sensitivity and the specificity of the Heidelberg Retina Tomograph (HRT) classification of 'Glaucoma' or 'Normal' with that derived from clinical impression (CI) based on several parameters
11992857.TypesofStudies: Consecutive observational case series, retrospective chart review
11992857.TypesofParticipants: 200 left eyes of 200 consecutive patients referred to the Glaucoma Service Diagnostic Laboratory of the Wills Eye Hospital
11992857.TypesofInterventions: Heidelberg Retina Tomograph (HRT) classification, clinical impression (CI) based on several parameters
11992857.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Sensitivity and specificity of HRT classification compared to clinical impression; clinical impression categories: Definite glaucoma, Probably glaucoma, Probably no glaucoma, No glaucoma

10964832.Objectives: To compare the effectiveness of scanning laser entoptic perimetry with static automated perimetry as a noninvasive instrument for screening for glaucomatous damage in visually asymptomatic subjects within the central 60 degrees (diameter) of vision.
10964832.TypesofInterventions: scanning laser entoptic perimetry (virtual reality-based) and static automated perimetry (achromatic threshold perimetry)
10964832.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: presence of a disturbance in the entoptic perimetry stimulus with the presence of defects in visual function as measured by Humphrey automated visual field perimetry

11022660.Objectives: Evaluate the incidence, risk factors and management of glaucoma following penetrating keratoplasty (PK) and check for possible correlations with the indication for PK
11022660.TypesofParticipants: 534 eyes of 483 patients (age 54.7 +/- 19.8 years) that had undergone PK from 1989 to 1999
11022660.TypesofInterventions: penetrating keratoplasty (PK) alone; PK combined with extracapsular cataract extraction and intraocular lens implantation; PK combined with secondary IOL implantation or IOL change; PK only after previous cataract surgery; cataract surgery after PK; nonmechanical trephination with 193 nm excimer laser; mechanical trephination; intraoperative iridotomy; standardized postoperative topical steroids
11022660.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: intraocular pressure (IOP) increase; glaucoma development; need for antiglaucomatous treatment; persistent glaucoma; anterior synechiae; Urrets-Zavalia syndrome

10571352.Objectives: To assess whether central corneal thickness (CCT) is a confounding factor in the classification of patients attending for glaucoma assessment in a district general hospital.
10571352.TypesofParticipants: Patients attending a general ophthalmic clinic: clinically normal eyes, normal-tension glaucoma (NTG) eyes, primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG) eyes, pseudoexfoliative glaucoma (PXE) eyes, chronic angle closure glaucoma (CACG) eyes, glaucoma suspect (GS) eyes
10571352.TypesofInterventions: Central corneal thickness measurement using ultrasonic pachymetry

12523884.Objectives: To assess factors for progression in the Early Manifest Glaucoma Trial (EMGT), including the effect of EMGT treatment
12523884.TypesofStudies: Randomized controlled trial (Early Manifest Glaucoma Trial)
12523884.TypesofInterventions: Argon laser trabeculoplasty plus topical betaxolol
12523884.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Progression determined by perimetric and photographic optic disc criteria

11231767.Objectives: To evaluate the feasibility of diode laser transscleral cyclophotocoagulation (TSCPC) as a primary surgical treatment for primary open-angle glaucoma and to compare 2 laser energy settings used for treatment
11231767.TypesofInterventions: diode laser transscleral cyclophotocoagulation (TSCPC) with two energy settings: 20 applications of 1.5 W for 1.5 seconds or 1.25 W for 2.5 seconds
11231767.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: intraocular pressure decrease (at least 20% drop, final IOP ≤22 mm Hg), visual acuity, complications (atonic pupil, hypotony, phthisis bulbi, sympathetic ophthalmia)

12213686.Objectives: To examine the association between depressive symptoms and mortality in older adults over 7 years
12213686.TypesofStudies: Secondary data analysis of a randomized controlled trial; prospective study

10413720.Objectives: to achieve a more definite answer to the question: do patients with COPD show a higher than normal prevalence of depression?
10413720.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)

16790039.Objectives: Evaluate effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of a minimal psychological intervention (MPI) to reduce depression in chronically ill elderly patients; evaluate whether effect differs between types of chronic illnesses
16790039.TypesofParticipants: Chronically ill elderly patients with diabetes mellitus type II or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and non-severe depression
16790039.TypesofInterventions: Minimal psychological intervention (MPI) administered by nurses trained in cognitive behavioural therapy and self-management
16790039.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Primary: depression (Beck Depression Inventory); secondary: quality of life, daily functioning, self-efficacy, autonomy, participation; economic: cost-effectiveness and cost-utility ratios

19201228.Objectives: To review studies evaluating the efficacy/effectiveness of CPAP and behavioral therapies for OSA, focusing on daytime sleepiness, mood, and cognitive function.
19201228.TypesofStudies: Randomized clinical trials, systematic bibliographic review
19201228.TypesofInterventions: Continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP), conservative behavioral therapies (weight reduction, smoking cessation, alcohol reduction, sleep posture changes, sleep hygiene), sham CPAP, oral placebos
19201228.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Daytime sleepiness, mood (including depression), cognitive function

17535503.Objectives: To evaluate the effectiveness of cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) for treating mild-to-moderate anxiety or depression in adults with clinically stable chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
17535503.TypesofStudies: systematic review of controlled trials; randomized controlled trials (RCTs)
17535503.TypesofParticipants: adults with clinically stable COPD and mild-to-moderate anxiety or depression
17535503.TypesofInterventions: cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) given with exercise training and education
17535503.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: anxiety and depression (treatment effects, reductions in symptoms)

16055871.Objectives: assess the level of psychological distress in a heterogeneous group of patients with COPD, and compare them with the general population and psychiatric outpatients
16055871.TypesofParticipants: patients with COPD, random sample of subjects from the general population, psychiatric outpatients

16253496.Objectives: Examine effects of outpatient pulmonary rehabilitation program on psychological morbidity (anxiety and depressive symptoms) in COPD patients
16253496.TypesofStudies: Controlled trial (rehabilitation group vs control group)
16253496.TypesofParticipants: Patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
16253496.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Hamilton depression rating scale (HAM-D), Hamilton anxiety rating scale (HAM-A), health status, exercise tolerance, dyspnea intensity

16531031.Objectives: Assess prevalence of anxiety and depression in COPD patients and subgroups by sex and severity; evaluate differences between patients with and without high anxiety/depression; find correlation between psychological aspects, symptoms, functional parameters, and QoL.
16531031.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients; healthy controls (sex- and age-matched)
16531031.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Anxiety, depression, dyspnea, quality of life (QoL), functional parameters

18268930.Objectives: identify and describe a population suffering from COPD highlighting the co-morbid conditions that may contribute to poor clinical outcomes
18268930.TypesofParticipants: adult COPD patients from general population, identified via administrative health services databases
18268930.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prevalence of COPD, prevalence of chronic comorbidities (cardiovascular disease, diabetes, depression), number of chronic comorbid conditions, non-respiratory drug prescriptions

16310027.Objectives: review research on psychological characteristics in COPD patients and summarize associations between psychological variables and aspects of life, illness, and health
16310027.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
16310027.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: psychological disorders, neuropsychological impairments, functional disability, reduced quality of life

17687614.Objectives: To examine the prevalence of guideline-concordant depression treatment among veterans with COPD and comorbid depression, and to examine whether the likelihood of receiving guideline-concordant treatment differed by care setting.
17687614.TypesofParticipants: Veterans with COPD who experienced a new treatment episode for major depressive disorder (5,517 veterans, over 65 years old, 97% male)
17687614.TypesofInterventions: Care setting (mental health clinic, pulmonary care, primary care) and delivery of evidence-based depression treatment
17687614.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Guideline-concordant depression treatment (antidepressant coverage and follow-up visits as defined by VA guidelines)

15300966.Objectives: To assess the clinical and economic effects of disease management in patients with chronic diseases.
15300966.TypesofParticipants: Patients with chronic diseases (depression, diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, chronic pain, coronary artery disease, asthma, heart failure, back pain, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, hypertension, hyperlipidemia)
15300966.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Clinical and economic outcomes, including patient satisfaction, patient adherence, disease control, and cost-related outcomes

12426264.Objectives: To investigate whether psychological factors predict outcome after emergency treatment for obstructive pulmonary disease
12426264.TypesofParticipants: patients presenting with exacerbation of asthma or COPD
12426264.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: hospital admission or relapse within 1 month, spirometry, blood sampling, pulse oximetry, breathing rate, pulse rate, dyspnea score, hospital anxiety and depression (HAD) scale

18729534.Objectives: examines the use of modafinil in clinical practice based on the results of randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trials
18729534.TypesofStudies: evidence-based review (of randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trials)
18729534.TypesofParticipants: healthy volunteers and individuals with clinical disorders associated with excessive sleepiness, fatigue, impaired cognition and other symptoms
18729534.TypesofInterventions: modafinil (compared with placebo, caffeine, methylphenidate, dexamfetamine)
18729534.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: mood, fatigue, sleepiness, cognition, illness severity, excessive sleepiness, depressive symptoms, recovery from anaesthesia, ability to attend lectures and drive, safety, placebo effect on outcomes

17568861.Objectives: To evaluate the presence and severity of symptoms of anxiety and depression in individuals with asthma or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
17568861.TypesofParticipants: Patients with asthma (controlled and uncontrolled) or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease at an outpatient clinic
17568861.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Symptoms of anxiety (State-Trait Anxiety Inventory) and symptoms of depression (Beck Depression Inventory)

1523109.Objectives: To investigate the relationship of selected physiological and psychological variables to functional status in patients with COPD

17929534.Objectives: To investigate the effectiveness of a home-based exercise programme on exercise tolerance, pulmonary functions, and quality of life in COPD patients
17929534.TypesofParticipants: stable COPD patients (22 males, 7 females, mean age 59.6 years)
17929534.TypesofInterventions: home-based exercise programme (walking at fastest pace, lower limb muscles, 8 weeks)
17929534.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: six-minute walking test (6MWT) distance, Borg breathlessness score, oxygen saturation, St. George's Hospital Respiratory Questionnaire (SGRQ), Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS), spirometry

12917854.Objectives: To assess the effect of psychotherapy on the anxiety and depression levels of patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
12917854.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
12917854.TypesofInterventions: psychotherapy (psychologic sessions) plus physical exercise, physiotherapy, and educational sessions; control group without psychotherapy
12917854.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Beck Anxiety Inventory (BAI), Beck Depression Inventory (BDI), 6-minute walk distance (6MWD)

19332381.Objectives: examine whether psychological variables enable us to predict adherence to CPAP in order to construct a predictive model to identify patients at risk of abandoning treatment

17504776.Objectives: To reveal associations of depression and anxiety with demographic, health-related quality of life and clinical characteristics of COPD patients seen in UK primary care.
17504776.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: EQ-5D visual analogue scale (EQ-5D VAS), COPD symptom control questionnaire, Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS), Medical Research Council dyspnea index; anxiety, depression, health-related quality of life, symptoms

19449602.Objectives: To study the effects of a respiratory nurse-led cognitive behavioural therapy programme on goal achievement, anxiety, depression, and medical admission rates in patients with COPD.
19449602.TypesofParticipants: Patients with COPD (10 patients across a range of severities)
19449602.TypesofInterventions: Individualised cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) delivered by a respiratory nurse
19449602.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Goal achievement, Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale scores (anxiety and depression domains), medical admission rates

18207055.Objectives: To know the prevalence of depression in medical inpatients with different chronic diseases and to assess the level of unrecognized depression
18207055.TypesofParticipants: medical inpatients with chronic diseases (cardiovascular disease, diabetes, chronic pulmonary heart disease, hypertension, coronary heart disease)
18207055.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: prevalence of depression and levels of depression

17598392.Objectives: to investigate the prevalence of depression in chronic somatic patients suffering from diabetes, epilepsy, asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and hypothyroidism
17598392.TypesofParticipants: chronic somatic patients aged between 18 and 80 years attending neurological and internal medicine out-patient departments
17598392.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: presence and severity of depression (mild, major, severe) as measured by Beck Depression Inventory

12003685.Objectives: to examine the interrelationships between chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), psychiatric illness, and tobacco use; to address current knowledge and future directions regarding diagnosis, treatment, and predictive values
12003685.TypesofInterventions: pharmacologic treatment with several classes of antidepressants; pulmonary rehabilitation
12003685.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: psychologic status; quality of life; daily functioning

1557484.Objectives: examine multiple outcome indicators in patients with disabling chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and comorbid depression
1557484.TypesofParticipants: patients with disabling chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and comorbid depression
1557484.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: depression, anxiety, respiratory symptoms, overall physical comfort, day-to-day function, physiological measures of pulmonary insufficiency

11001074.Objectives: Investigate how impairments in health status were distributed in male patients at each disease stage according to BTS guidelines, and analyse contribution of clinical indices, dyspnoea rating and psychological status to health status at three disease stages
11001074.TypesofParticipants: Male patients with stable chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
11001074.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Health status (St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire), dyspnoea rating (MRC scale), anxiety and depression (Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale), pulmonary function (FEV1), maximal oxygen uptake (VO2 max)

19714508.Objectives: to reveal the effect of emotional status on pulmonary function, quality of life, and exercise performance in patients with COPD
19714508.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients with irreversible airway obstruction (FEV1=1.5±0.6 L, mean age 67±12 years)
19714508.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: pulmonary function tests (FEV1), 6-minute walking test (6-MWT), Short form-36, Beck's Depression Inventory (BDI)

17324283.Objectives: Evaluate the treatment of depressive disorder in general hospitals based on a collaborative care framework, including cost-effectiveness
17324283.TypesofParticipants: outpatients with diabetes mellitus, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, or cardiovascular diseases, diagnosed with moderate to severe depression
17324283.TypesofInterventions: collaborative care approach including contracting, Problem Solving Treatment, antidepressant algorithm, and manual-guided self-help versus care as usual
17324283.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: severity of depressive symptoms (PHQ-9), cost-effectiveness (TiC-P, EuroQol, SF-36)

10118375.Objectives: to study the long-term effects of attendance at 'Help Yourself to Better Breathing' classes on knowledge attainment, coping strategies, and anxiety, depression, and hostility in individuals with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
10118375.TypesofStudies: time-series perspective (pre-post test with six-month follow-up)
10118375.TypesofParticipants: individuals with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
10118375.TypesofInterventions: Help Yourself to Better Breathing classes
10118375.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: knowledge attainment, coping strategies, anxiety, depression, hostility

12464831.Objectives: examine depressive symptoms, physiologic function, and cognition in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease after 3 weeks of rehabilitation
12464831.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, healthy controls
12464831.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Beck Depression Inventory, 6-minute walk test, spirometry, neuropsychological tests (sustained visual attention, verbal retention, visuospatial ability)

17210879.Objectives: Evaluate the impact of comorbid depression on mortality, hospital readmission, smoking behavior, respiratory symptom burden, and physical and social functioning in patients with COPD
17210879.TypesofParticipants: Patients with COPD hospitalized for acute exacerbation
17210879.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Mortality, hospital readmission, length of stay, persistent smoking, quality of life (St George Respiratory Questionnaire scores including symptoms, activities, impact)

18039994.Objectives: determine whether depressive or anxiety symptoms are associated with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) hospitalization or mortality
18039994.TypesofParticipants: patients with emphysema and severe airflow limitation randomized to medical therapy
18039994.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 1-year and 3-year mortality; COPD or respiratory-related hospitalization or emergency department visit

15204502.Objectives: Evaluate impact of inpatient pulmonary rehabilitation program upon changes in anxiety, depression, psychological outlook, and dyspnea; predict changes in these using pulmonary function tests and age
15204502.TypesofParticipants: patients with severe pulmonary disease, mean age 67.4 years (SD 9.2), mean FEV(1)=31.44% predicted
15204502.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Beck Depression Inventory, Hamilton Anxiety Scale, Goldberg Scale, Modified Borg Scale

18690488.Objectives: To determine the prevalence of depressive symptoms in COPD as compared to other chronic illnesses and to identify risk factors for depressive symptoms in COPD
18690488.TypesofParticipants: Persons aged ≥50 years with self-reported COPD from a US population sample
18690488.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Depressive symptoms assessed by CES-D8 scale (score ≥3 classified as clinically significant)

17922939.Objectives: compare cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) group treatment for anxiety and depression with COPD education for COPD patients with moderate-to-severe anxiety and/or depressive symptoms
17922939.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients with moderate-to-severe anxiety and/or depressive symptoms, treated for COPD the year before, with FEV1/FVC<70% and FEV1<70% predicted
17922939.TypesofInterventions: cognitive behavioral therapy group treatment and COPD education
17922939.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Chronic Respiratory Questionnaire (CRQ), Medical Outcomes Survey Short Form-36 (SF-36), Beck Anxiety Inventory (BAI), Beck Depression Inventory-II (BDI-II), 6-minute walk distance, use of health services

19198940.Objectives: Determine the frequency of COPD exacerbations in outpatients with and without depressive symptoms
19198940.TypesofParticipants: patients with a primary diagnosis of COPD, outpatients, after pulmonary rehabilitation program
19198940.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: frequency of acute exacerbations of COPD, time to first exacerbation, likelihood of having an exacerbation

16844889.Objectives: To determine the relative contribution of clinical depression and/or anxiety to quality of life in patients with COPD
16844889.TypesofParticipants: veterans with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and comorbid anxiety or depression
16844889.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: quality of life (Chronic Respiratory Questionnaire, Medical Outcomes Survey Short Form)

19711730.Objectives: assess the correlation between subjective and objective obstruction symptoms and the intensity of anxiety as a condition, as well as estimation of the intensity of anxiety and depression in patients suffering from obstructive syndromes
19711730.TypesofParticipants: patients diagnosed with obstructive disorders (asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease)
19711730.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: intensity of anxiety (state and trait) using Spielberg State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI), depression using Beck Depression Inventory, subjective dyspnoea levels using Borg scale, spirometric parameters (FVC, FEV1, FEV1%, FEV1%FVC)

11897643.Objectives: to define the role of nasal CPAP treatment in mild OSA
11897643.TypesofParticipants: patients with mild obstructive sleep apnea (AHI 5-30)
11897643.TypesofInterventions: nasal continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) and oral placebo tablet
11897643.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 24-hour blood pressure, neurobehavioral function, symptoms (snoring, restless sleep, daytime sleepiness, irritability), daytime sleepiness (Multiple Sleep Latency Test, Epworth Sleepiness Scale), quality of life (SF-36, Functional Outcomes of Sleep Questionnaire), mood (Profile of Moods States, Beck Depression Index)

19445356.Objectives: Assess prevalence of anxiety (especially panic disorder) and depression among patients with COPD; find correlation between psychological aspects (defence style, sense of coherence, family functioning) and psychiatric symptoms
19445356.TypesofParticipants: Patients with severe COPD (GOLD classification), hospitalized
19445356.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Psychiatric diagnoses (panic disorder, depression) via MINI; panic severity via Panic and Agoraphobia Scale; depression severity via Beck's Depression Inventory; family functioning via Family Functioning Questionnaire; sense of coherence via SOC-29; defence style via DSQ-40; life inventory

16024555.Objectives: Reveal associations of depressive symptoms with demographic and clinical characteristics in mild to moderate COPD
16024555.TypesofParticipants: primary care patients with mild to moderate COPD (FEV1 63.6% pred, range 45.1% to 82.1%)

17474645.Objectives: To compare long-term and short-term effects of pulmonary rehabilitation in a subtropical vs colder climate on health-related quality of life and symptoms of anxiety and depression in COPD patients.
17474645.TypesofParticipants: Patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
17474645.TypesofInterventions: Pulmonary rehabilitation in a subtropical climate (Spain) vs temperate climate (Norway)
17474645.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Health-related quality of life (St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire), anxiety and depression symptoms (Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale)

16670251.Objectives: To examine whether brief inpatient pulmonary rehabilitation is followed by improvement in depressive symptoms and function in COPD patients with major depression.
16670251.TypesofParticipants: Patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and major depression
16670251.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Depressive symptoms (Hamilton Depression scale score), disability domains, medical burden, social support, treatment satisfaction

17388769.Objectives: to determine if acupressure would lessen dyspnea and reduce depression in patients with COPD
17388769.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
17388769.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Geriatric Depression Scale (GDS), Dyspnea Visual Analogue Scale (DVAS), oxygen saturation, physiological indicators

15654000.Objectives: To understand the association between preferences for life-sustaining treatment and depression or quality of life in patients with oxygen-prescribed COPD
15654000.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: preferences for mechanical ventilation and cardiopulmonary resuscitation

16308576.Objectives: examine and describe the quality of mental health care for persons with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and anxiety/depressive disorders, as measured by adherence to practice guidelines
16308576.TypesofParticipants: primary care and mental health care patients with COPD and diagnosed with major depressive disorder, dysthymia, depression not otherwise specified, generalized anxiety disorder, or anxiety not otherwise specified
16308576.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: adherence to practice guidelines for diagnostic evaluation, acute treatment, and long-term management of anxiety and depressive disorders; recognition of anxiety or depressive disorder

18755925.Objectives: To investigate the independent effect of depression and anxiety on the risk of COPD exacerbations and hospitalizations.
18755925.TypesofParticipants: 491 patients with stable COPD in China
18755925.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: COPD exacerbations (symptom-based and event-based), hospitalizations, duration of event-based exacerbations

11352373.Objectives: To test whether a single 2-hour session of cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) with 6-week follow-up reduces anxiety and depression, improves physical and mental functioning, and leads to better quality of life and greater treatment satisfaction in older patients with COPD
11352373.TypesofStudies: Single blind randomized controlled clinical trial
11352373.TypesofParticipants: Elderly patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD); older patients recruited from a Veterans Affairs Hospital, private hospital, and local newspaper
11352373.TypesofInterventions: One 2-hour session of group CBT (relaxation training, cognitive interventions, graduated practice, homework, weekly calls for 6 weeks); compared to 2 hours of COPD education with weekly calls
11352373.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Geriatric Depression Scale, Beck Anxiety Inventory, SF-36, 6-minute walk test, FEV-1, Client Satisfaction Questionnaire

15841684.Objectives: examine how clinical and functional outcomes of depression treatment compare in primary-care patients with and without chronic medical illness
15841684.TypesofParticipants: primary-care patients initiating antidepressant treatment, including general sample and cohorts with ischemic heart disease, diabetes, or obstructive lung disease

20463178.TypesofInterventions: Indacaterol 150 or 300 microg once daily, placebo, or open-label tiotropium 18 microg once daily for 26 weeks
20463178.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Trough FEV(1) at 12 weeks; transition dyspnea index (TDI); St George's Respiratory Questionnaire (SGRQ) total score; exacerbations; serum potassium, blood glucose, QTc interval

20418370.Objectives: To assess the paradoxical trough effects of triple therapy with budesonide/formoterol and tiotropium bromide on pulmonary function outcomes in COPD using impulse oscillometry and body plethysmography.
20418370.TypesofStudies: Double-blind randomized crossover trial with nonrandomized run-in
20418370.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients with FEV1/FVC ratio < 0.7 and FEV1 < 60%
20418370.TypesofInterventions: Budesonide/formoterol 200/6 µg 2 puffs twice daily and tiotropium 18 µg once daily
20418370.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: FEV1, forced expiratory flow midexpiratory phase, impulse oscillometry parameters (reactance, resonant frequency, area under reactance curve), and body plethysmography (specific airway resistance)

16337781.Objectives: To compare the acute effect of tiotropium versus budesonide/formoterol on the degree of resting pulmonary hyperinflation in COPD patients
16337781.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients with FEV(1) ≤65% predicted and IC <80% predicted
16337781.TypesofInterventions: tiotropium 18 μg or budesonide/formoterol 320/9 μg single inhalation
16337781.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: changes in inspiratory capacity (IC), thoracic gas volume (TGV), residual volume (RV), and degree of pulmonary hyperinflation

23978116.Objectives: To evaluate the safety and efficacy of inhaled glycopyrronium in patients with COPD
23978116.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: forced expiratory volume in the first second, use of rescue medication, daytime dyspnoea scores, health status, dynamic hyperinflation, exercise tolerance, rate of exacerbations

24604791.Objectives: review of QVA149 use in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
24604791.TypesofParticipants: adults with COPD, low (exacerbation) risk patients with moderate to severe disease, high (exacerbation) risk patients with severe or very severe disease
24604791.TypesofInterventions: QVA149 (indacaterol/glycopyrronium fixed-dose combination), indacaterol, glycopyrronium, tiotropium, salmeterol/fluticasone
24604791.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: bronchodilation, lung function, dyspnoea, health status, COPD exacerbation risk, patient symptoms, rescue medication use, prevention of moderate to severe exacerbations, tolerability

23060624.Objectives: Efficacy and safety of NVA237 in patients with moderate-to-severe COPD
23060624.TypesofInterventions: NVA237 50 μg once-daily, placebo, open-label tiotropium 18 μg once-daily
23060624.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: trough FEV1, dyspnoea (Transition Dyspnoea Index), health status (St George's Respiratory Questionnaire), moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations, rescue medication use

21397482.Objectives: assess the effect of indacaterol on dyspnoea and health status, evaluate relative efficacy of indacaterol and existing bronchodilators
21397482.TypesofStudies: pooled analysis of three randomized, placebo-controlled studies
21397482.TypesofInterventions: indacaterol 150/300 μg once-daily, formoterol 12 μg twice-daily, salmeterol 50 μg twice-daily, tiotropium 18 μg once-daily, placebo
21397482.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: transition dyspnoea index (TDI) total score, St George's Respiratory Questionnaire (SGRQ) total score

22963553.TypesofInterventions: inhaled muscarinic acetylcholine receptor antagonists (ipratropium bromide, oxitropium bromide, tiotropium bromide, aclidinium bromide, glycopyrrolate bromide; also long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs) and dual-pharmacology muscarinic antagonist-beta2 agonist (MABA) molecules)

23245993.Objectives: clarify the additive efficacy of short-acting β2-agonists (SABA) or muscarinic antagonists (SAMA) on dynamic hyperinflation and exercise tolerance in COPD patients treated with long-acting bronchodilators
23245993.TypesofParticipants: stable COPD patients treated with long-acting bronchodilators, including long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMA)
23245993.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: dynamic hyperinflation (evaluated by step-wise metronome-paced incremental hyperventilation), exercise tolerance (incremental shuttle walking test), airflow limitation, respiratory resistance, breathlessness

24534204.Objectives: Evaluate effects of QVA149 on exercise tolerance, hyperinflation, lung function and lung volumes versus placebo and tiotropium
24534204.TypesofInterventions: QVA149 110/50 μg once daily, placebo, tiotropium 18 μg once daily
24534204.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Exercise endurance time at Day 21 (primary); dynamic inspiratory capacity at exercise isotime, trough FEV1, residual volume, functional residual capacity (secondary)

19451700.Objectives: To quantify whole lung deposition and regional lung deposition of aclidinium delivered by a multidose dry powder inhaler (Genuair) in healthy subjects
19451700.TypesofInterventions: single dose (200 microg) of aclidinium bromide via multidose dry powder inhaler (Genuair)
19451700.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: whole lung deposition, regional lung deposition (6 concentric zones), oropharyngeal deposition, amount retained in inhaler, amount exhaled

22854200.Objectives: Determine how clinically relevant dissociation rates under more physiological conditions influence differences in onset of action between tiotropium and NVA237; investigate kinetic selectivity at muscarinic receptor subtypes to determine if improved cardiovascular therapeutic index of NVA237 is attributable to differences in kinetic rate constants.
22854200.TypesofStudies: in vitro study (radioligand binding, calcium assay, rat tracheal strip assay) and simulations
22854200.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: dissociation half-lives, onset of action, off rates, equilibrium binding and kinetic selectivity, therapeutic index

23075544.Objectives: To review the COPD management potential of aclidinium bromide
23075544.TypesofStudies: review; pharmacokinetic study; phase II study; phase III studies (ACCORD I and ATTAIN)
23075544.TypesofParticipants: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
23075544.TypesofInterventions: aclidinium bromide 200 µg and 400 µg twice daily; tiotropium
23075544.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: trough FEV1, peak FEV1, health status, dyspnea, nighttime and morning symptoms of COPD

24669829.Objectives: To assess safety, tolerability, and bronchodilator effect of inhaled AZD9164; to investigate transient paradoxical bronchospasm
24669829.TypesofStudies: Phase I, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multiple ascending dose (MAD) studies
24669829.TypesofParticipants: Healthy subjects (white and Japanese) and patients with COPD
24669829.TypesofInterventions: Inhaled AZD9164 via Turbuhaler™ at doses 400, 1000, or 2800 μg delivered doses versus placebo
24669829.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Safety and tolerability; bronchodilator effect (FEV1); transient paradoxical bronchospasm (fall in FEV1 5-15 minutes post-inhalation)

21487451.Objectives: determine the relative efficacy of existing treatments and combinations to reduce the risk for COPD exacerbations
21487451.TypesofStudies: randomized clinical trials (RCTs), multiple treatment comparison meta-analysis
21487451.TypesofInterventions: LABA, LAMA, ICS, roflumilast, and combinations thereof
21487451.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: event rate of exacerbations, occurrence of at least one exacerbation

20854047.Objectives: assess the rate of onset of bronchodilation with aclidinium compared with placebo and tiotropium
20854047.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients with post-bronchodilator FEV1 ≥30% and <60% predicted
20854047.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: percentage of patients with increase in FEV1 ≥10% above baseline at 30 minutes post-dose; change from baseline in FEV1 and dyspnea over 3 hours post-dose

23856511.Objectives: Assess the cost effectiveness of budesonide/formoterol plus tiotropium vs placebo plus tiotropium in COPD patients eligible for ICS/LABA
23856511.TypesofStudies: Cost-effectiveness analysis based on a 12-week, randomised, double-blind trial (CLIMB trial)
23856511.TypesofParticipants: COPD patients with pre-bronchodilator FEV1 ≤ 50% and at least one exacerbation requiring systemic glucocorticosteroids or antibiotics in the preceding year
23856511.TypesofInterventions: Budesonide/formoterol 320/9 μg bid plus tiotropium 18 μg qd vs placebo bid plus tiotropium 18 μg qd
23856511.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Number of severe exacerbations avoided (hospitalisation/emergency room visit/systemic glucocorticosteroids); sub-analysis included antibiotics in exacerbation definition

23748050.Objectives: Discuss emerging drugs and novel targets for the treatment of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
23748050.TypesofParticipants: Patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
23748050.TypesofInterventions: Long-acting bronchodilators, LAMA glycopyrronium, LAMA/LABA fixed-dose combination QVA149, LABA/ICS combination QMF149, non-steroid based anti-inflammatory agents
23748050.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Lung function, exercise tolerance, health-related quality of life, symptoms, exacerbation rate

22366196.Objectives: assess the safety and pharmacokinetics of multiple doses of twice-daily aclidinium in healthy subjects
22366196.TypesofStudies: phase I, randomized, single-blind, multiple-dose clinical trial
22366196.TypesofInterventions: aclidinium bromide 200 μg, 400 μg, 800 μg, or placebo twice daily for 7 days
22366196.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: safety (adverse events, laboratory evaluations, vital signs, ECGs) and pharmacokinetics (plasma concentrations, Cmax, t½, exposure, steady state)

24986094.Objectives: Evaluate the effect of glycopyrronium on the QT interval and other cardiac parameters in healthy subjects
24986094.TypesofStudies: randomized, partially blinded, single-dose, placebo- and positive-controlled, three-way cross-over study
24986094.TypesofParticipants: healthy male and female subjects aged 18-45 years
24986094.TypesofInterventions: single inhaled supra-therapeutic dose (400 µg) of glycopyrronium; moxifloxacin (positive control); placebo
24986094.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Fridericia-corrected QT interval (QTcF; primary), Bazett-corrected QT interval (QTcB), heart rate, blood pressure, pharmacokinetics, safety, tolerability

18804362.Objectives: examine clinical efficacy and safety of formoterol, tiotropium and the combination in patients with COPD
18804362.TypesofParticipants: patients with COPD (mean FEV1 52% predicted, FEV1/FVC 53%)
18804362.TypesofInterventions: formoterol 10 microg b.i.d., tiotropium 18 microg o.d., combination therapy, placebo
18804362.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: FEV1 2h post-dose, COPD-related bad days, symptoms, use of rescue medication, peak expiratory flow, health-related quality of life, adverse events

16055868.Objectives: Comparison of bronchodilator effects of tiotropium, formoterol, and both combined in COPD
16055868.TypesofInterventions: tiotropium 18 mcg once daily, formoterol 12 mcg twice daily, or both combined once daily
16055868.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: 24-hour spirometry (FEV1, FVC), rescue salbutamol use, safety

21166630.Objectives: To assess the cardiovascular safety of QVA149 in COPD patients
21166630.TypesofParticipants: patients with moderate-to-severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD); mean age 63.8 years, 76.5% male
21166630.TypesofInterventions: QVA149 (indacaterol/NVA237) 600/100 μg, 300/100 μg, or 150/100 μg; indacaterol 300 μg; placebo; once daily for 14 days
21166630.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: change from baseline in 24-h mean heart rate on Day 14; QTc interval (Fridericia's) on Days 1, 7, and 14

20978028.Objectives: Assess efficacy and safety of once-daily QVA149 (indacaterol/glycopyrronium) compared with indacaterol monotherapy and placebo in patients with moderate to severe COPD
20978028.TypesofStudies: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, four-period crossover study
20978028.TypesofParticipants: Patients with moderate to severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
20978028.TypesofInterventions: QVA149 (indacaterol 300 μg/glycopyrronium 50 μg), indacaterol 300 μg, indacaterol 600 μg, placebo
20978028.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Trough forced expiratory volume in 1 s (FEV1), adverse events

23867808.Objectives: Safety and efficacy of QVA149 in moderate-to-severe COPD patients over 52 weeks
23867808.TypesofStudies: Multicenter, double-blind, parallel-group, placebo-controlled randomized controlled trial
23867808.TypesofInterventions: QVA149 (110 μg indacaterol/50 μg glycopyrronium) once daily via Breezhaler device versus placebo
23867808.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Primary: treatment-emergent adverse events (AEs); Secondary: vital signs, electrocardiograms (ECGs), laboratory evaluations, pre-dose forced expiratory volume in 1 s (FEV1)

22955035.Objectives: evaluate the safety and tolerability of repeat dosing of UMEC and VI in combination once daily for 28 days in patients with COPD
22955035.TypesofInterventions: umeclidinium/vilanterol (500/25 mcg) once daily vs placebo
22955035.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: weighted mean pulse rate, blood pressure, QTcF, adverse events, trough FEV₁

24321804.Objectives: To compare the efficacy, safety, and tolerability of QVA149 versus salmeterol-fluticasone (SFC) over 26 weeks in patients with moderate-to-severe COPD
24321804.TypesofParticipants: Patients with moderate-to-severe COPD (age ≥40 years, GOLD stages II-III, without exacerbations in previous year)
24321804.TypesofInterventions: Once-daily QVA149 110/50 μg (indacaterol/glycopyrronium) versus twice-daily salmeterol-fluticasone 50/500 μg
24321804.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Standardised area under the curve from 0 to 12 h for FEV1 (FEV1 AUC0-12h) at week 26; incidence of adverse events and serious adverse events including COPD exacerbations

24835833.Objectives: Compare efficacy and safety of umeclidinium plus vilanterol with tiotropium, umeclidinium, or vilanterol monotherapy in patients with moderate to very severe COPD
24835833.TypesofStudies: Two multicentre, randomised, blinded, double-dummy, parallel-group, active-controlled trials
24835833.TypesofParticipants: Current or former smokers aged 40 years or older with established clinical history of COPD, moderate to very severe COPD
24835833.TypesofInterventions: Umeclidinium 125 μg plus vilanterol 25 μg, umeclidinium 62.5 μg plus vilanterol 25 μg, tiotropium 18 μg, vilanterol 25 μg, umeclidinium 125 μg
24835833.TypesofOutcomeMeasures: Trough FEV1 on day 169, dyspnoea, health-related quality of life, symptoms, health status, risk of exacerbation, adverse events, serious adverse events, vital signs, clinical laboratory findings, electrocardiography findings
